units.title: 認識新朋友
units.scenario: Cheryl去室友的朋友跨年派對

sentences.en: Hey Cheryl! You’re coming to the party later tonight, right?
sentences.zh: 嘿 Cheryl！今晚你會來派對吧？

back.grammar: 用現在進行式 “You’re coming...” 表示已安排好的未來計畫；句尾 “right?” 是口語確認語氣。
variations: Hey Cheryl, you’re still coming tonight, right?
You’re gonna make it to the party later, yeah?
Cheryl! See you at the party tonight?

extraExamples.en: You’re coming to dinner tomorrow, right?
extraExamples.zh: 你明天會來吃晚餐，對吧？

extraExamples.en: We’re meeting at the café later tonight, right?
extraExamples.zh: 我們今晚晚一點在咖啡廳見，對吧？

sentences.en: Oh yeah! What time does it start?
sentences.zh: 哦，當然！派對什麼時候開始？

back.grammar: “What time does it start?” 用一般現在式詢問活動固定開始時間。
variations: Yeah! When does it start?
Totally! What time should I be there?
For sure! What time is it kicking off?

extraExamples.en: What time does the movie start?
extraExamples.zh: 電影幾點開始？

extraExamples.en: What time does your class start tomorrow?
extraExamples.zh: 你明天的課幾點開始？

sentences.en: At eight!
sentences.zh: 八點！

back.grammar: 口語中可省略完整句，直接用時間回答；“At eight” = “It starts at eight.”
variations: It starts at eight.
Eight o’clock.
Around eight.

extraExamples.en: Dinner’s at seven.
extraExamples.zh: 晚餐七點開始。

extraExamples.en: The meeting is at nine.
extraExamples.zh: 會議九點開始。

sentences.en: Sweet. I’ll start getting ready.
sentences.zh: 太棒了！那我開始準備。

back.grammar: “I’ll...” 是 “I will” 的縮寫，表示當下決定要做的事；“get ready” 是準備出門/打扮的口語片語。
variations: Nice. I’ll go get ready.
Cool, I should start getting ready then.
Awesome. I’m gonna start getting ready.

extraExamples.en: Sweet. I’ll text you when I leave.
extraExamples.zh: 太好了。我出門時會傳訊息給你。

extraExamples.en: I need to start getting ready for work.
extraExamples.zh: 我得開始準備去上班了。

sentences.en: You can borrow my pink dress. I have two dresses and I think you will look great in the pink one!
sentences.zh: 你可以借我的粉色洋裝。我有兩件洋裝，我覺得你穿粉色那件會很好看！

back.grammar: “You can borrow...” 表示允許對方借用；“look great in + 衣服” 表示穿某件衣服很好看。
variations: You can wear my pink dress. I think it’d look amazing on you.
Feel free to borrow my pink dress. You’d look so good in it.
I have an extra dress—you should wear the pink one.

extraExamples.en: You can borrow my jacket if you’re cold.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你冷，可以借我的外套。

extraExamples.en: She looks great in that blue sweater.
extraExamples.zh: 她穿那件藍色毛衣很好看。

sentences.en: Awww that would be great since I literally just landed and got no fancy outfit.
sentences.zh: 哇，那太好了！因為我剛下飛機，根本沒有帶正式的衣服。

back.grammar: “just landed” 是口語常用的現在完成/過去式感覺，表示剛抵達；“got no...” 是非常口語的 “don’t have any”。
variations: That would be amazing—I just got here and I don’t have anything dressy.
Thanks, that’d be perfect. I literally just landed and brought nothing fancy.
You’re a lifesaver. I just flew in and don’t have a nice outfit.

extraExamples.en: I literally just landed, so I’m still exhausted.
extraExamples.zh: 我真的剛下飛機，所以還很累。

extraExamples.en: I don’t have a fancy outfit for the wedding.
extraExamples.zh: 我沒有適合婚禮的正式服裝。

sentences.en: No worries! I got you, girl. Let’s start getting ready!
sentences.zh: 不用擔心！我幫你搞定，女孩～我們一起開始準備吧！

back.grammar: “I got you” 是美式口語，意思是「我罩你／我幫你搞定」；不是單純的「我抓到你」。
variations: Don’t worry, girl. I’ve got you.
It’s all good. I’ll help you out.
No stress. We’ll figure it out together.

extraExamples.en: No worries, I got you. I’ll bring an extra charger.
extraExamples.zh: 不用擔心，我幫你搞定。我會多帶一個充電器。

extraExamples.en: Let’s start getting ready before we’re late.
extraExamples.zh: 我們開始準備吧，不然會遲到。

sentences.en: So you’re Daisy’s new roomie, right?
sentences.zh: 所以妳是 Daisy 的新室友，對吧？

back.grammar: “roomie” 是 “roommate” 的口語暱稱；“right?” 用來確認自己聽到的資訊。
variations: So you’re Daisy’s new roommate, right?
You’re Daisy’s new roomie, yeah?
Wait, you’re the new roommate, right?

extraExamples.en: Are you Sam’s new roomie?
extraExamples.zh: 你是 Sam 的新室友嗎？

extraExamples.en: So you’re starting school next week, right?
extraExamples.zh: 所以你下週開始上學，對吧？

sentences.en: Yeah, hi!
sentences.zh: 對啊，嗨！

back.grammar: “Yeah, hi!” 是簡短自然的回應，先確認對方說的內容，再打招呼。
variations: Yep, hi!
Yeah, that’s me. Hi!
Hi! Yeah, I am.

extraExamples.en: Yeah, hi! I’m Cheryl.
extraExamples.zh: 對啊，嗨！我是 Cheryl。

extraExamples.en: Yep, that’s me. Nice to meet you.
extraExamples.zh: 對，就是我。很高興認識你。

sentences.en: Hi, I’m Jessica.
sentences.zh: 嗨，我是 Jessica。

back.grammar: 自我介紹最自然的句型是 “Hi, I’m + 名字。”；“I’m” 是 “I am” 的縮寫。
variations: Hey, I’m Jessica.
Hi there, Jessica.
Nice to meet you, I’m Jessica.

extraExamples.en: Hi, I’m Mark. I live next door.
extraExamples.zh: 嗨，我是 Mark。我住隔壁。

extraExamples.en: Hey, I’m Lily. We’re in the same class.
extraExamples.zh: 嘿，我是 Lily。我們同班。

sentences.en: Cheryl. Nice meeting you.
sentences.zh: Cheryl。很高興認識妳。

back.grammar: “Nice meeting you” 是 “It’s nice meeting you” 的省略口語；初次見面也常說 “Nice to meet you.”
variations: I’m Cheryl. Nice to meet you.
Cheryl. Great to meet you.
Hey, I’m Cheryl. Nice meeting you.

extraExamples.en: I’m Ben. Nice meeting you.
extraExamples.zh: 我是 Ben。很高興認識你。

extraExamples.en: Nice to meet you, Emma. I’ve heard a lot about you.
extraExamples.zh: 很高興認識你，Emma。我聽過很多關於你的事。

sentences.en: So… where are you from?
sentences.zh: 妳來自哪裡？

back.grammar: “Where are you from?” 用來問出生地、家鄉或來自哪裡；聊天開場很常見。
variations: So, where are you from originally?
Where did you grow up?
Are you from around here?

extraExamples.en: Where are you from originally?
extraExamples.zh: 你原本來自哪裡？

extraExamples.en: Did you grow up in this city?
extraExamples.zh: 你是在這個城市長大的嗎？

sentences.en: I’m from Taiwan!
sentences.zh: 我來自台灣！

back.grammar: “I’m from + 地方” 是回答來自哪裡的基本句型。
variations: I’m from Taiwan.
I come from Taiwan.
I grew up in Taiwan.

extraExamples.en: I’m from New York, but I live in Boston now.
extraExamples.zh: 我來自紐約，但我現在住在波士頓。

extraExamples.en: She grew up in Taiwan and moved here last year.
extraExamples.zh: 她在台灣長大，去年搬來這裡。

sentences.en: Wow, that’s nice. I have a friend who’s also from Taiwan! Well, I’m from Philly, but my family lives 2 hours away and we moved here 10 years ago from Virginia.
sentences.zh: 哇，真棒！我有一個朋友也來自台灣！我來自費城，但我家人住在離這裡兩小時的地方，我們十年前從維吉尼亞搬到這裡。

back.grammar: “who’s also from Taiwan” 是關係子句，用來補充說明 friend；“2 hours away” 表示距離大約兩小時車程。
variations: Oh cool, I have a friend from Taiwan too. I’m from Philly, but my family lives about two hours from here.
Nice! One of my friends is Taiwanese too. I’m from Philly, but we moved here from Virginia ten years ago.
That’s awesome. I’m from Philly, though my family’s a couple hours away now.

keyPhrases.phrase: a friend who’s also from Taiwan
keyPhrases.zh: 一位也來自台灣的朋友

extraExamples.en: I have a coworker who’s also from Taipei.
extraExamples.zh: 我有一位同事也來自台北。

extraExamples.en: My parents live about three hours away.
extraExamples.zh: 我爸媽住在大約三小時遠的地方。

sentences.en: Sweet! I’ve never been there but I would like to visit one day!
sentences.zh: 太酷了！我從沒去過那裡，但我希望有一天能去看看！

back.grammar: “I’ve never been there” 是現在完成式，表示到目前為止從沒去過；“would like to” 比 “want to” 稍微客氣。
variations: Cool! I’ve never been, but I’d love to go someday.
Nice! I haven’t been there yet, but I really want to visit.
Sweet! I’d love to check it out one day.

extraExamples.en: I’ve never been to Chicago, but I’d love to visit one day.
extraExamples.zh: 我從沒去過芝加哥，但我希望有一天能去看看。

extraExamples.en: She would like to visit Taiwan next summer.
extraExamples.zh: 她想明年夏天去台灣玩。

sentences.en: Yeah, for sure you should! There are a lot of museums and historical sites to visit.
sentences.zh: 妳一定要去！那裡有很多博物館和歷史景點可以參觀。

back.grammar: 「for sure」是美式口語中強調肯定的說法，意思像「一定、當然」。
variations: Yeah, you definitely should!
For sure, you’ve gotta check it out.
Absolutely—you should go!

extraExamples.en: You should visit Chicago for sure—the food is amazing.
extraExamples.zh: 你一定要去芝加哥，那裡的食物超棒。

extraExamples.en: There are a lot of cool places to visit downtown.
extraExamples.zh: 市中心有很多很酷的地方可以參觀。

sentences.en: I’d imagine, considering it’s one of the oldest cities.
sentences.zh: 我可以想像，畢竟那是最古老的城市之一。

back.grammar: 「I’d imagine」是「I would imagine」的縮寫，用來表示「我想也是、我可以想像」。
variations: I can imagine, since it’s such an old city.
That makes sense, given how old the city is.
I bet, since it’s one of the oldest cities.

extraExamples.en: I’d imagine the traffic is terrible during rush hour.
extraExamples.zh: 我可以想像尖峰時間交通一定很糟。

extraExamples.en: Considering it’s Friday night, the restaurant is probably packed.
extraExamples.zh: 考慮到今天是星期五晚上，餐廳可能擠滿人。

sentences.en: Yeah. So what’s your major?
sentences.zh: 對啊。妳主修什麼？

back.grammar: 「What’s your major?」是大學生聊天時常用來問「你主修什麼？」的自然說法。
variations: So, what are you studying?
What’s your field of study?
What are you majoring in?

extraExamples.en: What’s your major? I’m studying psychology.
extraExamples.zh: 你主修什麼？我讀心理學。

extraExamples.en: She changed her major from biology to marketing.
extraExamples.zh: 她把主修從生物改成行銷。

sentences.en: I am in Beasley School of Law.
sentences.zh: 我就讀於 Beasley 法學院。

back.grammar: 「I’m in...」可用來表示「我就讀於／我在某個學院或科系」。
variations: I go to Beasley School of Law.
I’m studying at Beasley Law.
I’m a student at Beasley School of Law.

extraExamples.en: I’m in the College of Engineering.
extraExamples.zh: 我就讀工程學院。

extraExamples.en: He’s in medical school right now.
extraExamples.zh: 他現在在讀醫學院。

sentences.en: Nice! I’m in Fox Business School. But I’m actually more interested in making music or playing pickleball.
sentences.zh: 很棒！我在 Fox 商學院。但我其實更喜歡製作音樂或玩輕鬆排球。

back.grammar: 「I’m actually more interested in...」用 actually 轉折，表示「其實我更感興趣的是……」。
variations: I’m at Fox Business School, but music and pickleball are more my thing.
I go to Fox, but I’m really into making music and playing pickleball.
I’m in business school, but honestly, I’d rather make music or play pickleball.

extraExamples.en: I’m in finance, but I’m actually more interested in design.
extraExamples.zh: 我讀金融，但其實我對設計更感興趣。

extraExamples.en: She’s more interested in cooking than eating out.
extraExamples.zh: 比起外食，她更喜歡自己做菜。

sentences.en: I’ve always wanted to try pickleball! What kind of music do you make?
sentences.zh: 我一直想試試輕鬆排球！妳做什麼類型的音樂？

back.grammar: 「I’ve always wanted to...」是現在完成式，表示從以前到現在一直想做某事。
variations: I’ve been wanting to try pickleball!
I’ve always been curious about pickleball!
Pickleball is something I’ve really wanted to try!

extraExamples.en: I’ve always wanted to learn how to surf.
extraExamples.zh: 我一直很想學衝浪。

extraExamples.en: What kind of movies do you usually watch?
extraExamples.zh: 你平常看什麼類型的電影？

sentences.en: It varies. Mostly country and jazz.
sentences.zh: 各種都有。主要是鄉村音樂和爵士樂。

back.grammar: 「It varies」是簡短口語回答，表示「不一定、會變、各種都有」。
variations: It depends, but mostly country and jazz.
A little bit of everything, but mainly country and jazz.
It changes, but I usually make country and jazz.

extraExamples.en: My schedule varies, but I’m usually free in the evening.
extraExamples.zh: 我的行程不一定，但我通常晚上有空。

extraExamples.en: The menu varies by season.
extraExamples.zh: 菜單會依季節變化。

sentences.en: Wow! So what brought you back to business school then?
sentences.zh: 哇！那妳為什麼又回來讀商學院呢？

back.grammar: 「What brought you back to...?」不是字面「什麼帶你回來」，而是問「你為什麼又回到……？」
variations: So what made you come back to business school?
Why did you decide to go back to business school?
What got you back into business school?

extraExamples.en: What brought you back to New York?
extraExamples.zh: 是什麼讓你回到紐約？

extraExamples.en: What made you come back to this company?
extraExamples.zh: 是什麼讓你回到這家公司？

sentences.en: Mostly I want to be an independent musician, well I have been. And my father encouraged me to come back to school to learn about the business side of the music industry.
sentences.zh: 主要是因為我想成為一名獨立音樂人，其實我已經是了。我父親鼓勵我回到學校，學習音樂產業的商業層面。

back.grammar: 「I have been」在這裡省略後面的 independent musician，表示「其實我已經是了」。
variations: Mostly, I want to be an independent musician—actually, I already am.
I’m trying to build a career as an independent musician, and I kind of already have.
My goal is to be an independent musician, which I’ve already started doing.

extraExamples.en: My mom encouraged me to apply for the internship.
extraExamples.zh: 我媽媽鼓勵我申請那個實習。

extraExamples.en: He wants to learn the business side of the fashion industry.
extraExamples.zh: 他想學習時尚產業的商業層面。

sentences.en: You have a wise dad.
sentences.zh: 妳有一位很睿智的爸爸。

back.grammar: 「You have a wise dad」是簡單的稱讚句型，用 have 表示「你有一位……的家人」。
variations: Your dad sounds really wise.
Sounds like your dad gives good advice.
Your dad seems like a smart guy.

extraExamples.en: You have a really supportive family.
extraExamples.zh: 你有一個非常支持你的家庭。

extraExamples.en: Your teacher sounds very wise.
extraExamples.zh: 你的老師聽起來很有智慧。

sentences.en: Yup. What about you? I assume you are gonna practice law?
sentences.zh: 嗯嗯，那妳呢？我猜你將來會執業律師吧？

back.grammar: 「gonna」是「going to」的口語縮讀，常用來談未來計畫或推測。
variations: What about you? Are you planning to practice law?
How about you? I’m guessing you’ll become a lawyer?
And you? Are you gonna go into law?

keyPhrases.zh: 執業律師；從事法律工作

extraExamples.en: I assume you’re gonna come to the party tonight?
extraExamples.zh: 我猜你今晚會來派對吧？

extraExamples.en: After law school, she plans to practice law in California.
extraExamples.zh: 法學院畢業後，她打算在加州執業。

sentences.en: Eh nah… I don’t think that’s gonna happen. I’m more interested in the business and technology world.
sentences.zh: 呃，我覺得應該不會… 我對商業和科技世界更感興趣。

back.grammar: 「that’s gonna happen」是否定未來的口語說法，意思是「那應該不會發生」。
variations: Nah, I don’t think so. I’m more into business and tech.
Honestly, I don’t see that happening. I’m more interested in business and technology.
Probably not. I’m more drawn to the business and tech world.

keyPhrases.phrase: I don’t think that’s gonna happen
keyPhrases.zh: 我覺得那應該不會發生

extraExamples.en: I don’t think that’s gonna happen anytime soon.
extraExamples.zh: 我覺得那近期應該不會發生。

extraExamples.en: He’s more interested in startups than big companies.
extraExamples.zh: 比起大公司，他對新創公司更感興趣。

sentences.en: Then you should pursue it, whatever it is that is true to your heart!
sentences.zh: 那妳就應該追求那個目標，做妳真正熱愛的事情！

back.grammar: 「whatever it is that...」是強調句，表示「不管那是什麼，只要是……」。
variations: Then go after it—whatever feels right to you.
You should chase that, if it’s what you really care about.
Then pursue it, as long as it’s what you truly want.

keyPhrases.zh: 忠於你的內心；真正熱愛的

extraExamples.en: If you love photography, you should pursue it.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你熱愛攝影，你就應該去追求它。

extraExamples.en: Choose a career that feels true to your heart.
extraExamples.zh: 選一個忠於你內心的職涯。

sentences.en: Wow this just got very deep.
sentences.zh: 哇，突然好有深度。

back.grammar: 「got very deep」是口語用法，表示話題突然變得很有深度或很嚴肅。
variations: Wow, that got deep fast.
Whoa, this conversation just got serious.
Okay, we suddenly got really deep.

extraExamples.en: The movie started funny, but it got really deep at the end.
extraExamples.zh: 那部電影一開始很好笑，但結尾變得很有深度。

extraExamples.en: Our chat just got serious out of nowhere.
extraExamples.zh: 我們的聊天突然莫名變得很嚴肅。

sentences.en: That’s a good thing! I’m gonna go grab some sliders and alcohol.
sentences.zh: 這是好事！我要去拿點迷你漢堡和酒。

back.grammar: 「I’m gonna go grab...」是很自然的口語，表示「我要去拿／買點……」。
variations: I’m gonna grab some sliders and a drink.
I’m going to get some mini burgers and alcohol.
I’m gonna go pick up some sliders and drinks.

extraExamples.en: I’m gonna go grab some coffee before class.
extraExamples.zh: 我上課前要去買杯咖啡。

extraExamples.en: Let’s grab some food after the meeting.
extraExamples.zh: 開完會後我們去吃點東西吧。

sentences.en: Aight. I’ll see you on the rooftop for the countdown later!
sentences.zh: 好啊。等會倒數時屋頂見！

back.grammar: 「Aight」是美式口語中 all right 的縮略說法；「I’ll see you...」常用來約定待會見面。
variations: Alright, I’ll meet you on the roof for the countdown later!
Cool, see you on the rooftop when it’s time to count down!
Sounds good. I’ll catch you on the rooftop later for the countdown!

keyPhrases.zh: 好、行、沒問題（all right 的口語縮寫）

extraExamples.en: Aight, I’ll see you outside the theater in ten minutes.
extraExamples.zh: 好，十分鐘後電影院外面見。

extraExamples.en: I’ll see you at the café later for our study session.
extraExamples.zh: 等會咖啡廳見，一起讀書。

units.title: 家庭對話
units.scenario: Ian下課後跟室友聊天煮晚餐

sentences.en: Hey, Ian! How’s your day been?
sentences.zh: 嘿，Ian！今天過得怎麼樣？

back.grammar: How’s = How has；「How’s your day been?」用現在完成式詢問「到目前為止今天過得如何」。
variations: How’s your day going so far?
How’s your day been treating you?
What’s your day been like?

keyPhrases.zh: 你今天到目前為止過得怎麼樣？

extraExamples.en: How’s your week been so far?
extraExamples.zh: 你這週到目前為止過得怎麼樣？

extraExamples.en: How’s your new job been treating you?
extraExamples.zh: 你的新工作最近還順利嗎？

sentences.en: Just got back from my last class. What about you?
sentences.zh: 剛從最後一堂課回來。妳呢？

back.grammar: 口語中常省略主詞：Just got back = I just got back；「get back from」表示從某處回來。
variations: I just got back from my last class. How about you?
Just finished my last class and got home. You?
I’m back from class. What’ve you been up to?

extraExamples.en: Just got back from the gym. I’m starving.
extraExamples.zh: 我剛從健身房回來，超餓的。

extraExamples.en: I just got back from work. What about you?
extraExamples.zh: 我剛下班回來。你呢？

sentences.en: Ugh… I think I’m a bit under the weather. Nothing too serious though. I’m just gonna take some Theraflu.
sentences.zh: 唉……我身體有點不太舒服，但應該沒什麼大礙。我打算服用一些感冒藥水。

back.grammar: 「under the weather」是口語片語，表示身體不舒服；gonna = going to，表示打算做某事。
variations: Ugh, I’m feeling kinda sick, but it’s nothing major. I’m gonna take some medicine.
I think I’m coming down with something. I’ll just take some Theraflu.
I feel a little off today. Nothing serious though—I’m gonna take something for it.

extraExamples.en: I’m a little under the weather, so I’m staying home tonight.
extraExamples.zh: 我有點不舒服，所以今晚待在家。

extraExamples.en: It’s nothing too serious. I just need some rest.
extraExamples.zh: 沒什麼大礙，我只是需要休息一下。

sentences.en: Oh no, get well soon! I have some Theraflu left in the kitchen cabinets.
sentences.zh: 喔不，快點好起來！我廚房櫃子裡還有一些感冒藥水。

back.grammar: 「get well soon」是生病慰問的固定說法；「have something left」表示還剩某物。
variations: Oh no, feel better soon! I’ve got some Theraflu in the kitchen cabinet.
Aw, hope you feel better. There’s some Theraflu left in the cabinet.
Oh no! Get some rest. I still have some Theraflu in the kitchen.

extraExamples.en: Get well soon! Let me know if you need anything.
extraExamples.zh: 早日康復！如果你需要什麼跟我說。

extraExamples.en: We still have some soup left in the fridge.
extraExamples.zh: 冰箱裡還剩一些湯。

sentences.en: Let me go get some. (Looking for stuff sound) Also, I went to the track with Mia earlier. You should join us tomorrow!
sentences.zh: 那我去拿一些。(找東西的聲音) 對了，我剛跟 Mia 去操場跑步，你明天也一起來吧！

back.grammar: 「Let me + 原形動詞」表示「讓我來……」；「You should join us」是自然的邀請與建議。
variations: I’ll go grab some. Also, Mia and I went to the track earlier—you should come with us tomorrow!
Let me grab it. Oh, and I hit the track with Mia earlier. Come join us tomorrow!
I’ll go get it. By the way, I went running with Mia earlier. You should come tomorrow!

extraExamples.en: Let me go get my jacket before we leave.
extraExamples.zh: 出門前讓我去拿外套。

extraExamples.en: We’re going to the coffee shop later. You should join us.
extraExamples.zh: 我們晚點要去咖啡店，你應該一起來。

sentences.en: Let me check. One sec… Aight I think I can do that!
sentences.zh: 我看看啊，等一下……好，我可以！

back.grammar: 「One sec」= one second，口語表示「等一下」；Aight = alright，非常口語的「好、行」。
variations: Let me see. Give me a sec… Yeah, I think I can make it!
Hold on, let me check… Okay, I think that works!
One second… Yup, I should be able to do that!

extraExamples.en: One sec, I’m checking my schedule.
extraExamples.zh: 等一下，我在看我的行程。

extraExamples.en: Aight, I can do that after lunch.
extraExamples.zh: 好，我午餐後可以做那件事。

sentences.en: Cool! Oh and I met up with Charles at the lab and we studied for the final for an hour or two.
sentences.zh: 太好了！還有啊，我在實驗室遇到 Charles，我們一起讀書準備期末考了大概一兩個小時。

back.grammar: 「meet up with someone」表示跟某人碰面；「for an hour or two」表示大概一兩個小時。
variations: Cool! I also met Charles at the lab, and we studied for the final for a couple hours.
Nice! I ran into Charles at the lab, and we reviewed for the final for an hour or two.
Cool! Charles and I met up at the lab and studied for the final for a bit.

extraExamples.en: I’m meeting up with Sarah after class.
extraExamples.zh: 我下課後要跟 Sarah 見面。

extraExamples.en: We studied for the midterm for an hour or two at the library.
extraExamples.zh: 我們在圖書館讀了大概一兩個小時準備期中考。

sentences.en: Nice! Seems like you had a very productive day, dude!
sentences.zh: 真棒！看來你今天過得很充實啊，兄弟！

back.grammar: 「Seems like + 子句」表示「看起來像是……」；productive day 指做了很多事、很充實的一天。
variations: Nice! Sounds like you got a lot done today!
Wow, you had such a productive day!
Looks like you were super productive today, man!

extraExamples.en: Seems like you had a great weekend.
extraExamples.zh: 看來你週末過得很棒。

extraExamples.en: I had a really productive morning and finished all my homework.
extraExamples.zh: 我早上超有效率，把作業都寫完了。

sentences.en: Yup, it’s been a long day. Let’s get cooking!
sentences.zh: 是啊，今天真是個漫長的一天。來一起做晚飯吧！

back.grammar: 「It’s been a long day」用現在完成式表達今天到現在為止很漫長；「Let’s get + V-ing」表示開始做某事。
variations: Yeah, it’s been a long day. Let’s start cooking!
Yup, I’m beat. Let’s make dinner!
For sure. Long day. Let’s get dinner going!

extraExamples.en: It’s been a long day, so I’m going straight to bed.
extraExamples.zh: 今天真是漫長，所以我要直接去睡覺。

extraExamples.en: We’re all hungry. Let’s get cooking.
extraExamples.zh: 我們都餓了，來開始煮飯吧。

sentences.en: Aight. I’ll help with the dirty dishes while you make dinner! Who’s on trash duty today?
sentences.zh: 好啊。我來幫忙洗碗，妳來做飯！今天是誰負責倒垃圾？

back.grammar: 「I’ll + 原形動詞」表示臨時決定或主動提出幫忙；「be on duty」表示輪到某人負責某事。
variations: Okay. I’ll do the dishes while you cook. Who’s taking out the trash today?
Sounds good. I’ll handle the dishes while you make dinner. Who’s on trash tonight?
Aight, I got the dishes. Who’s supposed to take out the trash today?

extraExamples.en: I’ll help with the laundry while you clean the bathroom.
extraExamples.zh: 你打掃浴室時，我來幫忙洗衣服。

extraExamples.en: Who’s on dish duty tonight?
extraExamples.zh: 今晚誰負責洗碗？

sentences.en: Not sure but let’s just divide up the chores. You’ll do the dishes and take out the trash, and I’ll make us some spinach omelette and baked chicken.
sentences.zh: 不確定，但我們可以分配一下家務。你洗碗和倒垃圾，我來做菠菜歐姆蛋和烤雞。

back.grammar: 「divide up」是片語動詞，表示分配；「take out the trash」是倒垃圾的固定說法。
variations: I’m not sure, but let’s split the chores. You do dishes and trash, and I’ll cook.
No idea, but we can divide the chores. You handle the dishes and trash, and I’ll make dinner.
Not sure, but let’s split things up. You take out the trash and do the dishes, and I’ll cook for us.

extraExamples.en: Let’s divide up the work so we can finish faster.
extraExamples.zh: 我們來分工，這樣可以更快完成。

extraExamples.en: Can you take out the trash before it starts to smell?
extraExamples.zh: 你可以在垃圾開始發臭前拿去倒嗎？

sentences.en: Yes, ma’am! (Cooking sizzling sound)
sentences.zh: 好的，遵命！ (煮飯的滋滋聲)

back.grammar: 「Yes, ma’am!」原本是對女性的禮貌回應，朋友間可開玩笑表示「遵命！」。
variations: You got it!
Yes, chef!
On it!

extraExamples.en: Yes, ma’am! I’ll take the trash out right now.
extraExamples.zh: 遵命！我現在就去倒垃圾。

extraExamples.en: On it! I’ll send you the file in five minutes.
extraExamples.zh: 馬上處理！我五分鐘內把檔案寄給你。

sentences.en: Before we go out on Friday, we’ll have a pregame in the living room and Frank will come over.
sentences.zh: 我們星期五出去前可以在客廳來場小酌，Frank 會過來。

back.grammar: 「Before + 子句」表示在某事之前；pregame 在美國口語中指正式出門喝酒前先小酌。
variations: Before we head out Friday, we’ll pregame in the living room, and Frank’s gonna come over.
We’re gonna have a little pregame in the living room before going out Friday. Frank’s coming too.
Before we go out on Friday, Frank’s coming over and we’ll have some drinks in the living room.

extraExamples.en: Before we go out, I need to change my shoes.
extraExamples.zh: 我們出門前，我需要換鞋。

extraExamples.en: Do you want to come over and watch a movie tonight?
extraExamples.zh: 你今晚要不要來我家看電影？

sentences.en: Hmm… I think I’ll skip the pregame this time coz I was planning to switch up my room during that time!
sentences.zh: 嗯……我這次可能不參加了，我正打算那時候重新佈置一下我的房間！

back.grammar: 「skip」口語表示不參加；「was planning to」表示原本／當時正計畫要做某事；coz = because。
variations: I think I’m gonna sit this one out because I was planning to rearrange my room then.
I’ll probably skip the pregame this time. I wanted to switch my room around during that time.
I might pass on the pregame this time since I’m planning to redo my room then.

keyPhrases.zh: 重新佈置／改變房間擺設

extraExamples.en: I’m gonna skip the party tonight and stay in.
extraExamples.zh: 我今晚不去派對，要待在家。

extraExamples.en: I want to switch up my room and move my desk by the window.
extraExamples.zh: 我想重新佈置房間，把書桌移到窗邊。

sentences.en: No biggies! You do you! Also, the faucet’s been acting up lately.
sentences.zh: 沒關係！你做自己就好！還有，那水龍頭最近又出狀況了。

back.grammar: 「No biggie」表示沒關係、小事一樁；「You do you」表示做你自己想做的；「act up」指機器或身體出狀況。
variations: No worries! Do your thing. Also, the faucet’s been acting weird lately.
It’s all good! You do you. By the way, the faucet’s been giving us trouble lately.
No big deal! Do what you need to do. Also, the faucet hasn’t been working right lately.

extraExamples.en: No biggie if you can’t make it tonight.
extraExamples.zh: 你今晚不能來也沒關係。

extraExamples.en: My laptop’s been acting up, so I might need to restart it.
extraExamples.zh: 我的筆電最近怪怪的，所以我可能需要重開機。

sentences.en: The faucet? Is it leaking again?
sentences.zh: 水龍頭？又漏水了嗎？

back.grammar: 用現在進行式「be + V-ing」描述現在正在發生的狀況；again 表示「又、再一次」。
variations: The faucet? Is it dripping again?
Wait, the sink is leaking again?
Is that faucet acting up again?

extraExamples.en: Is the AC making that noise again?
extraExamples.zh: 冷氣又在發出那個聲音了嗎？

extraExamples.en: The ceiling is leaking again after the storm.
extraExamples.zh: 暴風雨後天花板又在漏水了。

sentences.en: Yeah, we should call that plumber we used last time and ask him to fix it up.
sentences.zh: 對啊，我們應該打電話叫上次那個水管工來修一下。

back.grammar: should + 原形動詞表示「應該」；that plumber we used last time 是口語中省略關係代名詞的說法，意思是「上次我們找的那個水管工」。
variations: Yeah, we should call the plumber from last time and have him fix it.
Yep, let’s get that plumber we used before to take care of it.
Yeah, we’d better call our plumber and ask him to patch it up.

extraExamples.en: We should call the mechanic we used last time.
extraExamples.zh: 我們應該打電話找上次那個修車師傅。

extraExamples.en: Can you ask your brother to fix it up this weekend?
extraExamples.zh: 你可以請你哥這週末把它修好嗎？

sentences.en: Adulting is hard, Daisy!
sentences.zh: 長大了真的不容易，Daisy！

back.grammar: Adulting 是口語名詞/動名詞，指「像大人一樣處理生活責任」；is hard 表示「很不容易」。
variations: Being an adult is so hard, Daisy!
Grown-up life is rough, Daisy!
Why is adult life so stressful, Daisy?

keyPhrases.zh: 當大人、處理成年人的責任

extraExamples.en: Adulting is hard when you have bills to pay every month.
extraExamples.zh: 每個月都有帳單要繳時，當大人真的不容易。

extraExamples.en: I cooked dinner and did laundry today. Adulting is exhausting.
extraExamples.zh: 我今天煮晚餐又洗衣服。當大人真的很累。

sentences.en: I feel you, Ian. On the bright side, we will have a blast at the party on Friday.
sentences.zh: 我懂你，Ian。往好的方面想，我們星期五派對會玩得很開心。

back.grammar: I feel you 是美式口語，表示「我懂你的感受」；On the bright side 用來轉向正面角度；have a blast 表示「玩得超開心」。
variations: I totally get it, Ian. But hey, Friday’s party is gonna be awesome.
I hear you, Ian. At least we’re going to have so much fun on Friday.
I know what you mean, Ian. The good news is Friday’s party is going to be a blast.

extraExamples.en: I feel you. Work has been crazy for me too.
extraExamples.zh: 我懂你。我工作最近也超忙。

extraExamples.en: On the bright side, we still have the whole weekend to relax.
extraExamples.zh: 往好處想，我們還有整個週末可以放鬆。

sentences.en: Can’t wait!
sentences.zh: 等不及了！

back.grammar: Can’t wait! 是 I can’t wait 的口語省略，表示「等不及了、超期待」。
variations: I’m so excited!
I seriously can’t wait!
Friday can’t come soon enough!

extraExamples.en: Can’t wait to see you this weekend!
extraExamples.zh: 等不及這週末見到你了！

extraExamples.en: I can’t wait for our trip next month.
extraExamples.zh: 我等不及下個月的旅行了。

units.title: 餐廳點餐
units.scenario: Oliver去Chipotle點晚餐＋去Insomnia Cookies買點心

sentences.en: What can I get started for you today?
sentences.zh: 今天想點什麼呢？

back.grammar: 「What can I get started for you today?」是店員常用開場白，比「What do you want?」更自然、有禮貌。
variations: What can I get for you today?
What would you like to order?
What are we having today?

extraExamples.en: What can I get started for you while you look at the menu?
extraExamples.zh: 你看菜單的時候，我可以先幫你準備什麼嗎？

extraExamples.en: The barista asked, “What can I get started for you?”
extraExamples.zh: 咖啡師問：「要幫你先做什麼呢？」

sentences.en: Can I get a bowl?
sentences.zh: 我要一份碗裝的餐點。

back.grammar: 「Can I get...?」在美式口語中常用來點餐，意思是「我可以要……嗎？」比直翻的「I want」更客氣。
variations: Can I have a bowl?
I’ll do a bowl.
Let me get a bowl.

extraExamples.en: Can I get a large iced coffee?
extraExamples.zh: 我可以要一杯大杯冰咖啡嗎？

extraExamples.en: I’ll do a bowl with brown rice, please.
extraExamples.zh: 我要一份碗裝餐點，加糙米飯，謝謝。

sentences.en: You got it. What kind of rice? There is white rice and brown rice.
sentences.zh: 好的馬上做，要什麼飯？有白飯和糙米飯。

back.grammar: 「You got it」是美式口語中表示「沒問題／好的」的自然回應；「What kind of...?」用來詢問種類。
variations: Sure thing. What kind of rice would you like?
Got it. White or brown rice?
Absolutely. Which rice do you want?

extraExamples.en: You got it. What kind of milk would you like?
extraExamples.zh: 好的。你要哪種牛奶？

extraExamples.en: What kind of sauce do you want—mild or spicy?
extraExamples.zh: 你要哪種醬？溫和的還是辣的？

sentences.en: Brown rice.
sentences.zh: 糙米飯。

back.grammar: 點餐時可以直接用名詞片語回答，例如「Brown rice.」完整句是「I’ll have brown rice.」
variations: Brown rice, please.
I’ll have brown rice.
Let’s do brown rice.

extraExamples.en: Brown rice, please. No white rice for me.
extraExamples.zh: 糙米飯，謝謝。我不要白飯。

extraExamples.en: I’ll have brown rice with chicken.
extraExamples.zh: 我要糙米飯配雞肉。

sentences.en: And what kind of beans? Black beans or pinto beans?
sentences.zh: 那豆子呢？要黑豆還是花豆？

back.grammar: 「Black beans or pinto beans?」是用選項式問題讓客人選一個，口語中常省略完整句。
variations: Which beans would you like—black or pinto?
Do you want black beans or pinto beans?
For beans, black or pinto?

extraExamples.en: What kind of beans do you want in your burrito?
extraExamples.zh: 你的墨西哥捲要加哪種豆子？

extraExamples.en: For your side, rice or beans?
extraExamples.zh: 你的配菜要飯還是豆子？

sentences.en: Black beans.
sentences.zh: 黑豆。

back.grammar: 和第4句一樣，點餐回答可用簡短名詞片語；加上「please」會更有禮貌。
variations: Black beans, please.
I’ll take black beans.
Let’s go with black beans.

extraExamples.en: I’ll take black beans with my bowl.
extraExamples.zh: 我的碗裝餐點要加黑豆。

extraExamples.en: Let’s go with black beans this time.
extraExamples.zh: 這次就選黑豆吧。

sentences.en: And what kind of protein?
sentences.zh: 那蛋白質呢？

back.grammar: 「protein」在美式速食／健身語境中常指主菜蛋白質選項，如雞肉、牛肉、豆腐等。
variations: And what protein would you like?
What do you want for your protein?
Which protein are you going with?

keyPhrases.zh: 蛋白質；主菜肉類或素食蛋白

extraExamples.en: What kind of protein do you want in your salad?
extraExamples.zh: 你的沙拉要加哪種蛋白質？

extraExamples.en: I usually choose chicken as my protein.
extraExamples.zh: 我通常選雞肉當主菜蛋白質。

sentences.en: What do you have?
sentences.zh: 你們有什麼選擇？

back.grammar: 「What do you have?」在點餐時意思是「你們有哪些選擇？」不是單純問對方擁有什麼。
variations: What are my options?
What proteins do you have?
What choices are there?

extraExamples.en: What do you have for dessert?
extraExamples.zh: 你們甜點有什麼？

extraExamples.en: What are my options for sides?
extraExamples.zh: 配菜我有哪些選擇？

sentences.en: There’s chicken, um… steak, carnitas, sofritas, grilled veggies…
sentences.zh: 有雞肉、牛排、卡尼塔豬肉、素食豆腐和烤蔬菜。

back.grammar: 「There’s...」口語中常用來列舉，即使後面接多個東西，美國人口語也常說「There’s chicken, steak...」。
variations: We have chicken, steak, carnitas, sofritas, and grilled veggies.
Your options are chicken, steak, carnitas, sofritas, and veggies.
For protein, we’ve got chicken, steak, carnitas, sofritas, and grilled veggies.

keyPhrases.zh: Chipotle 常見的辣味豆腐素食蛋白

extraExamples.en: There’s coffee, tea, and lemonade.
extraExamples.zh: 有咖啡、茶和檸檬水。

extraExamples.en: We’ve got chicken, fish, and a veggie option.
extraExamples.zh: 我們有雞肉、魚肉，還有一個蔬食選項。

sentences.en: Can you do chicken and veggies, half and half?
sentences.zh: 可以雞肉和蔬菜各一半嗎？

back.grammar: 「Can you do...?」在點餐時是「可以幫我做成……嗎？」；「half and half」表示各一半。
variations: Can I do half chicken, half veggies?
Could I get chicken and veggies, half and half?
Can you make it half chicken and half veggies?

extraExamples.en: Can you do half rice and half salad?
extraExamples.zh: 可以飯和沙拉各一半嗎？

extraExamples.en: Could I get the drink half sweet tea and half lemonade?
extraExamples.zh: 飲料可以半甜茶半檸檬水嗎？

sentences.en: I think so, yes of course! Any salsa? Mild, medium, or hot?
sentences.zh: 可以的，當然！要哪種莎莎醬？溫和、中辣還是辣？

back.grammar: 「Any salsa?」是省略問句，完整是「Would you like any salsa?」；「mild, medium, or hot」是辣度選項。
variations: Would you like any salsa—mild, medium, or hot?
Do you want salsa? We have mild, medium, and hot.
Any salsa for you? Mild, medium, or spicy?

extraExamples.en: Any dressing on your salad?
extraExamples.zh: 你的沙拉要加醬嗎？

extraExamples.en: Do you want it mild, medium, or hot?
extraExamples.zh: 你要溫和、中辣還是辣？

sentences.en: The green one looks good to me.
sentences.zh: 我覺得那個綠色的看起來不錯。

back.grammar: 「looks good to me」表示「我覺得看起來不錯／可以」，是很自然的口語判斷。
variations: The green one looks good.
I’ll try the green one.
That green one sounds good.

extraExamples.en: That sandwich looks good to me.
extraExamples.zh: 那個三明治我覺得看起來不錯。

extraExamples.en: The blue shirt looks good to me.
extraExamples.zh: 那件藍色襯衫我覺得看起來不錯。

sentences.en: You got it. That’s the medium one. It’s also my personal favorite! Anything else?
sentences.zh: 好的，那是中辣的，也是我個人最喜歡的！還需要什麼嗎？

back.grammar: 「Anything else?」是店員常用來問「還需要其他的嗎？」；「personal favorite」表示個人最愛。
variations: Sure. That’s the medium one. It’s my favorite, too. Anything else?
Got it. That’s medium. It’s actually my favorite. What else?
No problem. That one’s medium. Anything else for you?

extraExamples.en: This drink is my personal favorite.
extraExamples.zh: 這杯飲料是我個人最喜歡的。

extraExamples.en: Anything else, or is that all for today?
extraExamples.zh: 還需要其他的嗎？還是今天就這樣？

sentences.en: Corn, cheese, and guac.
sentences.zh: 玉米、起司和酪梨醬。

back.grammar: 點餐時可以直接列出想加的配料；「guac」是「guacamole」的口語縮寫。
variations: I’ll take corn, cheese, and guac.
Can I get corn, cheese, and guac?
Add corn, cheese, and guac, please.

keyPhrases.zh: 酪梨醬，guacamole 的口語縮寫

extraExamples.en: Can I get cheese and guac on the side?
extraExamples.zh: 我可以要起司和酪梨醬另外放嗎？

extraExamples.en: Add corn and cheese, please.
extraExamples.zh: 請加玉米和起司。

sentences.en: Guac is extra. Is that okay?
sentences.zh: 酪梨醬要額外加錢，可以嗎？

back.grammar: 「extra」在點餐時常指「要額外加錢」；「Is that okay?」是在確認客人是否接受。
variations: Guac costs extra. Is that alright?
There’s an extra charge for guac. Is that okay?
Just so you know, guac is extra.

extraExamples.en: Cheese is extra. Is that okay?
extraExamples.zh: 起司要額外加錢，可以嗎？

extraExamples.en: Just so you know, delivery is extra.
extraExamples.zh: 先跟你說，外送要另外收費。

sentences.en: Yes.
sentences.zh: 可以。

back.grammar: 簡短回答 “Yes.” 可表示同意或可以；在點餐情境中很常用。
variations: Yeah, sure.
Yes, please.
That works.

extraExamples.en: Yes, that’s fine.
extraExamples.zh: 可以，那樣沒問題。

extraExamples.en: Yes, I’ll take it.
extraExamples.zh: 可以，我要這個。

sentences.en: Any chips and guac?
sentences.zh: 要加薯片和酪梨醬嗎？

back.grammar: “Any...?” 是省略句，完整可理解為 “Do you want any...?”，點餐時很常見。
variations: Would you like any chips and guac?
Do you want chips and guac with that?
Want to add chips and guac?

extraExamples.en: Any drinks with that?
extraExamples.zh: 要搭配飲料嗎？

extraExamples.en: Any salsa on the side?
extraExamples.zh: 要另外附莎莎醬嗎？

sentences.en: Just a small bag of chips.
sentences.zh: 就一小袋薯片。

back.grammar: “Just” 在點餐中表示「只要、就好」，語氣自然又簡潔。
variations: Just the small chips.
I’ll just do a small bag of chips.
A small bag of chips is fine.

extraExamples.en: Just a water, please.
extraExamples.zh: 只要一杯水，謝謝。

extraExamples.en: I’ll just get a small coffee.
extraExamples.zh: 我就要一杯小杯咖啡。

sentences.en: Your total is $18.50 today. Cash or card?
sentences.zh: 今天總共是 $18.50，付現還是刷卡？

back.grammar: “Your total is...” 是結帳告知金額的固定說法；“Cash or card?” 是省略問句。
variations: That’ll be $18.50. Cash or card?
Your total comes to $18.50. Will that be cash or card?
It’s $18.50 today. Paying with cash or card?

extraExamples.en: Your total is $12.75. Cash or card?
extraExamples.zh: 總共是 12.75 美元。付現還是刷卡？

extraExamples.en: That’ll be $9.20. Will that be cash or card?
extraExamples.zh: 總共 9.20 美元。要付現還是刷卡？

sentences.en: Card.
sentences.zh: 刷卡。

back.grammar: 用單字 “Card.” 回答付款方式很自然，完整句是 “I’ll pay by card.”
variations: I’ll use a card.
Card, please.
I’ll pay with a card.

extraExamples.en: I’ll pay with a card today.
extraExamples.zh: 我今天用卡付款。

extraExamples.en: Card is fine.
extraExamples.zh: 刷卡可以。

sentences.en: You’re all set. Have a good one!
sentences.zh: 都完成了，祝你今天愉快！

back.grammar: “You’re all set.” 是服務業常用口語，表示「都好了、完成了」；“Have a good one” 是美式道別祝福。
variations: You’re good to go. Have a great day!
All set. Take care!
You’re all done. Have a good day!

extraExamples.en: You’re all set. Your receipt is in the bag.
extraExamples.zh: 都好了。你的收據在袋子裡。

extraExamples.en: Thanks for stopping by. Have a good one!
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝你光臨。祝你今天愉快！

sentences.en: You as well!
sentences.zh: 你也一樣！

back.grammar: “You as well!” 是回應祝福的禮貌說法，意思是「你也一樣」。
variations: You too!
Same to you!
Thanks, you too!

extraExamples.en: Have a great weekend! You as well!
extraExamples.zh: 週末愉快！你也一樣！

extraExamples.en: Enjoy your day. Thanks, you too!
extraExamples.zh: 祝你今天愉快。謝謝，你也是！

sentences.en: How can I help you?
sentences.zh: 需要我幫忙嗎？

back.grammar: “How can I help you?” 是店員或客服招呼客人的標準開場句。
variations: What can I get for you?
How can I help you today?
What can I do for you?

keyPhrases.phrase: What can I get for you?

extraExamples.en: Hi there, how can I help you?
extraExamples.zh: 哈囉，需要我幫忙嗎？

extraExamples.en: Welcome in! What can I get for you?
extraExamples.zh: 歡迎光臨！你想點什麼？

sentences.en: Which flavor is the most popular one here?
sentences.zh: 這裡最受歡迎的口味是什麼？

back.grammar: “Which ... is the most popular?” 用來詢問「哪一個最受歡迎」；最高級 “the most popular” 前通常加 the。
variations: What’s your most popular flavor?
Which one do people usually get here?
What flavor is everyone getting?

extraExamples.en: Which drink is the most popular here?
extraExamples.zh: 這裡最受歡迎的飲料是哪一種？

extraExamples.en: What’s your most popular sandwich?
extraExamples.zh: 你們最受歡迎的三明治是什麼？

sentences.en: Chocolate peanut butter cup and triple chocolate.
sentences.zh: 巧克力花生醬杯和三重巧克力。

back.grammar: 回答品項時可直接列出名稱，省略完整句 “The most popular ones are...”。
variations: The chocolate peanut butter cup and the triple chocolate are big sellers.
People love the chocolate peanut butter cup and triple chocolate.
Our most popular are chocolate peanut butter cup and triple chocolate.

extraExamples.en: The vanilla bean and salted caramel are big sellers.
extraExamples.zh: 香草籽和海鹽焦糖都很熱賣。

extraExamples.en: People love the strawberry cheesecake flavor.
extraExamples.zh: 大家很喜歡草莓起司蛋糕口味。

sentences.en: Cool, I’ll have those. Hmmm… Can I get a box of six?
sentences.zh: 酷，那我就要這些吧。嗯……我可以要六個裝一盒的嗎？

back.grammar: “I’ll have...” 是點餐常用句；“Can I get...?” 在美式口語中等於「我可以要……嗎？」
variations: Nice, I’ll take those. Can I do a box of six?
Great, I’ll get those. Could I get six in a box?
Sounds good. Can I get a six-pack?

extraExamples.en: I’ll have the chicken sandwich. Can I get fries with that?
extraExamples.zh: 我要雞肉三明治。可以搭薯條嗎？

extraExamples.en: Can I get a box of twelve donuts?
extraExamples.zh: 我可以要一盒十二個甜甜圈嗎？

sentences.en: Okay. Which ones do you want in the box?
sentences.zh: 好的，盒子裡要哪幾種？

back.grammar: “Which ones do you want...?” 用 “ones” 代替前面提過的品項，避免重複。
variations: Sure. Which flavors would you like in the box?
Okay. What do you want in the box?
Got it. Which ones should I put in there?

extraExamples.en: Which ones do you want to try?
extraExamples.zh: 你想試哪幾種？

extraExamples.en: Which flavors should I put in the bag?
extraExamples.zh: 我要把哪幾種口味放進袋子？

sentences.en: I’m gonna get the peanut butter cup and triple chocolate, two of each. And one of the s’mores. And the last one’s gonna be the classic chocolate chunk.
sentences.zh: 我要兩個花生醬杯、兩個三重巧克力、一個棉花糖口味，最後一個經典巧克力塊。

back.grammar: “I’m gonna get...” 是 “I’m going to get...” 的口語縮讀；“two of each” 表示「每種兩個」。
variations: I’ll do two peanut butter cup, two triple chocolate, one s’mores, and one classic chocolate chunk.
Let’s do two of each of those, plus one s’mores and one chocolate chunk.
I’ll get two peanut butter cup, two triple chocolate, one s’mores, and the last one chocolate chunk.

extraExamples.en: I’m gonna get two coffees and one iced tea.
extraExamples.zh: 我要兩杯咖啡和一杯冰茶。

extraExamples.en: We’ll take two of each, please.
extraExamples.zh: 每種我們要兩個，謝謝。

sentences.en: Ok, let me get those packed for you.
sentences.zh: 好的，我幫你打包。

back.grammar: “Let me...” 是口語中表示「我來幫你……」；“get those packed” 表示把那些東西打包好。
variations: Sure, I’ll pack those up for you.
Okay, I’ll get those boxed up.
No problem, let me box those up for you.

extraExamples.en: Let me grab a bag for you.
extraExamples.zh: 我幫你拿個袋子。

extraExamples.en: I’ll get these packed up for you.
extraExamples.zh: 我幫你把這些打包好。

sentences.en: Ok, it’s gonna be a box of six. Two peanut butter cup, two triple chocolate, one s’mores, and one chocolate chunk.
sentences.zh: 好的，是一盒六塊。兩個花生醬杯、兩個三重巧克力、一個棉花糖口味和一個巧克力塊。

back.grammar: “It’s gonna be...” 是 “It is going to be...” 的口語縮讀，店員確認訂單時常用。
variations: Okay, so that’s a box of six: two peanut butter cup, two triple chocolate, one s’mores, and one chocolate chunk.
Got it. A box of six with two peanut butter cup, two triple chocolate, one s’mores, and one chocolate chunk.
Alright, I have a six-pack: two peanut butter cup, two triple chocolate, one s’mores, and one chocolate chunk.

extraExamples.en: It’s gonna be one latte, one cold brew, and a muffin.
extraExamples.zh: 是一杯拿鐵、一杯冷萃和一個馬芬。

extraExamples.en: So that’s a box of six with three glazed and three chocolate.
extraExamples.zh: 所以是一盒六個，三個糖霜和三個巧克力。

sentences.en: Yes, that’s correct.
sentences.zh: 對，沒錯。

back.grammar: That’s correct. 是確認資訊正確的常用說法，比單說 yes 更完整、禮貌。
variations: Yep, that’s right.
Yes, exactly.
That’s it.

extraExamples.en: Yes, that’s correct. My appointment is at 3 p.m.
extraExamples.zh: 對，沒錯。我的預約是下午三點。

extraExamples.en: That’s correct—two coffees and one bagel.
extraExamples.zh: 沒錯，兩杯咖啡和一個貝果。

sentences.en: Your total is going to be $18.50. Cash or card?
sentences.zh: 總共是 $18.50，付現還是刷卡？

back.grammar: is going to be 常用來告知金額或結果，語氣比直接說 is 更自然、服務業很常見。
variations: Your total comes to $18.50. Cash or card?
That’ll be $18.50. Will that be cash or card?
It’s $18.50 altogether. Cash or card?

keyPhrases.zh: 將會是；這裡用來報金額

extraExamples.en: Your total is going to be $42.10 today.
extraExamples.zh: 你今天的總金額會是 42.10 美元。

extraExamples.en: That’ll be $9.75. Cash or card?
extraExamples.zh: 總共 9.75 美元。付現還是刷卡？

sentences.en: Card, please.
sentences.zh: 刷卡，謝謝。

back.grammar: Card, please. 是省略句，完整意思是 I’ll pay by card, please.，點餐付款時很自然。
variations: I’ll use a card, please.
Card is fine.
I’ll pay by card.

extraExamples.en: I’ll pay by card, please.
extraExamples.zh: 我用卡付款，謝謝。

extraExamples.en: Card is fine, thanks.
extraExamples.zh: 刷卡就好，謝謝。

sentences.en: Did you need the receipt printed?
sentences.zh: 需要列印收據嗎？

back.grammar: Did you need...? 是服務業常用的禮貌問法，雖然用過去式 did，但意思是現在是否需要。
variations: Would you like a printed receipt?
Do you need a receipt printed?
Should I print the receipt for you?

extraExamples.en: Did you need a bag for that?
extraExamples.zh: 你需要袋子裝這個嗎？

extraExamples.en: Would you like the receipt printed or emailed?
extraExamples.zh: 你想要紙本收據還是電子收據？

sentences.en: No, it’s okay.
sentences.zh: 不用，沒關係。

back.grammar: No, it’s okay. 在口語中表示「不用了／沒關係」，語氣比直接說 no 柔和。
variations: No, that’s okay.
No receipt, thanks.
I’m good, thanks.

extraExamples.en: No, it’s okay. I don’t need a bag.
extraExamples.zh: 不用，沒關係。我不需要袋子。

extraExamples.en: I’m good, thanks. I’ll carry it.
extraExamples.zh: 不用了，謝謝。我自己拿就好。

sentences.en: Alrighty! Your cookies will be out in just a second. You’ll love ‘em!
sentences.zh: 好的！餅乾馬上就好，你一定會喜歡的！

back.grammar: will be out 表示食物「做好／出爐」；’em 是 them 的口語縮短，常見於輕鬆對話。
variations: Alright! Your cookies will be ready in just a minute. You’re gonna love them!
Sounds good! They’ll be out in a sec. You’ll love ’em!
Okay! Your cookies are almost ready. I think you’ll really like them!

extraExamples.en: Your coffee will be out in just a second.
extraExamples.zh: 你的咖啡馬上就好。

extraExamples.en: Try these fries—you’ll love ’em.
extraExamples.zh: 試試這些薯條，你一定會喜歡的。

sentences.en: I know I will!
sentences.zh: 我知道我會的！

back.grammar: I know I will! 是回應對方預測時的自然說法，will 代替前面提到的動作，避免重複。
variations: I’m sure I will!
I bet I will!
I know, I can’t wait!

extraExamples.en: You’re going to have fun at the party. — I know I will!
extraExamples.zh: 你在派對一定會玩得很開心。— 我知道我會的！

extraExamples.en: You’ll like this movie. — I’m sure I will!
extraExamples.zh: 你會喜歡這部電影的。— 我相信我會的！

units.title: 購物與結帳
units.scenario: Cheryl去Forever21買衣服

sentences.en: Hi, welcome! Are you looking for anything specific today?
sentences.zh: 嗨，歡迎光臨！今天有特別想找的東西嗎？

back.grammar: 店員招呼客人時常用現在進行式 “Are you looking for…?”，語氣比 “Do you want…?” 更自然、委婉。
variations: Hi there! Looking for anything in particular today?
Welcome in! Can I help you find something?
Hey! Are you shopping for anything specific?

keyPhrases.zh: 今天；在服務情境中常用來讓語氣更親切

extraExamples.en: Are you looking for a gift for someone?
extraExamples.zh: 你是在找送人的禮物嗎？

extraExamples.en: I’m looking for something comfortable for work.
extraExamples.zh: 我在找上班穿舒服一點的東西。

sentences.en: No, I’m just browsing.
sentences.zh: 沒有，我只是隨便看看。

back.grammar: “I’m just browsing.” 是逛店時很常用的口語，表示「只是看看」，不需要店員幫忙。
variations: I’m just looking, thanks.
Just browsing for now.
I’m good, just taking a look.

extraExamples.en: Thanks, but I’m just browsing for now.
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝，但我目前只是隨便看看。

extraExamples.en: We’re just taking a look before dinner.
extraExamples.zh: 我們只是晚餐前先逛逛看看。

sentences.en: Lemme know if you need help with anything!
sentences.zh: 好的，有需要幫忙的話隨時告訴我！

back.grammar: “Lemme” 是 “let me” 的口語縮讀，常出現在輕鬆、友善的對話中。
variations: Let me know if you need anything.
Just give me a shout if you need help.
If you need a hand, I’ll be right over here.

extraExamples.en: Lemme know if you want a different size.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你想要別的尺寸，跟我說。

extraExamples.en: Give me a shout if you can’t find the fitting room.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你找不到試衣間，叫我一下。

sentences.en: Thanks!
sentences.zh: 謝謝！

back.grammar: “Thanks!” 比 “Thank you.” 更口語、輕鬆，日常互動中很常用。
variations: Thank you!
Thanks a lot!
Appreciate it!

extraExamples.en: Thanks! I’ll take a look.
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝！我看一下。

extraExamples.en: Appreciate it! That was really helpful.
extraExamples.zh: 謝啦！那真的很有幫助。

sentences.en: This looks so nice… let me try it on. Excuse me, where is the fitting room?
sentences.zh: 哇！這件看起來好漂亮……我想試穿一下。不好意思，請問試衣間在哪裡？

back.grammar: “let me + 原形動詞” 表示「讓我來……」，像 “let me try it on” 就是「我試穿看看」。
variations: This is really cute. Can I try it on? Where are the fitting rooms?
I love this. I’m gonna try it on—where’s the fitting room?
This looks great. Excuse me, where can I try it on?

extraExamples.en: This jacket looks nice. Let me try it on.
extraExamples.zh: 這件外套看起來不錯，讓我試穿看看。

extraExamples.en: Excuse me, where is the fitting room?
extraExamples.zh: 不好意思，請問試衣間在哪裡？

sentences.en: Straight down the hall way to the right.
sentences.zh: 一直走過走廊，然後右轉。

back.grammar: 指路常用 “straight down…” 表示「沿著……一直走」，再用 “to the right/left” 說方向。
variations: Go straight down the hallway, then it’s on your right.
Head down the hall and turn right.
It’s down the hallway on the right.

extraExamples.en: Go straight down the hall, and the restroom is on your left.
extraExamples.zh: 沿著走廊一直走，洗手間在你的左手邊。

extraExamples.en: The fitting rooms are down the hallway to the right.
extraExamples.zh: 試衣間在走廊直走後右邊。

sentences.en: Ok, thanks! I’ll go try it on.
sentences.zh: 好的，謝謝！我去試穿看看。

back.grammar: “I’ll + 原形動詞” 表示臨時決定或接下來要做的事，這裡是「我去試穿看看」。
variations: Great, thanks. I’ll try it on.
Okay, thank you. I’m gonna go try it on.
Got it, thanks. I’ll head to the fitting room.

extraExamples.en: I’ll go ask the cashier.
extraExamples.zh: 我去問收銀員。

extraExamples.en: I’ll try on the smaller size first.
extraExamples.zh: 我先試穿小一號的。

sentences.en: Woah, I really like how it fits.I just wish that there is a beige one. Let me go ask the staff.
sentences.zh: 哇，我真的很喜歡這件的版型。不過我希望有米色的款式。我去問一下店員。

back.grammar: “I like how it fits” 是稱讚衣服版型/穿起來合身的自然說法；“I wish there were/was…” 比 “I wish there is…” 更標準自然。
variations: Wow, I love the fit. I just wish it came in beige.
Whoa, this fits really well. I’m gonna ask if they have it in beige.
I really like the way this looks on me. Let me ask the staff about other colors.

keyPhrases.zh: 它穿起來的版型／合身度

extraExamples.en: I love how these jeans fit, but I wish they came in black.
extraExamples.zh: 我很喜歡這條牛仔褲的版型，但希望它有黑色。

extraExamples.en: Let me go ask the staff if they have another size.
extraExamples.zh: 我去問店員有沒有別的尺寸。

sentences.en: Excuse me, do you have this dress in a different color?
sentences.zh: 不好意思，這件有其他顏色嗎？

back.grammar: “Do you have this in…?” 是購物時詢問顏色、尺寸、款式的萬用句型。
variations: Do you have this in any other colors?
Does this dress come in a different color?
Can I get this dress in another color?

extraExamples.en: Do you have this shirt in a medium?
extraExamples.zh: 這件襯衫有 M 號嗎？

extraExamples.en: Does this bag come in brown?
extraExamples.zh: 這個包有棕色的嗎？

sentences.en: I can help you with that. (Pause) Let’s see… it is available online and in store. And it comes in these colors. Burgundy, navy, baby blue, and baby pink.
sentences.zh: 我可以幫你查一下。（停頓）讓我看看……這款有線上和門市都可以買到，有以下幾種顏色：酒紅色、藏青色、粉藍色和粉紅色。

back.grammar: “Let’s see…” 是查資料或思考時常用的口語填充語；“available online and in store” 表示線上與門市都有。
variations: Sure, let me check. It looks like we have it online and in store.
I can look that up for you. It comes in burgundy, navy, baby blue, and baby pink.
Let’s see… yes, it’s available both online and in stores, in these colors.

keyPhrases.phrase: I can help you with that

extraExamples.en: Let’s see… this sweater is available online, but not in store.
extraExamples.zh: 我看看……這件毛衣線上有，但門市沒有。

extraExamples.en: These shoes come in black, white, and gray.
extraExamples.zh: 這雙鞋有黑色、白色和灰色。

sentences.en: Ah I was hoping it comes in beige.
sentences.zh: 啊，我真希望有米色的。

back.grammar: “I was hoping…” 用過去進行式讓願望聽起來更委婉；這裡自然說法是 “I was hoping it came in beige.”
variations: Oh, I was hoping it came in beige.
Ah, I really wanted a beige one.
Shoot, I was looking for something in beige.

extraExamples.en: I was hoping they had this in a smaller size.
extraExamples.zh: 我本來希望這件有小一點的尺寸。

extraExamples.en: She was hoping the table came in white.
extraExamples.zh: 她本來希望那張桌子有白色款。

sentences.en: I can check if there’s something similar in store. Are you looking for a going out dress or a more casual dress?
sentences.zh: 我可以幫你看看店裡有沒有類似的款式。妳是想找外出穿的洋裝還是比較休閒的？

back.grammar: “if there’s something similar” 是間接問法，語氣自然；“Are you looking for A or B?” 用來縮小選項。
variations: I can see if we have anything similar in store. Do you want something dressy or more casual?
Let me check for a similar style. Are you thinking of a going-out dress or an everyday dress?
I can look around for something like that. Do you want it more fancy or casual?

keyPhrases.zh: 外出約會／聚會穿的洋裝

extraExamples.en: I can check if there’s something similar in your size.
extraExamples.zh: 我可以幫你查有沒有類似款是你的尺寸。

extraExamples.en: Are you looking for a work bag or a travel bag?
extraExamples.zh: 你是在找上班包還是旅行包？

sentences.en: A casual one.
sentences.zh: 休閒一點的。

back.grammar: 省略句 “A casual one.” 是自然回答，完整句是 “I’m looking for a casual one.”
variations: Something casual.
A more casual dress.
I’m looking for something casual.

extraExamples.en: I need something casual for the weekend.
extraExamples.zh: 我需要週末穿的休閒款。

extraExamples.en: Do you have a more casual one?
extraExamples.zh: 你們有比較休閒一點的款式嗎？

sentences.en: Do you want a maxi dress like this one or midi and mini are also okay?
sentences.zh: 你想要像這樣的長裙，還是中長裙跟短裙也可以嗎？

back.grammar: 列舉選項時可用 “A or B?”；更自然的說法是 “Are midi and mini okay too?” 表示「中長或短的也可以嗎？」
variations: Do you want a maxi dress like this, or are midi and mini okay too?
Are you looking for a maxi, or would a midi or mini work?
Do you prefer a long dress, or are shorter styles okay?

extraExamples.en: Are you looking for a maxi dress for the wedding?
extraExamples.zh: 你是在找婚禮要穿的長洋裝嗎？

extraExamples.en: Would a midi dress work for the office party?
extraExamples.zh: 中長洋裝適合辦公室派對嗎？

sentences.en: I’d prefer a maxi or midi one.
sentences.zh: 我比較喜歡長裙或中長裙。

back.grammar: “I’d prefer…” 是 “I would prefer…” 的縮寫，比 “I want…” 更委婉、有禮貌。
variations: I’d rather go with a maxi or midi.
I’m leaning toward a maxi or midi dress.
A maxi or midi would be better for me.

extraExamples.en: I’d prefer a table by the window.
extraExamples.zh: 我比較想要靠窗的位子。

extraExamples.en: I’m leaning toward the navy one.
extraExamples.zh: 我比較傾向藏青色那款。

sentences.en: Sleeveless, shortsleeves, longsleeves, strapless, or no preference?
sentences.zh: （袖子要）無袖、短袖、長袖、平肩還是沒特別偏好？

back.grammar: 店員詢問偏好時常用名詞/形容詞並列，最後加上 “or no preference?” 表示「還是都可以？」
variations: Do you want sleeveless, short sleeves, long sleeves, strapless, or does it not matter?
Any preference on sleeves—sleeveless, short-sleeve, long-sleeve, or strapless?
What kind of sleeves are you looking for, if any?

extraExamples.en: Do you have a color preference, or no preference?
extraExamples.zh: 你有顏色偏好嗎，還是都可以？

extraExamples.en: I’m looking for something sleeveless for summer.
extraExamples.zh: 我想找一件夏天穿的無袖款。

sentences.en: A sleeveless or strapless one would be better.
sentences.zh: 無袖或平肩的比較好。

back.grammar: “would be better” 是委婉表達偏好的說法，比直接說 “I want...” 更柔和。
variations: Sleeveless or strapless would work better.
I think sleeveless or strapless would be best.
I’d prefer a sleeveless or strapless one.

extraExamples.en: A smaller size would be better.
extraExamples.zh: 小一號會比較好。

extraExamples.en: I’d prefer something in black or navy.
extraExamples.zh: 我比較想要黑色或深藍色的。

sentences.en: You got it. We’re looking at three options on screen here- one is a sleeveless denim maxi with the buttons, another one is a midi crew neck black and white striped knit dress, and the other one looks the closest to the one you’re holding… it’s a beige strapless midi dress with a satin texture.
sentences.zh: 好的。螢幕上有三個選項：第一個是無袖的牛仔長裙，帶有釦子的設計；第二個是中長款圓領黑白條紋針織裙；第三個看起來最接近妳手上的那件……是一件米色的中長款平肩緞面洋裝。

back.grammar: “You got it.” 是美式口語，表示「沒問題／了解」；介紹選項時常用 “one is..., another one is..., and the other one...” 來列舉。
variations: Got it. We’ve got three options here on the screen.
Sure thing. There are three choices here.
Absolutely. I’m seeing three options that might work.

extraExamples.en: You got it. I’ll check the back for your size.
extraExamples.zh: 沒問題。我去後面幫你查一下尺寸。

extraExamples.en: This jacket is the closest to the one you showed me.
extraExamples.zh: 這件外套最接近你給我看的那件。

sentences.en: I’ll take the last one.
sentences.zh: 我要最後那件。

back.grammar: “I’ll take...” 是購物時決定購買的自然說法，意思是「我要買……」。
variations: I’ll go with the last one.
I’ll get the last one.
Let’s do the last one.

extraExamples.en: I’ll take the blue one.
extraExamples.zh: 我要藍色那個。

extraExamples.en: I’ll go with the medium.
extraExamples.zh: 我選中號。

sentences.en: No problem. I’ll bring it to the fitting room for you.
sentences.zh: 沒問題，我幫妳拿到試衣間去。

back.grammar: “I’ll bring it...” 用未來式表示接下來要幫客人做的事；“for you” 讓語氣更服務、親切。
variations: Sure. I’ll take it to the fitting room for you.
No worries. I can bring that over to the fitting room.
Of course. I’ll put it in a fitting room for you.

extraExamples.en: I’ll bring a smaller size to the fitting room for you.
extraExamples.zh: 我幫你拿小一號到試衣間。

extraExamples.en: No problem. I’ll grab that for you.
extraExamples.zh: 沒問題。我幫你拿那個。

sentences.en: I’ll take this one. Where is the register… Ah found it.
sentences.zh: 我要這件。收銀台在哪裡……啊，找到了。

back.grammar: “I’ll take this one.” 表示決定購買；“Ah, found it.” 口語中常省略主詞 “I”。
variations: I’ll get this one. Where’s the register? Oh, there it is.
I’m gonna take this one. Where do I check out? Oh, I found it.
I’ll go with this one. Where’s checkout? Ah, got it.

extraExamples.en: I’ll take this one, but can I get a new one from the back?
extraExamples.zh: 我要買這件，但可以幫我拿後面新的嗎？

extraExamples.en: Where’s the register? I’m ready to pay.
extraExamples.zh: 收銀台在哪？我準備結帳了。

sentences.en: Are you a member with us?
sentences.zh: 請問妳是我們的會員嗎？

back.grammar: “Are you a member with us?” 是店員詢問會員身分的常見說法；“with us” 指「我們店／我們品牌」。
variations: Do you have a membership with us?
Are you in our rewards program?
Do you have an account with us?

extraExamples.en: Are you a member with us, or would you like to sign up?
extraExamples.zh: 你是我們的會員嗎，還是想註冊？

extraExamples.en: Do you have an account with us under your phone number?
extraExamples.zh: 你有用電話號碼建立我們的帳號嗎？

sentences.en: Nope.
sentences.zh: 不是。

back.grammar: “Nope.” 是比 “No.” 更口語、輕鬆的否定回答。
variations: Nope, I’m not.
No, I don’t.
Not yet.

extraExamples.en: Nope, I haven’t been here before.
extraExamples.zh: 沒有，我之前沒來過這裡。

extraExamples.en: Not yet, but I might sign up later.
extraExamples.zh: 還沒有，但我之後可能會加入。

sentences.en: Would you like to join our membership to get 10% off the lowest priced item and a gift of your choice?
sentences.zh: 要不要加入我們的會員？可以享受最低價單品九折和一份禮物。

back.grammar: “Would you like to...?” 是禮貌提議；“to get...” 說明加入會員的目的或好處。
variations: Do you want to sign up and get 10% off the cheapest item plus a free gift?
Would you like to join for 10% off your lowest priced item and a gift?
You can sign up today for 10% off one item and choose a free gift.

extraExamples.en: Would you like to sign up to get free shipping?
extraExamples.zh: 你想註冊會員來獲得免運嗎？

extraExamples.en: You can get 20% off your first order if you join today.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你今天加入，第一筆訂單可以打八折。

sentences.en: Nah, I’m good.
sentences.zh: 不用了，謝謝。

back.grammar: “Nah, I’m good.” 是美式口語中委婉拒絕推銷或幫助的說法，意思是「不用了，謝謝」。
variations: No thanks, I’m good.
I’ll pass, thanks.
Not today, thanks.

extraExamples.en: Nah, I’m good. I don’t need a receipt.
extraExamples.zh: 不用了，謝謝。我不需要收據。

extraExamples.en: I’ll pass on the membership today.
extraExamples.zh: 今天會員我先不用加入。

sentences.en: Your total’s gonna be $87.63 today. Cash or card?
sentences.zh: 今天的總金額是87.63美元。你要支付現金還是刷卡？

back.grammar: “Your total’s gonna be...” 是結帳常用句；“gonna” 是 “going to” 的口語縮讀。
variations: Your total comes to $87.63. Cash or card?
That’ll be $87.63 today. Will that be cash or card?
Your total is $87.63. How would you like to pay?

extraExamples.en: Your total’s gonna be $42.18 today.
extraExamples.zh: 你今天的總金額是42.18美元。

extraExamples.en: That’ll be $15. Do you want to pay cash or card?
extraExamples.zh: 總共15美元。你要付現還是刷卡？

sentences.en: Card.
sentences.zh: 刷卡。

back.grammar: 購物結帳時可以直接用單字回答 “Card.”，簡短自然。
variations: Card, please.
I’ll do card.
I’ll pay by card.

extraExamples.en: Card, please. Do I tap here?
extraExamples.zh: 刷卡，謝謝。我是感應這裡嗎？

extraExamples.en: I’ll pay by card this time.
extraExamples.zh: 這次我用卡付款。

sentences.en: When you’re ready, it’ll ask you a question on the screen.
sentences.zh: 當妳準備好後，螢幕上會有提示問題。

back.grammar: “When you’re ready...” 是服務場合常用的溫和指示；“it’ll ask you...” 中的 “it” 指刷卡機或螢幕。
variations: Whenever you’re ready, just answer the question on the screen.
It’ll prompt you on the screen when you’re ready.
Go ahead when you’re ready. The screen will ask you a question.

keyPhrases.zh: 問你一個問題；跳出提示

extraExamples.en: When you’re ready, you can tap your card.
extraExamples.zh: 你準備好時，可以感應你的卡。

extraExamples.en: The machine will ask you if you want a receipt.
extraExamples.zh: 機器會問你要不要收據。

sentences.en: Ok, enter tip amount… here you go. Submit.
sentences.zh: 好的，輸入小費金額……好了，送出。

back.grammar: “Here you go.” 可用在把東西交給對方，或表示「好了／完成了」；這裡是在操作螢幕時自言自語。
variations: Okay, I’ll enter the tip amount… there we go. Submit.
Alright, tip amount… done. Submit.
Okay, put in the tip… here we go. Submit.

extraExamples.en: Enter your phone number here, then hit submit.
extraExamples.zh: 在這裡輸入你的電話號碼，然後按送出。

extraExamples.en: Here you go—your receipt is in the bag.
extraExamples.zh: 給你／好了，你的收據在袋子裡。

sentences.en: Did you need a bag?
sentences.zh: 需要袋子嗎？

back.grammar: “Did you need...?” 在美式服務業常用來禮貌詢問需求，雖是過去式但意思是現在「需要嗎？」
variations: Do you need a bag?
Would you like a bag?
Do you want a bag for this?

extraExamples.en: Did you need a receipt today?
extraExamples.zh: 你今天需要收據嗎？

extraExamples.en: Would you like a bag for your clothes?
extraExamples.zh: 你的衣服需要袋子嗎？

sentences.en: Yes, please.
sentences.zh: 需要，謝謝。

back.grammar: 「Yes, please.」是禮貌接受對方提議或詢問時最自然、簡短的回答。
variations: Sure, thanks.
Yes, that’d be great.
Please do, thank you.

extraExamples.en: Would you like a bag? Yes, please.
extraExamples.zh: 你需要袋子嗎？需要，謝謝。

extraExamples.en: Can I get you some water? Yes, please. That’d be great.
extraExamples.zh: 要我幫你拿點水嗎？好，謝謝。那太好了。

sentences.en: Do you want a copy of your receipt?
sentences.zh: 請問你要收據嗎？

back.grammar: 「Do you want...?」是日常口語中詢問對方是否需要某物的常見句型，比較直接但很自然。
variations: Would you like a copy of your receipt?
Do you need a receipt?
Would you like your receipt printed?

extraExamples.en: Do you want a bag for that?
extraExamples.zh: 你要袋子裝那個嗎？

extraExamples.en: Would you like a copy of the form?
extraExamples.zh: 你需要一份表格副本嗎？

sentences.en: No, it’s okay.
sentences.zh: 不用，沒關係。

back.grammar: 「No, it’s okay.」是婉拒別人提供的東西或幫忙時的口語說法，語氣比單說 No 更客氣。
variations: No, I’m good, thanks.
No, that’s okay.
I’m all set, thank you.

extraExamples.en: Do you need help carrying that? No, it’s okay.
extraExamples.zh: 你需要幫忙拿那個嗎？不用，沒關係。

extraExamples.en: Want anything else? No, I’m good, thanks.
extraExamples.zh: 還需要別的嗎？不用了，謝謝。

sentences.en: You’re all set. Have a wonderful day!
sentences.zh: 好了，祝妳有美好的一天！

back.grammar: 「You’re all set.」是服務業常用口語，表示事情已經辦好了、可以離開了。
variations: You’re good to go. Have a great day!
That’s everything. Have a nice day!
You’re all done. Enjoy the rest of your day!

extraExamples.en: Your order is paid for. You’re all set.
extraExamples.zh: 你的訂單已付款。都好了。

extraExamples.en: I fixed the settings, so you’re good to go.
extraExamples.zh: 我把設定修好了，所以你可以用了。

sentences.en: Thanks, you as well!
sentences.zh: 謝謝，妳也是！

back.grammar: 「you as well」是較有禮貌的回應，意思是「你也是」，常用來回祝對方。
variations: Thanks, you too!
Thank you, same to you!
Thanks, have a good one!

extraExamples.en: Have a great weekend! Thanks, you as well!
extraExamples.zh: 週末愉快！謝謝，你也是！

extraExamples.en: Good luck on your presentation. Thanks, same to you!
extraExamples.zh: 祝你簡報順利。謝謝，也祝你一樣！

units.title: 住家維修
units.scenario: Ian家裡冷氣壞掉

sentences.en: Hello this is air conditioning USA. How can I help you?
sentences.zh: 哈囉，這裡是美國冷氣公司。請問我可以如何協助你？

back.grammar: 電話開場常用 This is... 表示「這裡是／我是……」，比 I am 更自然。
variations: Thanks for calling Air Conditioning USA. How can I help?
Air Conditioning USA, how can I help you today?
Hi, you’ve reached Air Conditioning USA. What can I do for you?

keyPhrases.zh: 請問我可以怎麼協助你？

extraExamples.en: Hello, this is Dr. Lee’s office. How can I help you?
extraExamples.zh: 你好，這裡是李醫師診所。請問我可以怎麼協助你？

extraExamples.en: Thanks for calling Quick Plumbing. How can I help?
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝你致電快速水電。請問有什麼需要幫忙的？

sentences.en: Hi! I’m calling because my AC is not working. I was wondering when you guys can come out and check it?
sentences.zh: 嗨！我打電話來是因為我的冷氣壞了。我想詢問你們什麼時候可以過來檢查一下？

back.grammar: I’m calling because... 是打電話說明原因的自然開頭；I was wondering... 是禮貌詢問。
variations: Hi, I’m calling about my AC. It’s not working. When could someone come take a look?
Hey, my air conditioner stopped working. Do you know when you can send someone out?
Hi, my AC isn’t running. I wanted to see when you guys could come check it out.

extraExamples.en: I’m calling because my internet has been really slow all day.
extraExamples.zh: 我打電話是因為我的網路一整天都很慢。

extraExamples.en: I was wondering if you could come out and check the heater this week.
extraExamples.zh: 我想問你們這週能不能過來檢查暖氣。

sentences.en: We can get a technician out there tomorrow if it’s an emergency. Can you explain what’s been happening so I can let the technician know?
sentences.zh: 如果是緊急情況，我們可以安排技術人員明天過去。你可以說明一下情況嗎？所以我可以讓技術人員知道。

back.grammar: get someone out there 表示「安排某人到現場」；what’s been happening 是現在完成進行式，強調最近持續發生的狀況。
variations: We can send a technician tomorrow if it’s urgent. Can you tell me what’s going on?
If this is an emergency, we can have someone come out tomorrow. What’s been happening with the unit?
We could get someone there tomorrow. Can you describe the issue for me?

extraExamples.en: We can get a plumber out there this afternoon if there’s a leak.
extraExamples.zh: 如果有漏水，我們可以今天下午安排水電工過去。

extraExamples.en: Can you explain what’s been happening with your car?
extraExamples.zh: 你可以說明一下你的車最近發生什麼問題嗎？

sentences.en: It was working normally and then it just stopped. It hasn’t turned on since yesterday. Before that it was not making any noises.
sentences.zh: 它之前都可以正常運作，但突然就停止了。它從昨天開始就沒再啟動。之前也沒有發出什麼噪音。

back.grammar: It hasn’t turned on since yesterday 使用現在完成式，表示「從昨天到現在都沒有啟動」。
variations: It was fine, and then it suddenly stopped working. It hasn’t come on since yesterday.
It had been running normally, but then it just shut off. It hasn’t turned back on.
It was working okay, then it quit. I haven’t heard any weird noises before that.

extraExamples.en: My laptop hasn’t turned on since last night.
extraExamples.zh: 我的筆電從昨晚開始就開不了機。

extraExamples.en: The fridge was working normally, and then it just stopped.
extraExamples.zh: 冰箱本來正常運作，然後突然就停了。

sentences.en: Okay, so it was working normally… I’ll just let the technician know that, and we’ll send him in on Monday.
sentences.zh: 好的，所以之前都可以正常運作……我會告訴技術人員，我們安排他週一過去。

back.grammar: I’ll let... know 是「我會告知……」；send him in/on Monday 口語中表示安排他週一過去。
variations: Got it. I’ll pass that along to the technician, and we’ll have him come by Monday.
Okay, I’ll make a note of that and send the technician out on Monday.
Sounds good. I’ll tell the technician, and he’ll be there Monday.

extraExamples.en: I’ll let the manager know you called.
extraExamples.zh: 我會告訴經理你打過電話。

extraExamples.en: We’ll send someone out on Friday to fix the sink.
extraExamples.zh: 我們會週五派人過去修水槽。

sentences.en: Perfect. Thanks!
sentences.zh: 太好了，謝謝！

back.grammar: Perfect 在口語中常表示「太好了／沒問題」，不一定是真的「完美」。
variations: Great, thank you!
Sounds good. Thanks!
Awesome, I appreciate it!

extraExamples.en: Perfect, I’ll see you at three.
extraExamples.zh: 太好了，我三點見你。

extraExamples.en: Sounds good. Thanks for your help!
extraExamples.zh: 聽起來不錯，謝謝你的幫忙！

sentences.en: Hi, I’m here from air conditioning USA.
sentences.zh: 嗨，我是來自美國冷氣公司的。

back.grammar: I’m here from... 是到府服務人員常用自我介紹，表示「我是……公司派來的」。
variations: Hi, I’m with Air Conditioning USA.
Hey, I’m the technician from Air Conditioning USA.
Hi, I’m here to take a look at your AC.

extraExamples.en: Hi, I’m here from the internet company.
extraExamples.zh: 嗨，我是網路公司派來的。

extraExamples.en: I’m here to take a look at the washing machine.
extraExamples.zh: 我是來檢查洗衣機的。

sentences.en: Oh, yes! The unit is out here in the backyard.
sentences.zh: 哦，是的！設備在後院這邊。

back.grammar: out here 表示「在外面這邊」，常用來帶人到某個位置。
variations: Sure, it’s out back in the yard.
Yes, the AC unit is right over here in the backyard.
Of course. It’s outside behind the house.

extraExamples.en: The trash cans are out here by the garage.
extraExamples.zh: 垃圾桶在車庫旁邊外面這裡。

extraExamples.en: The water heater is in the backyard, right over there.
extraExamples.zh: 熱水器在後院，就在那邊。

sentences.en: OK let me take a look at it… It looks like the compressor blew out and you are also low on refrigerant here.
sentences.zh: 好的，讓我檢查一下……看起來是壓縮機壞掉了，而且你的冷媒也快不足了。

back.grammar: It looks like... 是委婉判斷「看起來像……」；low on... 表示「……不足」。
variations: Let me check it out… Looks like the compressor went bad, and the refrigerant is low.
I’ll take a look… It seems like the compressor failed, and you’re low on refrigerant.
After checking it, it looks like the compressor is shot and the refrigerant level is low.

extraExamples.en: Let me take a look at your phone and see what’s wrong.
extraExamples.zh: 讓我看一下你的手機，看看哪裡有問題。

extraExamples.en: We’re low on gas, so we should stop soon.
extraExamples.zh: 我們汽油不多了，所以應該快點停下來加油。

sentences.en: Oh no. What is the next step then?
sentences.zh: 不是吧，那接下來該怎麼辦？

back.grammar: What is the next step? 是詢問後續處理方式；then 放句尾表示「那麼／所以接下來」。
variations: Oh no. What do we do next?
Yikes. So what happens now?
Oh man. What’s the next move?

extraExamples.en: The application was rejected. What’s the next step?
extraExamples.zh: 申請被拒絕了。下一步該怎麼辦？

extraExamples.en: Oh no, the tire is flat. What do we do next?
extraExamples.zh: 糟了，輪胎沒氣了。接下來怎麼辦？

sentences.en: I listed all these problems out on an itemized estimate. The total before taxes is gonna be $5,325.25.
sentences.zh: 我把所有問題都列在一份分項估價單上了。稅前價格總共是5,325.25美元。

back.grammar: itemized estimate 是「分項估價單」；gonna 是 going to 的口語縮讀。
variations: I put everything on an itemized estimate. Before tax, it comes out to $5,325.25.
I wrote up a detailed estimate. The total before taxes will be $5,325.25.
Here’s the breakdown. Before tax, you’re looking at $5,325.25.

extraExamples.en: The mechanic gave me an itemized estimate for the repairs.
extraExamples.zh: 技師給了我一份維修的分項估價單。

extraExamples.en: Before taxes, the laptop is gonna be about nine hundred dollars.
extraExamples.zh: 稅前這台筆電大概要九百美元。

sentences.en: Holy moly… that’s expensive!
sentences.zh: 天啊……真是太貴了！

back.grammar: Holy moly 是美式口語驚嘆詞，表示驚訝、震驚，語氣比髒話溫和。
variations: Wow, that’s really expensive!
Oh my gosh, that’s a lot!
Yikes, that’s pricey!

extraExamples.en: Holy moly, this line is long!
extraExamples.zh: 天啊，這排隊也太長了吧！

extraExamples.en: Yikes, that restaurant is pretty pricey.
extraExamples.zh: 哇，那間餐廳滿貴的。

sentences.en: Yeah… honestly your unit is kind of old, so it’s gonna run into a lot of problems. I would recommend possibly looking at replacing your unit instead.
sentences.zh: 是啊……說實話你的設備有點舊了，所以未來可能會遇到很多問題。我建議你考慮更換設備。

back.grammar: kind of 表示「有點」；run into problems 是「遇到問題」；recommend + V-ing 表示「建議做某事」。
variations: Honestly, your unit is pretty old, so it may keep having issues. I’d think about replacing it.
To be honest, this system is getting old, so you’ll probably run into more problems. Replacing it might be better.
Yeah, it’s an older unit, so repairs may keep coming up. I’d recommend looking into a replacement.

extraExamples.en: My car is kind of old, so it keeps running into problems.
extraExamples.zh: 我的車有點舊，所以一直遇到問題。

extraExamples.en: I’d recommend looking at a new phone instead of fixing this one.
extraExamples.zh: 我會建議你考慮買新手機，而不是修這支。

sentences.en: Will that be cheaper than fixing it?
sentences.zh: 這樣比修理會便宜嗎？

back.grammar: Will that be...? 用未來式詢問某方案結果；cheaper than... 表示「比……便宜」。
variations: Would that cost less than fixing it?
Is replacing it cheaper than repairing it?
Would that be more affordable than getting it fixed?

extraExamples.en: Would taking the train be cheaper than driving?
extraExamples.zh: 搭火車會比開車便宜嗎？

extraExamples.en: Is buying a new printer cheaper than fixing this one?
extraExamples.zh: 買新印表機會比修這台便宜嗎？

sentences.en: In the long run, yes because you will run into less problems in the future so you won’t have to worry about fixing it for many years. I can show you some prices if you’re unsure.
sentences.zh: 從長遠來看，是的，因為未來問題會少很多，你也不需要擔心多年內的維修。我可以給你看一些價格，如果你不確定的話。

back.grammar: In the long run 表示「從長遠來看」；won’t have to 表示「不必」。
variations: Long term, yes. You’ll have fewer issues and won’t need to fix it as often.
Over time, yes, because a new unit should give you fewer problems.
In the long run, replacing it usually saves money. I can go over some options with you.

extraExamples.en: In the long run, cooking at home saves a lot of money.
extraExamples.zh: 從長遠來看，在家煮飯可以省很多錢。

extraExamples.en: If you buy a newer car, you won’t have to worry about repairs as much.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你買比較新的車，就不用那麼擔心維修。

sentences.en: Okay, let’s do that.
sentences.zh: 好的，那我們看看吧。

back.grammar: “let’s + 原形動詞”是口語中提議一起做某事的說法；“do that”指代前面提到的事情。
variations: Okay, let’s go with that.
Sure, let’s do it.
Alright, that works.

extraExamples.en: Okay, let’s do that and schedule it for Friday.
extraExamples.zh: 好的，那我們就這樣做，排在星期五吧。

extraExamples.en: If the price is fair, let’s go with that option.
extraExamples.zh: 如果價格合理，我們就選那個方案吧。

sentences.en: What I’ll do is I will send you this estimate for repairing your current unit along with a few options to get a new one. I also noticed that the thermostat seems off because it’s not reading the temperature correctly. So I think getting a new unit might be your best option.
sentences.zh: 這樣吧，我會把修理當前設備的估價單，以及幾個新設備的選項一起發給你。我也發現恆溫器好像有問題，因為它沒有正確顯示溫度。所以我認為換新設備可能是最佳選擇。

back.grammar: “What I’ll do is...” 是很常見的服務業口語開場，用來說明接下來自己會怎麼處理；“might be” 表示較委婉的建議。
variations: I’ll send over the repair estimate and a few replacement options. The thermostat also seems to be acting up, so replacing the unit might make the most sense.
Here’s what I’ll do: I’ll email you the repair quote plus some options for a new unit. Since the thermostat isn’t reading right, a new one may be your best bet.
I can send you an estimate to fix this unit, along with a couple options to replace it. The thermostat looks off, so getting a new unit might be the better way to go.

extraExamples.en: What I’ll do is send you the invoice along with the warranty information.
extraExamples.zh: 我會把發票連同保固資訊一起寄給你。

extraExamples.en: The Wi-Fi seems off because my phone keeps disconnecting.
extraExamples.zh: Wi-Fi 好像有問題，因為我的手機一直斷線。

sentences.en: Sounds good. Thanks for stopping by!
sentences.zh: 聽起來不錯。謝謝你過來一趟！

back.grammar: “Sounds good” 是省略主詞的口語說法，完整是 “That sounds good”；“Thanks for + V-ing” 用來感謝對方做了某事。
variations: That sounds good. Thanks for coming by!
Works for me. I appreciate you stopping by.
Sounds great. Thanks for taking a look.

extraExamples.en: Sounds good. Thanks for helping me move the couch.
extraExamples.zh: 聽起來不錯。謝謝你幫我搬沙發。

extraExamples.en: Thanks for stopping by after work to drop off the keys.
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝你下班後過來一趟把鑰匙送來。

sentences.en: Anytime!
sentences.zh: 不客氣！隨時為您服務！

back.grammar: “Anytime!” 是回應感謝的自然口語，意思像 “You’re welcome”，也帶有「隨時都可以幫忙」的感覺。
variations: No problem!
You’re welcome!
Happy to help!

extraExamples.en: Anytime! Just let me know if you need anything else.
extraExamples.zh: 不客氣！如果還需要什麼再跟我說。

extraExamples.en: Happy to help! I know moving can be stressful.
extraExamples.zh: 很樂意幫忙！我知道搬家會讓人很有壓力。

units.title: 旅行與住宿
units.scenario: Oliver第一次到洛杉磯機場＋Oliver旅館check in

sentences.en: What are you here for?
sentences.zh: 你來這裡的目的是什麼？

back.grammar: What are you here for? 是口語常用問法，用來詢問「目的」；介系詞 for 放句尾很自然。
variations: What brings you here?
What's the purpose of your visit?
Why are you visiting?

keyPhrases.zh: 你來這裡做什麼／目的為何？

extraExamples.en: I'm here for a job interview.
extraExamples.zh: 我來這裡是為了面試。

extraExamples.en: What are you here for, business or vacation?
extraExamples.zh: 你來這裡是出差還是度假？

sentences.en: I’m here for a vacation.
sentences.zh: 我來這裡度假。

back.grammar: I’m here for + 名詞 表示「我來這裡是為了……」，I’m 是 I am 的口語縮寫。
variations: I'm visiting for vacation.
I'm here on vacation.
I'm taking a vacation here.

extraExamples.en: I'm here for a conference.
extraExamples.zh: 我來這裡參加會議。

extraExamples.en: She's in Hawaii on vacation with her family.
extraExamples.zh: 她和家人在夏威夷度假。

sentences.en: Where are you staying?
sentences.zh: 你住在哪裡？

back.grammar: 現在進行式 are you staying 用來問「這段期間會住在哪裡」，不是只問此刻正在做什麼。
variations: Where are you staying while you're here?
What's your hotel?
Where will you be staying?

extraExamples.en: Where are you staying during the trip?
extraExamples.zh: 你旅行期間住在哪裡？

extraExamples.en: We're staying at an Airbnb near downtown.
extraExamples.zh: 我們住在市中心附近的 Airbnb。

sentences.en: I’m staying at a hotel for two nights then staying with my old coworker while I visit her.
sentences.zh: 我會先住兩晚酒店，然後住在我的老同事家，順便拜訪她。

back.grammar: I’m staying... then staying... 用現在進行式描述已安排好的行程；while 表示「在……期間／順便」。
variations: I'll stay at a hotel for two nights, then with my old coworker while I'm visiting her.
I'm spending two nights at a hotel, then staying at my former coworker's place.
I'll be at a hotel for the first two nights, and then I'll stay with my old coworker.

keyPhrases.zh: 我拜訪她期間／順便拜訪她

extraExamples.en: I'm staying with my sister while I look for an apartment.
extraExamples.zh: 我找房子的期間會住在我姊姊家。

extraExamples.en: We're staying at a hotel for one night, then driving to San Diego.
extraExamples.zh: 我們會先住一晚飯店，然後開車去聖地牙哥。

sentences.en: What’s the address?
sentences.zh: 地址是什麼？

back.grammar: What’s the address? 是 What is 的縮寫；問地址時直接用 the address 很自然。
variations: Can I get the address?
What's the street address?
Do you have the address?

extraExamples.en: What's the address of the restaurant?
extraExamples.zh: 那家餐廳的地址是什麼？

extraExamples.en: Can I get the address so I can call a taxi?
extraExamples.zh: 可以給我地址嗎？這樣我可以叫計程車。

sentences.en: The address of the hotel is 216 Los Angeles Blvd and my coworker’s address… Do you also need that?
sentences.zh: 酒店地址是洛杉磯大道216號，我同事的地址…你也需要嗎？

back.grammar: Do you also need that? 用助動詞 do 問「也需要那個嗎？」that 指前面提到的 coworker’s address。
variations: The hotel's address is 216 Los Angeles Blvd. Do you need my coworker's address too?
It's 216 Los Angeles Blvd for the hotel. Should I give you my coworker's address as well?
The hotel is at 216 Los Angeles Blvd. Do you want the other address too?

extraExamples.en: My office address is on the form. Do you also need my home address?
extraExamples.zh: 我的公司地址在表格上。你也需要我的住家地址嗎？

extraExamples.en: The Airbnb address is 50 Maple Street, and my friend's address is in my phone.
extraExamples.zh: Airbnb 的地址是楓樹街 50 號，我朋友的地址在我手機裡。

sentences.en: Yes, please. How long do you plan to stay here for?
sentences.zh: 是的，麻煩您說一下。您計劃在這裡待多久？

back.grammar: How long do you plan to stay here for? 問停留時間；for 可放句尾，口語很常見。
variations: How long are you planning to stay?
How long will you be here?
How many days are you staying?

extraExamples.en: How long do you plan to stay in New York?
extraExamples.zh: 你打算在紐約待多久？

extraExamples.en: We plan to stay for about a week.
extraExamples.zh: 我們打算待大約一週。

sentences.en: In total 32 days. The first 2 days at the hotel and the rest with my old coworker. Ah, here it is. Her address is 19567 Washington St.
sentences.zh: 總共32天，前兩天住酒店，之後都住在我老同事家。啊，在這裡，她的地址是華盛頓街19567號。

back.grammar: In total 表示「總共」；the rest 指「剩下的部分」；Here it is. 是找到東西時常說的「在這裡」。
variations: Thirty-two days total: two days at the hotel and the rest with my old coworker.
I'll be here for 32 days altogether. The first two nights are at the hotel, then I'll stay with my coworker.
It's 32 days in total. Oh, I found it—her address is 19567 Washington St.

extraExamples.en: In total, we spent $300 on food this weekend.
extraExamples.zh: 我們這個週末總共花了 300 美元在食物上。

extraExamples.en: I'll work Monday and Tuesday, and take the rest of the week off.
extraExamples.zh: 我週一和週二上班，這週剩下的時間請假。

sentences.en: How much money are you bringing in cash?
sentences.zh: 你帶了多少現金？

back.grammar: How much money...? 用來問不可數名詞 money 的數量；in cash 表示「以現金形式」。
variations: How much cash are you bringing?
How much money do you have in cash?
Are you carrying any cash? How much?

extraExamples.en: How much cash should I bring for the trip?
extraExamples.zh: 這趟旅行我該帶多少現金？

extraExamples.en: I don't carry much cash anymore; I usually use my card.
extraExamples.zh: 我現在不太帶現金，通常刷卡。

sentences.en: A little under $2,000.
sentences.zh: 不到2000美元。

back.grammar: A little under + 數字 表示「略少於／不到」，是很自然的估算說法。
variations: Just under $2,000.
A bit less than $2,000.
Almost $2,000, but not quite.

extraExamples.en: The flight took a little under three hours.
extraExamples.zh: 這趟班機花了不到三小時。

extraExamples.en: The total was just under $50.
extraExamples.zh: 總金額剛好不到 50 美元。

sentences.en: Ok, you’re good on that. No need to declare it. And what is your profession?
sentences.zh: 好的，這樣可以，不需要申報。您的職業是什麼？

back.grammar: You’re good on that 是美式口語，意思是「那方面沒問題」；No need to + 原形動詞 表示「不需要……」。
variations: That's fine. You don't need to declare it.
You're okay there. No declaration needed.
That's under the limit, so you don't have to declare it. What's your job?

keyPhrases.zh: 那方面沒問題／這樣可以
