extraExamples.en: You're good on documents, but I still need your passport.
extraExamples.zh: 你的文件部分沒問題，但我還需要你的護照。

extraExamples.en: No need to print the ticket; you can show it on your phone.
extraExamples.zh: 不需要印票，你可以用手機出示。

sentences.en: I am a marketing manager for a tech company.
sentences.zh: 我是科技公司的行銷主管。

back.grammar: I am a/an + 職業 表示「我是……」；for a company 表示「任職於某公司／為某公司工作」。
variations: I work as a marketing manager at a tech company.
I'm a marketing manager at a tech firm.
I work in marketing for a tech company.

extraExamples.en: My brother works for a software company.
extraExamples.zh: 我哥哥在一家軟體公司工作。

extraExamples.en: She's a project manager at a big tech company.
extraExamples.zh: 她是一家大型科技公司的專案經理。

sentences.en: Ok, you’re all set. Welcome to the United States.
sentences.zh: 好的，您已經準備好了。歡迎來到美國。

back.grammar: You’re all set 是美式口語，表示「都好了／手續完成了」；常用於服務情境。
variations: You're good to go. Welcome to the U.S.
Everything's all set. Welcome to the United States.
That's it. Welcome to America.

extraExamples.en: Your room key is ready, and you're all set.
extraExamples.zh: 你的房卡好了，手續都完成了。

extraExamples.en: I signed the form, so we're good to go.
extraExamples.zh: 我簽好表格了，所以我們可以走了。

sentences.en: Thank you.
sentences.zh: 謝謝。

back.grammar: Thank you 是最基本禮貌用語；口語中也常說 Thanks，語氣較輕鬆。
variations: Thanks.
Thank you so much.
I appreciate it.

extraExamples.en: Thank you for your help.
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝你的幫忙。

extraExamples.en: I appreciate it. That really helps.
extraExamples.zh: 我很感謝，這真的很有幫助。

sentences.en: Hi, checking in?
sentences.zh: 您好，您是來辦理入住的嗎？

back.grammar: Checking in? 是 Are you checking in? 的省略口語說法，飯店櫃台常用來確認是否辦理入住。
variations: Hi, are you checking in?
Hello, here to check in?
Hi there, do you have a reservation?

extraExamples.en: We're checking in under the name Chen.
extraExamples.zh: 我們要用 Chen 這個名字辦理入住。

extraExamples.en: Hi, I have a reservation for two nights.
extraExamples.zh: 你好，我有預訂兩晚。

sentences.en: Yes, under Chang.
sentences.zh: 是的，用「張」這個名字預訂的。

back.grammar: 省略句：完整說法是 “Yes, the reservation is under Chang.”，飯店/餐廳報訂位姓名常用 under。
variations: Yes, it’s under Chang.
The reservation should be under Chang.
It’s booked under Chang.

keyPhrases.zh: 用張這個姓氏／名字登記

extraExamples.en: The table is under Lin.
extraExamples.zh: 這桌是用林這個名字訂的。

extraExamples.en: My appointment should be under Chen.
extraExamples.zh: 我的預約應該是用陳這個名字登記的。

sentences.en: Can I see the credit card that you used for your reservation?
sentences.zh: 我可以看一下您用來預訂的信用卡嗎？

back.grammar: 關係子句 that you used 修飾 credit card，表示「你用來……的信用卡」。
variations: Could I see the card you used to book the room?
May I have the credit card used for the reservation?
Can I take a quick look at the card you booked with?

extraExamples.en: Can I see the ID that you used to check in?
extraExamples.zh: 我可以看一下您用來辦理入住的證件嗎？

extraExamples.en: This is the card I used for the booking.
extraExamples.zh: 這是我用來訂房的卡。

sentences.en: Here you go.
sentences.zh: 這邊給你。

back.grammar: Here you go. 是口語中遞東西給對方時常用的說法，意思是「給你／這邊給你」。
variations: Here you are.
Sure, here it is.
Of course, here you go.

extraExamples.en: Here you go—your coffee.
extraExamples.zh: 給你，你的咖啡。

extraExamples.en: Here you go. Let me know if you need anything else.
extraExamples.zh: 這邊給你。如果還需要什麼再跟我說。

sentences.en: Alrighty. Mr. Chang. I see that you’re a platinum member with us. Thank you for choosing us for your stay. Here is your card.
sentences.zh: 好的，張先生。我看到您是我們的白金會員。感謝您選擇入住我們酒店。這是您的信用卡。

back.grammar: I see that... 表示「我看到／系統顯示……」；with us 是服務業常用，指「在我們這裡／本公司」。
variations: Alright, Mr. Chang. I can see you’re one of our platinum members.
Okay, Mr. Chang. It looks like you’re a platinum member with us.
Great, Mr. Chang. You’re showing as a platinum member in our system.

extraExamples.en: I see that you booked a king room for tonight.
extraExamples.zh: 我看到您今晚訂的是一間特大床房。

extraExamples.en: Thank you for choosing us for your vacation.
extraExamples.zh: 感謝您選擇我們作為您的度假住宿。

sentences.en: Thank you.
sentences.zh: 謝謝。

back.grammar: Thank you. 是最基本也最自然的道謝；口語也常簡化成 Thanks.。
variations: Thanks.
Thank you so much.
I appreciate it.

extraExamples.en: Thank you for your help.
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝你的幫忙。

extraExamples.en: Thanks for checking that for me.
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝你幫我查。

sentences.en: So you’re staying with us for two nights. Are we here to celebrate anything?
sentences.zh: 您在這裡住兩晚，我們來這是來特別慶祝什麼嗎？

back.grammar: 現在進行式 are staying 表示已安排好的住宿期間；Are we here to...? 是服務業禮貌問法，把 you 說成 we 比較親切。
variations: You’ll be with us for two nights. Are you celebrating anything special?
Looks like you’re staying two nights. Is this for a special occasion?
So, two nights with us—are we celebrating something?

extraExamples.en: You’re staying with us for three nights, right?
extraExamples.zh: 您在我們這裡住三晚，對嗎？

extraExamples.en: Are you here to celebrate an anniversary?
extraExamples.zh: 您是來慶祝週年紀念日的嗎？

sentences.en: Yes. It’s my birthday solo trip.
sentences.zh: 是的，這是我的生日獨自旅行。

back.grammar: solo trip 是口語常用名詞片語，意思是「一個人的旅行」；birthday solo trip 就是「生日獨旅」。
variations: Yes, I’m taking a solo birthday trip.
Yeah, it’s a birthday trip just for me.
Yes, I’m here on a little birthday getaway by myself.

extraExamples.en: I’m planning a solo trip to New York.
extraExamples.zh: 我正在計畫一趟紐約獨旅。

extraExamples.en: This is my first birthday trip by myself.
extraExamples.zh: 這是我第一次自己一個人生日旅行。

sentences.en: Oh, happy birthday, Mr. Chang.
sentences.zh: 哦，生日快樂，張先生！

back.grammar: 祝賀生日用 Happy birthday；前面加 Oh 讓語氣更自然、親切。
variations: Oh, happy birthday!
Happy birthday, Mr. Chang!
That’s wonderful—happy birthday!

keyPhrases.zh: 先生，用於稱呼男性姓氏前

extraExamples.en: Oh, happy birthday! I hope you have a great day.
extraExamples.zh: 喔，生日快樂！希望你今天過得很棒。

extraExamples.en: Happy birthday, Mom!
extraExamples.zh: 媽媽，生日快樂！

sentences.en: Thank you! And I was wondering if you have any suite upgrades available today?
sentences.zh: 謝謝！我想問一下今天有套房升級的選項嗎？

back.grammar: I was wondering if... 是非常禮貌、委婉的詢問方式，比 Do you have...? 更客氣。
variations: I was wondering if there are any suite upgrades available today.
Do you happen to have any suite upgrades available?
Would there be any chance of a suite upgrade today?

extraExamples.en: I was wondering if you have any late checkouts available.
extraExamples.zh: 我想請問今天是否可以延後退房。

extraExamples.en: Do you happen to have any window seats available?
extraExamples.zh: 請問剛好還有靠窗座位嗎？

sentences.en: Let me check that for you. One moment, please.
sentences.zh: 我幫您查一下，請稍等。

back.grammar: Let me... 是口語中表示「我來幫你……」；One moment, please. 是服務業請對方稍等的禮貌說法。
variations: Let me take a look for you. One moment.
I’ll check on that for you. Just a moment, please.
Sure, let me see what we have available.

extraExamples.en: Let me check your reservation for you.
extraExamples.zh: 我幫您查一下您的預訂。

extraExamples.en: One moment, please. I’ll be right back.
extraExamples.zh: 請稍等一下。我馬上回來。

sentences.en: Okay.
sentences.zh: 好的。

back.grammar: Okay. 可表示同意、了解或回應對方「稍等」；語氣簡短自然。
variations: Sure.
Sounds good.
No problem.

extraExamples.en: Okay, I’ll wait here.
extraExamples.zh: 好的，我在這裡等。

extraExamples.en: Sounds good. Thank you.
extraExamples.zh: 可以，謝謝你。

sentences.en: So I upgraded you to an ocean view suite on the 33rd floor with a Jacuzzi. You will love it! You’ll also get free high-speed WiFi. Would you like a complimentary breakfast or extra points for your stay?
sentences.zh: 我已經幫您升級到33樓帶按摩浴缸的海景套房。您一定會喜歡的！此外，您還可以免費使用高速無線網絡。您想要免費早餐還是額外點數累積呢？

back.grammar: I upgraded you to... 是「我幫你升級到……」；You’ll also get... 用未來式說明接下來可享有的服務。
variations: I was able to upgrade you to an ocean-view suite on the 33rd floor.
We’ve upgraded you to a suite with an ocean view and a Jacuzzi.
You’re all set in an ocean-view suite on the 33rd floor.

extraExamples.en: We upgraded you to a room with a city view.
extraExamples.zh: 我們幫您升級到市景房。

extraExamples.en: You’ll also get free parking during your stay.
extraExamples.zh: 您入住期間也會有免費停車。

sentences.en: Just the points.
sentences.zh: 積分就好。

back.grammar: Just the points. 是省略句，完整意思是 “I’ll take just the points.”，用 just 表示「只要……就好」。
variations: I’ll take the points.
The points, please.
Just the extra points is fine.

extraExamples.en: Just water, please.
extraExamples.zh: 水就好，謝謝。

extraExamples.en: I’ll take the points instead of breakfast.
extraExamples.zh: 我選積分，不要早餐。

sentences.en: You got it. Now the elevator is all the way across the lobby to your left. Did you need one or two key cards?
sentences.zh: 好的。電梯在穿過大廳後的左邊。您需要一張還是兩張房卡？

back.grammar: You got it. 是美式口語，表示「沒問題／好的」；all the way across 表示「一路穿過到對面」。
variations: Absolutely. The elevator is across the lobby on your left.
No problem. The elevators are straight across the lobby to the left.
You got it. You’ll find the elevator across the lobby, on the left.

keyPhrases.phrase: all the way across the lobby

extraExamples.en: You got it. I’ll add the points to your account.
extraExamples.zh: 沒問題。我會把點數加到您的帳戶裡。

extraExamples.en: The restroom is all the way across the lobby to your right.
extraExamples.zh: 洗手間在穿過大廳後的右邊。

sentences.en: Hmm… two, just to be safe.
sentences.zh: 嗯…兩張吧，以防萬一。

back.grammar: just to be safe 是口語片語，表示「以防萬一／保險起見」。
variations: Two, just in case.
I’ll take two to be safe.
Let’s do two, just to be safe.

extraExamples.en: Bring an umbrella, just to be safe.
extraExamples.zh: 帶把傘吧，以防萬一。

extraExamples.en: I’ll make a copy of the file, just in case.
extraExamples.zh: 我會備份一份檔案，以防萬一。

sentences.en: You got it.
sentences.zh: 好的。

back.grammar: 「You got it.」是很常見的口語回應，表示「沒問題／交給我／好的」，比單純說 OK 更有服務感。
variations: Sure thing.
No problem.
Absolutely.

extraExamples.en: Can you bring me another towel? — You got it.
extraExamples.zh: 可以再拿一條毛巾給我嗎？— 沒問題。

extraExamples.en: Please send me the address later. — You got it.
extraExamples.zh: 請晚點把地址傳給我。— 好的。

sentences.en: Oh, is there a gym here? And what time does it open and close?
sentences.zh: 哦，這裡有健身房嗎？它的開放和關閉時間是什麼？

back.grammar: 用「Is there...?」詢問某地是否有某設施；「What time does it open and close?」用一般現在式問固定營業時間。
variations: Do you have a gym here? What are the hours?
Is there a fitness center? When does it open and close?
Do you guys have a gym? What time is it open till?

keyPhrases.phrase: What time does it open and close?

keyPhrases.zh: 開放和關閉；營業和打烊

extraExamples.en: Is there a pool here? What time does it close?
extraExamples.zh: 這裡有游泳池嗎？它幾點關？

extraExamples.en: What time does the coffee shop open in the morning?
extraExamples.zh: 咖啡店早上幾點開？

sentences.en: It’s on the third floor and it opens daily from 6 in the morning til midnight.
sentences.zh: 健身房在三樓，每天早上6點到午夜12點開放。

back.grammar: 「It’s on the third floor」說明樓層；「from A till B」表示從某時間到某時間，til/till 是口語常見用法。
variations: It’s on the third floor, and it’s open every day from 6 a.m. to midnight.
You’ll find it on the third floor. It’s open daily, 6 a.m. to midnight.
It’s up on the third floor, and the hours are 6 in the morning until midnight.

keyPhrases.phrase: from 6 in the morning till midnight

extraExamples.en: The pool is on the second floor and opens daily from 8 a.m. to 10 p.m.
extraExamples.zh: 游泳池在二樓，每天早上八點到晚上十點開放。

extraExamples.en: The restaurant is on the first floor and serves breakfast till 11.
extraExamples.zh: 餐廳在一樓，早餐供應到十一點。

sentences.en: Awesome. I’m gonna go check it out later.
sentences.zh: 太好了，我等會兒去看看。

back.grammar: 「gonna」是「going to」的口語縮讀；「check it out」是片語動詞，意思是「去看看／了解一下」。
variations: Great. I’ll go take a look later.
Nice. I’m gonna stop by later.
Cool. I’ll check it out in a bit.

keyPhrases.zh: 我要；我打算（going to 的口語縮讀）

extraExamples.en: That new bakery looks good. I’m gonna check it out this weekend.
extraExamples.zh: 那家新麵包店看起來不錯。我這週末要去看看。

extraExamples.en: Awesome, I’ll check out the rooftop bar later tonight.
extraExamples.zh: 太好了，我今晚晚點去看看頂樓酒吧。

sentences.en: I do want to let you know that there is a $20 per day resort fee that you can use as credits at our gift shop and the restaurants in the lobby.
sentences.zh: 另外提醒您，每天有20美元的度假村費用，您可以用它作為禮品店和大廳餐廳的消費抵扣額。

back.grammar: 「I do want to let you know」中的 do 用來加強語氣，表示「我確實想提醒您」；「that you can use as...」補充說明費用用途。
variations: Just so you know, there’s a $20 daily resort fee, and you can use it as credit at the gift shop and lobby restaurants.
I should mention there’s a $20-a-day resort fee, which can be applied as credit at our gift shop and lobby restaurants.
One thing to note: there’s a $20 per day resort fee, but you can use it toward purchases at the gift shop and restaurants in the lobby.

keyPhrases.phrase: I do want to let you know
keyPhrases.zh: 我確實想提醒您；我想先告知您
每天二十美元的度假村費用

extraExamples.en: I do want to let you know that breakfast ends at 10:30.
extraExamples.zh: 我想提醒您，早餐到十點半結束。

extraExamples.en: You can use this voucher as credit at the hotel restaurant.
extraExamples.zh: 您可以把這張券當作飯店餐廳的消費抵扣額使用。

sentences.en: Ok, I guess that’s fine.
sentences.zh: 好的，我覺得這沒問題。

back.grammar: 「I guess that’s fine」表示勉強接受或不是非常滿意但可以接受，語氣比「That’s fine」更保留。
variations: Okay, that works.
Alright, I guess that’s okay.
Okay, that’s not a problem.

keyPhrases.zh: 我想；我覺得（帶點保留語氣）

extraExamples.en: The room won’t be ready until 4? Okay, I guess that’s fine.
extraExamples.zh: 房間要到四點才好嗎？好吧，我想可以。

extraExamples.en: If there’s only one table left outside, I guess that’s fine.
extraExamples.zh: 如果只剩外面的桌子，我覺得也可以。

sentences.en: Anything else I can help you with?
sentences.zh: 還有什麼可以幫助您的嗎？

back.grammar: 「Anything else I can help you with?」是服務業常用句，完整說法是「Is there anything else I can help you with?」，口語中常省略開頭。
variations: Is there anything else I can help you with?
Can I help you with anything else?
Anything else you need?

extraExamples.en: Anything else I can help you with before you go?
extraExamples.zh: 您離開前還有什麼我可以幫忙的嗎？

extraExamples.en: Can I help you with anything else today?
extraExamples.zh: 今天還有其他需要我協助的地方嗎？

sentences.en: Nope, that’s it.
sentences.zh: 沒有了，就這些。

back.grammar: 「Nope」是「No」的更口語、輕鬆說法；「that’s it」表示「就這樣／就這些」。
variations: No, that’s all.
Nope, I’m good.
That’s everything, thanks.

extraExamples.en: Do you need a bag? — Nope, that’s it, thanks.
extraExamples.zh: 你需要袋子嗎？— 不用，就這樣，謝謝。

extraExamples.en: Anything else? — Nope, I’m good.
extraExamples.zh: 還需要其他的嗎？— 不用了，我可以了。

sentences.en: Enjoy your vacation!
sentences.zh: 祝您旅途愉快！

back.grammar: 「Enjoy your vacation!」是祝福用語，祈使句形式，用 enjoy 開頭表示「祝你玩得開心／好好享受」。
variations: Have a great vacation!
Enjoy your stay!
Hope you have an amazing trip!

extraExamples.en: Enjoy your dinner! Let me know if you need anything.
extraExamples.zh: 祝您用餐愉快！如果需要什麼請告訴我。

extraExamples.en: Have a great vacation, and safe travels!
extraExamples.zh: 祝你假期愉快，旅途平安！

units.title: 健康與醫療
units.scenario: Ian發燒拉肚子掛病

sentences.en: Hi, what brings you here today?
sentences.zh: 您好，今天哪裡不舒服？

back.grammar: What brings you here today? 是診所/客服常用開場，字面是「什麼把你帶來」，實際問「今天來的原因是什麼？」
variations: What can we help you with today?
What seems to be the problem?
What are you being seen for today?

keyPhrases.zh: 你來這裡的原因是什麼

extraExamples.en: What brings you to our office today?
extraExamples.zh: 你今天來我們辦公室是為了什麼呢？

extraExamples.en: Hi, what can I help you with today?
extraExamples.zh: 嗨，今天有什麼我可以幫你的嗎？

sentences.en: I think I have a fever. It started last night.
sentences.zh: 我覺得我發燒了，昨晚開始的。

back.grammar: I think I have... 用來委婉描述症狀；It started last night 用一般過去式說明症狀開始時間。
variations: I feel like I have a fever. It started last night.
I think I'm running a fever. It began last night.
I’ve had a fever since last night.

extraExamples.en: I think I have a cold. It started this morning.
extraExamples.zh: 我覺得我感冒了，是今天早上開始的。

extraExamples.en: She’s had a headache since last night.
extraExamples.zh: 她從昨晚開始就頭痛。

sentences.en: Ok, I will need your picture id.
sentences.zh: 好的，我需要您的身份證。

back.grammar: will need 是禮貌但直接的說法，櫃檯常用來告知需要哪些文件；picture ID 指有照片的身分證件。
variations: I’ll need to see your photo ID.
Can I get your picture ID, please?
I’m going to need your ID with a photo.

extraExamples.en: I’ll need your photo ID before you check in.
extraExamples.zh: 辦理報到前我需要你的照片證件。

extraExamples.en: Can I see your picture ID, please?
extraExamples.zh: 可以請我看一下你的有照證件嗎？

sentences.en: Here you go.
sentences.zh: 給您。

back.grammar: Here you go. 是遞東西給別人時的自然口語，相當於「給你」。
variations: Here you are.
There you go.
Sure, here it is.

extraExamples.en: Here you go—your coffee.
extraExamples.zh: 給你，你的咖啡。

extraExamples.en: Here it is. I found the receipt.
extraExamples.zh: 在這裡，我找到收據了。

sentences.en: Let’s see, date of birth and name, got it. I also need the card… the insurance card too, please.
sentences.zh: 讓我看看，出生日期和名字，好的。我還需要您的卡…保險卡也需要，謝謝。

back.grammar: Let’s see 是邊確認資料邊說的口語；got it 表示「確認好了/了解」。
variations: Let me check—date of birth and name, okay. I’ll also need your insurance card.
Okay, I have your name and date of birth. Can I see your insurance card too?
Let’s see... name, DOB, got it. I need your insurance card as well.

extraExamples.en: Let’s see, your appointment is at 3:30. Got it.
extraExamples.zh: 我看一下，你的預約是3點半。好了。

extraExamples.en: I’ll need your insurance card too, please.
extraExamples.zh: 也請給我你的保險卡。

sentences.en: I don’t have it with me but I have a picture of it.
sentences.zh: 我沒有帶在身上，但我有它的照片。

back.grammar: with me 表示「帶在身上」；a picture of it 指「它的照片」。
variations: I don’t have the actual card with me, but I have a photo of it.
I didn’t bring it, but I can show you a picture.
I don’t have it on me, but I have a picture on my phone.

extraExamples.en: I don’t have my passport with me, but I have a picture of it.
extraExamples.zh: 我沒把護照帶在身上，但我有它的照片。

extraExamples.en: Do you have your membership card on you?
extraExamples.zh: 你有帶會員卡在身上嗎？

sentences.en: Okay, email that to me. Also, fill out this top portion of the form, sign, and put down the date, alright?
sentences.zh: 好的，請把照片發到我的電子郵件。另外，請填寫表格的上半部分，簽名並填上日期，好的嗎？

back.grammar: email that to me 是「把那個用 email 寄給我」；fill out、sign、put down 都是表格相關常用動詞片語。
variations: Go ahead and email that to me. Then fill out the top part, sign it, and date it.
Please send that to my email, and fill out the top section of this form.
Email me that picture, then complete the top portion, sign, and write today’s date.

keyPhrases.zh: 把那個用電子郵件寄給我

extraExamples.en: Can you email the receipt to me?
extraExamples.zh: 你可以把收據寄到我的電子信箱嗎？

extraExamples.en: Please fill out this form and put down today’s date.
extraExamples.zh: 請填寫這張表格並寫上今天的日期。

sentences.en: Okay.
sentences.zh: 好的。

back.grammar: Okay 是最常見的回應，表示「好的、沒問題、了解」。
variations: Sure.
No problem.
Sounds good.

extraExamples.en: Okay, I’ll wait over there.
extraExamples.zh: 好的，我會在那邊等。

extraExamples.en: Sure, I can do that.
extraExamples.zh: 可以，我可以那樣做。

sentences.en: The pen is over there. After filling it out, take a seat there and the doctor will call you.
sentences.zh: 筆在那邊。填好後，請坐在那邊，醫生會叫您的。

back.grammar: After + V-ing 表示「做完某事之後」；take a seat 是比 sit down 更禮貌自然的說法。
variations: The pen’s over there. Once you’re done, have a seat and the doctor will call you.
You can grab a pen over there. After you finish, please take a seat.
There’s a pen over there. When you’re finished, sit over there and the doctor will call your name.

extraExamples.en: After signing in, take a seat in the waiting area.
extraExamples.zh: 簽到後，請在等候區坐一下。

extraExamples.en: The nurse will call your name when it’s your turn.
extraExamples.zh: 輪到你時，護理師會叫你的名字。

sentences.en: Got it. Thanks.
sentences.zh: 明白了，謝謝。

back.grammar: Got it. 是口語中表示「了解、知道了」的簡短說法。
variations: Okay, thanks.
Sounds good, thank you.
Gotcha, thanks.

extraExamples.en: Got it. I’ll be there at 2.
extraExamples.zh: 了解，我兩點會到。

extraExamples.en: Gotcha, I’ll bring my ID next time.
extraExamples.zh: 了解，下次我會帶身分證。

sentences.en: Ian, right? So what brings you here today? It says on here that you’ve been having a fever?
sentences.zh: 是 Ian 對吧？那麼今天是什麼情況呢？這裡寫著您發燒了？

back.grammar: right? 放句尾用來確認資訊；It says on here that... 是看著表格/系統說「這上面寫著……」。
variations: You’re Ian, right? So what brings you in today? It looks like you’ve had a fever?
Ian, correct? What are you here for today? I see you’ve been having a fever.
Ian, right? Tell me what’s going on today. It says here you’ve had a fever.

extraExamples.en: You’re here for a checkup, right?
extraExamples.zh: 你是來做健康檢查的，對吧？

extraExamples.en: It says here that your appointment is at 10.
extraExamples.zh: 這上面寫著你的預約是10點。

sentences.en: Yes, I’ve been having a fever since yesterday, diarrhea too.
sentences.zh: 是的，從昨天開始發燒，還有拉肚子。

back.grammar: I’ve been having... 是現在完成進行式，強調症狀從過去開始到現在仍持續；since yesterday 表示「從昨天起」。
variations: Yes, I’ve had a fever since yesterday, and diarrhea too.
Yeah, I started having a fever yesterday, and I’ve also had diarrhea.
Yes, I’ve been running a fever since yesterday, plus diarrhea.

extraExamples.en: I’ve been having stomach pain since this morning.
extraExamples.zh: 我從今天早上開始一直胃痛。

extraExamples.en: He’s had diarrhea since last night.
extraExamples.zh: 他從昨晚開始拉肚子。

sentences.en: When did you start having diarrhea?
sentences.zh: 您什麼時候開始拉肚子的？

back.grammar: When did you start + V-ing? 用來詢問某件事或症狀是什麼時候開始的。
variations: When did the diarrhea start?
When did you first notice the diarrhea?
How long have you been having diarrhea?

extraExamples.en: When did you start feeling dizzy?
extraExamples.zh: 你什麼時候開始覺得頭暈？

extraExamples.en: How long have you been having this cough?
extraExamples.zh: 你咳嗽多久了？

sentences.en: Yesterday… around 11 at night. At that point, I hadn’t eaten anything for like 30 hours because I was working.
sentences.zh: 昨天…大約晚上11點的時候。當時我已經大概30個小時沒吃東西了，因為我在工作。

back.grammar: hadn’t eaten 是過去完成式，表示在昨晚11點那個時間點之前「已經沒有吃東西」；for like 30 hours 中 like 是口語的「大概」。
variations: Yesterday, around 11 p.m. By then, I hadn’t eaten for about 30 hours because I was working.
It started around 11 last night. I hadn’t eaten anything for like 30 hours since I was working.
Around 11 last night. At that point, I’d gone about 30 hours without eating because of work.

extraExamples.en: At that point, I hadn’t slept for almost 24 hours.
extraExamples.zh: 那時候我已經快24小時沒睡了。

extraExamples.en: I’d gone all day without eating because I was so busy.
extraExamples.zh: 我因為太忙，一整天都沒吃東西。

sentences.en: Oh you were working all day?
sentences.zh: 哦，您工作了一整天？

back.grammar: were working all day 是過去進行式，強調過去某段時間一直在工作；all day 表示「一整天」。
variations: Oh, you were at work all day?
So you worked the whole day?
Oh wow, you were working the entire day?

extraExamples.en: I was studying all day yesterday.
extraExamples.zh: 我昨天讀了一整天書。

extraExamples.en: She was at work all day and didn’t have time to eat.
extraExamples.zh: 她一整天都在上班，沒時間吃飯。

sentences.en: Yeah, after I ate, I started getting chills. I was feeling nauseous and having diarrhea.
sentences.zh: 是的，吃完東西後，我開始感覺發冷，還感到噁心並且拉肚子。

back.grammar: 「started getting + 症狀」表示「開始變得／出現某症狀」；「was feeling / having」用過去進行式描述當時持續的不舒服狀態。
variations: Yeah, after I ate, I started feeling cold and sick to my stomach, and I had diarrhea.
Yeah, once I finished eating, I got the chills and felt nauseous, and I had diarrhea.
Yeah, pretty soon after eating, I started having chills, nausea, and diarrhea.

extraExamples.en: After the long flight, I started getting chills and felt really tired.
extraExamples.zh: 長途飛行後，我開始發冷，而且覺得很累。

extraExamples.en: She was feeling nauseous after the car ride, so we pulled over.
extraExamples.zh: 她坐車後覺得噁心，所以我們把車停到路邊。

sentences.en: Any exposure to someone with a flu?
sentences.zh: 有接觸過得流感的人嗎？

back.grammar: 「exposure to + 人／疾病」是醫療情境常用法，表示「接觸到、暴露於」感染來源。
variations: Have you been around anyone with the flu?
Were you exposed to anyone who had the flu?
Have you had contact with someone who’s been sick with the flu?

extraExamples.en: Have you had any exposure to someone with COVID recently?
extraExamples.zh: 你最近有接觸過得 COVID 的人嗎？

extraExamples.en: The school asked parents to report any exposure to chickenpox.
extraExamples.zh: 學校要求家長通報是否有接觸過水痘。

sentences.en: I don’t think so.
sentences.zh: 我想應該沒有。

back.grammar: 「I don’t think so」是自然口語，表示「我想不是／應該沒有」，語氣比直接說 no 更保留。
variations: I don’t believe so.
I don’t think I have.
Not that I can remember.

extraExamples.en: Do we need to bring cash? I don’t think so.
extraExamples.zh: 我們需要帶現金嗎？我想應該不用。

extraExamples.en: Have you met her before? Not that I can remember.
extraExamples.zh: 你以前見過她嗎？我記得沒有。

sentences.en: The good news is you’re negative for covid, and also negative for the flu.
sentences.zh: 好消息是，您的 COVID 和流感檢測結果都是陰性。

back.grammar: 「negative for + 疾病」表示檢測結果為陰性；相反是「positive for」。
variations: The good news is you tested negative for COVID and the flu.
Good news—you don’t have COVID or the flu.
Your COVID test and flu test both came back negative.

extraExamples.en: The good news is your X-ray looks normal.
extraExamples.zh: 好消息是你的 X 光看起來正常。

extraExamples.en: My test came back negative for the flu.
extraExamples.zh: 我的流感檢測結果是陰性。

sentences.en: That’s good to know.
sentences.zh: 那就好。

back.grammar: 「That’s good to know」是口語回應，表示「知道這點讓人放心／那就好」。
variations: That’s a relief.
Good to hear.
I’m glad to hear that.

keyPhrases.zh: 知道這點很好／讓人安心

extraExamples.en: The clinic is open on Sundays? That’s good to know.
extraExamples.zh: 診所星期天有開？知道這點很好。

extraExamples.en: Your test results are normal. That’s a relief.
extraExamples.zh: 你的檢查結果正常。那真是鬆了一口氣。

sentences.en: And you don’t think it’s associated with anything that you ate? You work from home, right? So did you go out to a restaurant? Or did you travel?
sentences.zh: 您不覺得這和您吃的東西有關嗎？您是在家工作對吧？那麼您有去餐廳吃飯或旅行嗎？

back.grammar: 「be associated with」表示「和……有關」；醫生用一連串問句來釐清病因，例如飲食、外食、旅行。
variations: Do you think it might be related to something you ate? You work from home, right? Did you eat out or travel anywhere?
Could it have been something you ate? Since you work from home, did you go to a restaurant or take a trip?
Do you think food had anything to do with it? You work remotely, right? Any restaurants or travel recently?

extraExamples.en: Do you think your stomach pain is associated with the seafood you had?
extraExamples.zh: 你覺得你的胃痛跟你吃的海鮮有關嗎？

extraExamples.en: I work from home, so I don’t commute anymore.
extraExamples.zh: 我在家工作，所以不再通勤了。

sentences.en: No, I just had some leftover pasta from a restaurant.
sentences.zh: 沒有，我只是吃了些餐廳的剩義大利麵。

back.grammar: 「leftover + 食物」表示「剩下的食物」；「had」在口語中常用來表示「吃了」。
variations: No, I just ate some leftover pasta from a restaurant.
No, just some restaurant pasta I had left over.
No, I only had some pasta leftovers from a restaurant.

extraExamples.en: I had leftover pizza for breakfast this morning.
extraExamples.zh: 我今天早餐吃了剩披薩。

extraExamples.en: We brought home some leftover pasta from dinner.
extraExamples.zh: 我們把晚餐剩下的義大利麵帶回家。

sentences.en: And you started feeling the symptoms right away?
sentences.zh: 然後您馬上開始出現症狀了？

back.grammar: 「start + V-ing」表示開始做某事；「right away」表示「立刻、馬上」。
variations: And the symptoms started right away?
So you felt sick almost immediately?
And you started noticing symptoms pretty much right after that?

extraExamples.en: I started feeling dizzy right away after taking the medicine.
extraExamples.zh: 我吃藥後馬上開始覺得頭暈。

extraExamples.en: She noticed the symptoms right away and called her doctor.
extraExamples.zh: 她立刻注意到症狀，並打電話給醫生。

sentences.en: Yeah, basically.
sentences.zh: 是的，基本上是這樣。

back.grammar: 「Yeah, basically」是口語中用來表示「對，差不多是這樣」的簡短回應。
variations: Yeah, pretty much.
Yeah, more or less.
Yeah, that’s about right.

extraExamples.en: So you work from home full-time? Yeah, basically.
extraExamples.zh: 所以你是全職在家工作嗎？對，基本上是。

extraExamples.en: Is this the same form I filled out last time? Yeah, pretty much.
extraExamples.zh: 這跟我上次填的表格一樣嗎？對，差不多。

sentences.en: Are you allergic to any medications?
sentences.zh: 您對任何藥物過敏嗎？

back.grammar: 「be allergic to + 名詞」表示「對……過敏」；醫療問診常問 medications。
variations: Do you have any medication allergies?
Are you allergic to any medicine?
Any allergies to medications?

extraExamples.en: I’m allergic to penicillin.
extraExamples.zh: 我對盤尼西林過敏。

extraExamples.en: Are you allergic to any foods or medications?
extraExamples.zh: 你對任何食物或藥物過敏嗎？

sentences.en: No, not that I know of.
sentences.zh: 據我所知沒有。

back.grammar: 「not that I know of」是常見口語，表示「據我所知沒有」，保留不確定性。
variations: No, not that I’m aware of.
No, as far as I know.
I don’t think so, no.

extraExamples.en: Is anyone else coming? Not that I know of.
extraExamples.zh: 還有其他人要來嗎？據我所知沒有。

extraExamples.en: Do you have any food allergies? No, as far as I know.
extraExamples.zh: 你有任何食物過敏嗎？就我所知沒有。

sentences.en: They took your blood pressure and temperature and everything seems fine. Your heart rate is a bit elevated but not so much that we need to worry about for now. So here’s what I’m going to do. I’m gonna have you call the phone number on this list and give them a stool sample. Once we get the result, we’ll call you to let you know if you need any antibiotics.
sentences.zh: 他們已經測了您的血壓和體溫，一切看起來正常。您的心率有些偏高，但目前還沒有什麼特別需要擔心的。所以我要這樣安排：我會讓您打這張單子上的電話，送糞便樣本過去。一旦我們拿到結果，會打電話通知您是否需要使用抗生素。

back.grammar: 「I’m gonna have you + 原形動詞」是醫療／服務情境常見說法，表示「我會請你／安排你去做……」；gonna 是 going to 的口語縮讀。
variations: Everything looks okay. Your heart rate is a little high, but not enough to be concerning right now. I’ll have you call the number on this list and provide a stool sample. Once we have the results, we’ll let you know if you need antibiotics.

Your blood pressure and temperature look fine. Your heart rate is slightly elevated, but we’re not too worried for now. I’m going to ask you to call this number and submit a stool sample. We’ll call when the results come in.

Your vitals look good overall. Your heart rate is a bit high, but nothing alarming at the moment. Please call the number on this sheet and give a stool sample. We’ll follow up about antibiotics once we get the results.

extraExamples.en: I’m gonna have you wait here while we get the test results.
extraExamples.zh: 我會請你在這裡等一下，等我們拿到檢查結果。

extraExamples.en: Your temperature is a bit elevated, so try to drink plenty of fluids.
extraExamples.zh: 你的體溫有點偏高，所以要多喝水。

sentences.en: Sounds good.
sentences.zh: 好的，明白了。

back.grammar: 「Sounds good」是非常常見的口語回應，表示「好的、聽起來可以、沒問題」。
variations: Okay, sounds good.
Got it.
That works.

extraExamples.en: Let’s meet at 7. Sounds good.
extraExamples.zh: 我們七點見。好啊。

extraExamples.en: You can email me the form tomorrow. That works.
extraExamples.zh: 你可以明天把表格寄給我。可以。

sentences.en: Chills and headaches are primarily caused by dehydration. You probably got dehydrated from not drinking and eating.
sentences.zh: 發冷和頭痛主要是因為脫水造成的。您可能因為沒有喝水和吃東西而脫水了。

back.grammar: 「be caused by」表示「由……造成」；「got dehydrated from + V-ing」表示「因為做某事而脫水」。
variations: The chills and headaches are mostly from dehydration. You probably got dehydrated because you haven’t been eating or drinking much.
Your chills and headache are likely caused by dehydration. Not drinking or eating enough probably dried you out.
You’re probably having chills and headaches because you’re dehydrated from not eating or drinking.

extraExamples.en: Her headache was caused by dehydration after the hike.
extraExamples.zh: 她健行後頭痛是脫水造成的。

extraExamples.en: I got dehydrated from being outside in the sun all day.
extraExamples.zh: 我因為整天待在太陽下而脫水了。

sentences.en: Ah, I see.
sentences.zh: 啊，我明白了。

back.grammar: 「I see」在口語中表示「我懂了／原來如此」，常用來回應對方解釋。
variations: Oh, got it.
Okay, that makes sense.
Oh, I understand.

extraExamples.en: Ah, I see. So I need to take it with food.
extraExamples.zh: 啊，我懂了。所以我需要跟食物一起吃。

extraExamples.en: Okay, that makes sense. I’ll drink more water today.
extraExamples.zh: 好，這樣有道理。我今天會多喝水。

sentences.en: In the meanwhile, try to stay away from spicy food, greasy food, and dairy. If you want to get something over the counter, electrolyte drinks will help you a lot.
sentences.zh: 同時，盡量避免辛辣食物、油膩食物和乳製品。如果需要藥房的非處方藥，電解質飲料對您會有很大幫助。

back.grammar: 「stay away from」是口語中常用的片語，表示「避免、不要碰」；「over the counter」指不用處方就能買到的藥或產品。
variations: For now, avoid spicy, greasy, and dairy foods. Electrolyte drinks can really help if you want something from the pharmacy.
In the meantime, try not to eat anything spicy, oily, or dairy-based. You can grab some electrolyte drinks over the counter—they should help.
Meanwhile, stay off spicy food, greasy food, and dairy. If you’re looking for something you can buy without a prescription, electrolyte drinks are a good idea.

keyPhrases.zh: 非處方的；不用醫生處方就能買到的

extraExamples.en: In the meanwhile, try to get some rest and stay away from alcohol.
extraExamples.zh: 在這段期間，盡量休息並避免喝酒。

extraExamples.en: You can get cough drops over the counter at most drugstores.
extraExamples.zh: 你可以在大多數藥局買到非處方的喉糖。

sentences.en: Okay, I will.
sentences.zh: 好的，我會注意的。

back.grammar: 「I will」在這裡省略了前面提到的動作，意思是「我會的／我會照做」，是很自然的簡短回應。
variations: Got it, I will.
Okay, I’ll do that.
Sounds good, I’ll be careful.

extraExamples.en: A: Don’t forget to drink water. B: I will.
extraExamples.zh: A：別忘了喝水。B：我會的。

extraExamples.en: A: Please call me when you get home. B: Okay, I will.
extraExamples.zh: A：你到家後請打給我。B：好的，我會。

sentences.en: And you know we’re open tomorrow too. So if you still feel like you’re not getting hydrated well enough, you can always come in tomorrow. You might need IV fluids if you’re still having chills. We’re open at 10 o’clock till 7 pm. So take care… and feel better!
sentences.zh: 我們明天也有開診。如果你覺得補水還是不夠，你隨時可以明天再過來。如果仍然有發冷的症狀，你可能需要打點滴。我們的營業時間是上午10點到下午7點。所以保重…希望您早日康復！

back.grammar: 「feel like + 子句」表示「覺得好像／感覺自己…」；「come in」在診所情境中常指「來看診、過來」。
variations: We’re open tomorrow as well, so if you still don’t feel hydrated enough, you can come back in.
If you’re still feeling dehydrated tomorrow, feel free to come in. You may need IV fluids if the chills continue.
We’ll be here tomorrow too, from 10 to 7. If you’re still not keeping fluids down or you still have chills, come on in.

extraExamples.en: If you feel like your fever is getting worse, you should come in tomorrow.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你覺得發燒變嚴重，明天應該過來看診。

extraExamples.en: She was so dehydrated that the doctor gave her IV fluids.
extraExamples.zh: 她脫水很嚴重，所以醫生幫她打點滴。

sentences.en: Thank you so much!
sentences.zh: 非常感謝！

back.grammar: 「Thank you so much」比「Thank you」更強烈、更真誠，口語中很常用來表達感謝。
variations: Thanks so much!
I really appreciate it!
Thank you, I appreciate your help.

extraExamples.en: Thank you so much for helping me today.
extraExamples.zh: 非常感謝你今天幫我。

extraExamples.en: I really appreciate it. You made this a lot easier.
extraExamples.zh: 我真的很感謝。你讓這件事簡單多了。

units.title: 客訴與抱怨
units.scenario: Oliver租車結果輪胎壞掉

sentences.en: Hello. This is Elephant Car Rental customer support. How can I help you?
sentences.zh: 喂，這裡是大象租車客服。請問有什麼可以幫助您的？

back.grammar: 客服電話常用開場：This is... 表示「這裡是／我是……」，How can I help you? 是禮貌詢問需求。
variations: Thanks for calling Elephant Car Rental. How can I help?
Elephant Car Rental customer service. What can I do for you?
Hi, you’ve reached Elephant Car Rental. How may I help you today?

keyPhrases.zh: 請問有什麼可以幫您的？

extraExamples.en: Hi, this is the front desk. How can I help you?
extraExamples.zh: 您好，這裡是櫃台。請問有什麼可以幫您的？

extraExamples.en: Thanks for calling TechCare customer support. What can I do for you?
extraExamples.zh: 感謝您致電 TechCare 客服。請問我能為您做什麼？

sentences.en: Hi, I’m calling because the tire pressure light has been on. I did try to put air in the tires but the light is still on after I put air in three different gas stations. The tires are not completely flat but I didn't want to risk it on a 2-hour freeway drive.
sentences.zh: 您好，我打電話是因為輪胎氣壓燈亮著。我已經試過在三個不同的加油站打氣，但燈還是亮著。輪胎並沒有完全洩氣，但我不想冒險開兩個小時的高速公路。

back.grammar: I’m calling because... 是電話中說明來電原因的自然開頭；has been on 是現在完成式，表示燈從過去亮到現在。
variations: Hi, I’m calling about the tire pressure light. It’s been on for a while.
The tire pressure warning light won’t turn off, even after I added air.
I put air in the tires, but the warning light is still showing.

extraExamples.en: I’m calling because my reservation isn’t showing up in the app.
extraExamples.zh: 我打電話是因為我的預訂沒有顯示在 app 裡。

extraExamples.en: I don’t want to risk it if the brakes are making that sound.
extraExamples.zh: 如果煞車發出那種聲音，我不想冒險。

sentences.en: Ok. And who am I speaking to?
sentences.zh: 好的。請問怎麼稱呼您？

back.grammar: Who am I speaking to? 是電話中禮貌詢問對方姓名的固定說法。
variations: May I ask who I’m speaking with?
Can I get your name, please?
Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with?

keyPhrases.zh: 請問您是哪位？／請問怎麼稱呼？

extraExamples.en: Before we continue, who am I speaking to?
extraExamples.zh: 在我們繼續之前，請問您怎麼稱呼？

extraExamples.en: May I ask who I’m speaking with today?
extraExamples.zh: 請問今天是哪位來電？

sentences.en: Oliver Chang.
sentences.zh: 我是Oliver Chang。

back.grammar: 電話中報姓名可直接說全名；若要更完整，也可說 This is Oliver Chang.。
variations: This is Oliver Chang.
My name is Oliver Chang.
It’s Oliver Chang.

extraExamples.en: This is Jason Lin. I’m calling about my booking.
extraExamples.zh: 我是 Jason Lin。我打來是詢問我的預訂。

extraExamples.en: My name is Emily Wang, and I have a question about my bill.
extraExamples.zh: 我叫 Emily Wang，我有帳單方面的問題。

sentences.en: Alright Mr. Chang. I apologize for the inconvenience we caused you. We will send someone to tow your car away. Where are you located right now?
sentences.zh: 好的，Chang先生，很抱歉為您帶來不便。我們會派人去拖走您的車。請問您現在在哪裡？

back.grammar: I apologize for... 是正式道歉；send someone to... 表示「派人去做某事」。
variations: I’m sorry for the trouble. We’ll send someone to tow the car.
Sorry about the inconvenience. We’ll have someone come get the car.
We apologize for the hassle. Where are you right now?

extraExamples.en: I apologize for the delay. We’ll send someone to check the room.
extraExamples.zh: 很抱歉延誤了。我們會派人去檢查房間。

extraExamples.en: They sent someone to tow my car after it broke down.
extraExamples.zh: 我的車拋錨後，他們派人來拖車。

sentences.en: Well, we are right down the ramp of a freeway. Oh, and I see a Mcdonald’s across the street. Let me check what the address is.
sentences.zh: 嗯，我們就在高速公路出口匝道的下面。哦，我看到街對面有一家麥當勞。我查一下它的地址。

back.grammar: right down the ramp of a freeway 是描述位置的口語說法；Let me check... 表示「讓我確認一下」。
variations: We’re just off the freeway exit ramp.
We’re right by the freeway off-ramp.
Let me look up the address real quick.

keyPhrases.zh: 就在匝道下面／下來不遠處

extraExamples.en: There’s a pharmacy across the street from the hotel.
extraExamples.zh: 飯店街對面有一家藥局。

extraExamples.en: Let me check the address before I send it to you.
extraExamples.zh: 我傳給你之前先確認一下地址。

sentences.en: No problem.
sentences.zh: 好的，沒問題。

back.grammar: No problem 在口語中可表示「沒問題／可以／不急」，常用來回應對方要查資料或請求。
variations: Sure, take your time.
That’s fine.
No worries.

extraExamples.en: No problem. I can wait a minute.
extraExamples.zh: 沒問題，我可以等一下。

extraExamples.en: Sure, take your time. I’m not in a rush.
extraExamples.zh: 當然，慢慢來。我不趕時間。

sentences.en: Ok, it’s 3891 Washington Street, California.
sentences.zh: 地址是加州華盛頓街3891號。

back.grammar: 報地址時英文順序通常是門牌號碼＋街名＋州名；it’s 可用來直接回答地址。
variations: The address is 3891 Washington Street, California.
It looks like it’s 3891 Washington Street in California.
I have 3891 Washington Street, California.

extraExamples.en: The address is 220 Lake Avenue, Pasadena.
extraExamples.zh: 地址是 Pasadena 的 Lake Avenue 220號。

extraExamples.en: It’s 15 Maple Street, right next to the bank.
extraExamples.zh: 地址是楓樹街15號，就在銀行旁邊。

sentences.en: 3891 Washington… and what’s the zip?
sentences.zh: 華盛頓街3891號……請問郵遞區號是多少？

back.grammar: what’s the zip? 是口語問「郵遞區號是多少？」zip 是 ZIP code 的簡短說法。
variations: What’s the ZIP code?
Do you have the ZIP?
Can you give me the ZIP code for that address?

extraExamples.en: What’s the ZIP code for your billing address?
extraExamples.zh: 您的帳單地址郵遞區號是多少？

extraExamples.en: I need the ZIP code to look up the store location.
extraExamples.zh: 我需要郵遞區號來查店的位置。

back.grammar: 回答數字資訊時可簡短用 It’s...；郵遞區號通常一個數字一個數字念。
variations: The ZIP is 94817.
It’s nine-four-eight-one-seven.

extraExamples.en: The gate code is 2468.
extraExamples.zh: 大門密碼是2468。

extraExamples.en: It’s 90210, if you need the ZIP code.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你需要郵遞區號，是90210。

sentences.en: Alright. The tow truck is on the way. It should get there in about 20 minutes.
sentences.zh: 好的，拖車已經派遣過去了，大約20分鐘後會到。

back.grammar: on the way 表示「正在來的路上」；should 表示合理預期，不是百分之百保證。
variations: The tow truck is headed your way.
A tow truck should be there in about 20 minutes.
Help is on the way. They’ll be there in around 20 minutes.

extraExamples.en: Your food is on the way and should arrive soon.
extraExamples.zh: 你的餐點正在路上，應該很快會到。

extraExamples.en: The technician should get there in about 30 minutes.
extraExamples.zh: 技術人員應該大約30分鐘後會到。

sentences.en: Okay. But will the tow truck also drop off another car when it picks up our current one? We were supposed to be at the airport in 3 hours because our flight is taking off in 4 hours!
sentences.zh: 好的，但拖車在拖走我們的車時，會不會也送來另一輛車？我們應該在三個小時內到達機場，因為我們的航班四個小時後就要起飛了！

back.grammar: will... also...? 用來確認是否會額外做某事；were supposed to 表示「原本應該／預定要」。
variations: Will they bring us a replacement car when they come?
Are they going to drop off another car when they pick this one up?
We’re supposed to be at the airport in three hours, and our flight leaves in four.

extraExamples.en: Can you drop off the keys when you pick up the package?
extraExamples.zh: 你來拿包裹時可以順便把鑰匙送來嗎？

extraExamples.en: We’re supposed to meet them at the restaurant at seven.
extraExamples.zh: 我們原本應該七點在餐廳跟他們碰面。

sentences.en: No, the towing service is just a third-party company that we use. So they will only pick up the current car. But we can send a driver to pick you up… but let’s see, the quickest driver will get to you in 40 minutes.
sentences.zh: 不，拖車服務是我們聘請的第三方公司，他們只負責拖走您現有的車。不過我們可以派一位司機來接您……讓我看看，最快的司機大約40分鐘後可以到達。

back.grammar: third-party company 指「第三方公司」；let’s see 是口語中邊查資料邊說的填充語。
variations: No, the tow company is a separate company we work with.
They’ll only take the car, but we can send someone to get you.
Let me check... the earliest driver can be there in 40 minutes.

extraExamples.en: The delivery is handled by a third-party company.
extraExamples.zh: 配送是由第三方公司處理的。

extraExamples.en: Let’s see, the next available appointment is Friday morning.
extraExamples.zh: 我看看，下一個可預約時段是週五早上。

sentences.en: 40 minutes? That wouldn’t work though. The drive to the airport from where we’re at takes about 3 hours. So we were already cutting it close. Is there someone you can send sooner?
sentences.zh: 40分鐘？那不行啊。從我們這裡到機場開車大約需要3個小時，我們已經很趕了。能派更快的人過來嗎？

back.grammar: That wouldn’t work 是委婉但明確地說「那不行」；cutting it close 表示時間很緊、差點來不及。
variations: That’s not going to work. We’re already short on time.
That won’t be enough time. We’re cutting it really close.
Is there any way someone can get here faster?

keyPhrases.zh: 時間抓得很緊／差點來不及

extraExamples.en: Leaving at 6:30 might be cutting it close if traffic is bad.
extraExamples.zh: 如果塞車，6:30出發可能會很趕。

extraExamples.en: That wouldn’t work for me because I have a meeting then.
extraExamples.zh: 那對我不行，因為我那時候有會議。

sentences.en: I’m afraid not, Mr. Chang.
sentences.zh: 恐怕不行，Chang先生。

back.grammar: I’m afraid not 是禮貌又委婉地說「恐怕不行／沒有辦法」。
variations: Unfortunately, no.
I’m sorry, but we don’t have anyone sooner.
I’m afraid we can’t send anyone earlier.

extraExamples.en: I’m afraid not. We’re fully booked tonight.
extraExamples.zh: 恐怕不行。我們今晚已經全滿了。

extraExamples.en: Unfortunately, we can’t change the delivery time today.
extraExamples.zh: 很遺憾，我們今天不能更改配送時間。

sentences.en: If we get an Uber, will the cost be compensated by you guys?
sentences.zh: 如果我們叫Uber，這筆費用你們會補償嗎？

back.grammar: If + 現在式, will + 原形動詞：用來問「如果發生某事，結果會怎樣？」；compensated by you guys 較正式，口語常說 reimburse us 或 cover the cost。
variations: If we grab an Uber, will you guys cover it?
If we take an Uber, can we get reimbursed?
Would you pay us back if we get an Uber?

extraExamples.en: If I take a taxi home, will the company cover the cost?
extraExamples.zh: 如果我搭計程車回家，公司會支付費用嗎？

extraExamples.en: Can I get reimbursed if I pay for parking myself?
extraExamples.zh: 如果我自己付停車費，可以報銷嗎？

sentences.en: We can’t really do that. I can either order you a driver or not.
sentences.zh: 我們真的無法補償。我可以幫您安排司機，或者不安排。

back.grammar: can’t really do that 是委婉拒絕，表示「我們不太能那樣做」；either A or B 表示「不是 A 就是 B／二選一」。
variations: I’m sorry, but we can’t cover that. I can set up a driver for you, or I can leave it as is.
Unfortunately, that’s not something we can do. I can either arrange a driver or not.
We’re not able to reimburse that. I can get you a driver, or I can’t.

keyPhrases.zh: 不是 A 就是 B

extraExamples.en: I can’t really do that, but I can ask my manager for you.
extraExamples.zh: 我真的不太能那樣做，但我可以幫你問主管。

extraExamples.en: You can either wait here or come back in an hour.
extraExamples.zh: 你可以在這裡等，或者一小時後再回來。

sentences.en: But…(Sigh) What would you suggest then? What other solutions do you guys offer?
sentences.zh: 但是……（嘆氣）那你建議怎麼辦？你們還有其他解決方案嗎？

back.grammar: What would you suggest? 是禮貌詢問建議的常見說法；do you guys offer 是口語中問公司或店家「你們有提供嗎？」
variations: So what do you recommend? Do you have any other options?
Then what should I do? What else can you offer?
What would you do in my situation? Are there any other solutions?

keyPhrases.zh: 你們；貴公司／你們這邊（口語）

extraExamples.en: What would you suggest if all the flights are sold out?
extraExamples.zh: 如果所有航班都賣完了，你會建議怎麼辦？

extraExamples.en: Do you guys offer same-day delivery?
extraExamples.zh: 你們有提供當日配送嗎？

sentences.en: We can get you a ride to our nearest Elephant Car Rental location or to the airport if you wish.
sentences.zh: 我們可以安排車送您到最近的大象租車門店，或者送您到機場，如果您需要的話。

back.grammar: get someone a ride 表示「幫某人安排車／找車」；if you wish 是較禮貌、正式的「如果您願意／需要的話」。
variations: We can arrange a ride to the closest Elephant Car Rental location or to the airport if you’d like.
We can send a car to take you to our nearest branch or to the airport.
If you want, we can get you transportation to the nearest location or the airport.

extraExamples.en: I can get you a ride home if your car won’t start.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你的車發不動，我可以幫你安排車回家。

extraExamples.en: We can move your appointment to tomorrow if you wish.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你需要的話，我們可以把你的預約改到明天。

sentences.en: But…we wouldn’t make it on time… nevermind, I guess I will figure that part out. Please just send the tow truck here as soon as you can.
sentences.zh: 但是……[我們根本來不及_]算了，我自己想辦法吧。請儘快把拖車派過來。

back.grammar: make it on time 是口語中很常用的片語，表示「準時趕到」；figure that out 表示「自己想辦法解決」。
variations: We’re not gonna make it in time. Never mind—I’ll deal with that part. Just send the tow truck as soon as possible.
There’s no way we’ll get there on time. Forget it, I’ll figure it out. Please send the tow truck right away.
We won’t make it in time anyway. Never mind, I’ll handle that. Please get the tow truck here ASAP.

extraExamples.en: If we leave now, we might still make it on time.
extraExamples.zh: 如果我們現在出發，可能還來得及準時到。

extraExamples.en: Don’t worry about dinner. I’ll figure that part out.
extraExamples.zh: 晚餐你不用擔心，那部分我會想辦法。

sentences.en: Yes, it is on the way already.
sentences.zh: 好的，拖車已經在路上了。

back.grammar: on the way 表示「在路上」；already 放在句尾或句中都可，用來強調事情已經發生。
variations: Yes, it’s already on its way.
Yes, the tow truck is headed there now.
Yes, it’s on the way to you right now.

extraExamples.en: Your food is already on the way.
extraExamples.zh: 你的餐點已經在路上了。

extraExamples.en: I’m headed there now. I’ll be there in ten minutes.
extraExamples.zh: 我現在正往那裡去，十分鐘後到。

sentences.en: I might have to miss my flight! Do you compensate for hotel stays if I have to get a room because of your rental car issue?
sentences.zh: 我可能會錯過我的航班！如果因為你們的租車問題我不得不住酒店，你們會補償住宿費嗎？

back.grammar: might have to + 原形動詞 表示「可能不得不」；Do you compensate for...? 可問是否補償某項費用，口語更常說 cover 或 reimburse。
variations: I might miss my flight! Will you cover a hotel if I need one because of the rental car problem?
There’s a chance I’ll miss my flight. Do you reimburse hotel costs if this is because of your car issue?
I may have to get a hotel. Would you guys pay for that if it’s due to the rental car problem?

extraExamples.en: I might have to cancel dinner if this meeting runs late.
extraExamples.zh: 如果這個會議拖太晚，我可能不得不取消晚餐。

extraExamples.en: Will the airline cover hotel stays if the flight gets canceled?
extraExamples.zh: 如果航班被取消，航空公司會支付住宿費嗎？

sentences.en: I’m sorry to hear that Mr. Chang. Unfortunately, we can’t do that from the customer support side. You can talk to the branch manager to see if they can help you out.
sentences.zh: 很抱歉聽到這件事，Chang先生。不過，我們客服這邊無法處理這件事。您可以和門店經理聯繫，看看他們能否幫助您。

back.grammar: I’m sorry to hear that 是客服常用安慰語；to see if... 表示「看看是否……」；help you out 是口語的「幫你一把」。
variations: I’m sorry about that, Mr. Chang. Unfortunately, customer support can’t handle that, but you can check with the branch manager.
Sorry to hear that, Mr. Chang. We can’t approve that from here, but the branch manager may be able to help.
I understand, Mr. Chang. Unfortunately, that’s outside customer support, but you can speak with the branch manager and see what they can do.

keyPhrases.zh: 從客服這邊／以客服權限來說

extraExamples.en: I’m sorry to hear that your package still hasn’t arrived.
extraExamples.zh: 很抱歉聽到你的包裹還沒送到。

extraExamples.en: Talk to the front desk to see if they can help you out.
extraExamples.zh: 去跟櫃台談談，看他們能不能幫你。

sentences.en: Aight. Bye!
sentences.zh: 好的，再見！

back.grammar: Aight 是 all right 的非常口語、非正式縮法，常見於朋友間或很隨性的對話，不適合正式客服場合。
variations: Alright. Bye.
Okay, bye.
Got it. Bye.

extraExamples.en: Aight, I’ll text you when I get there.
extraExamples.zh: 好啦，我到那裡再傳訊息給你。

extraExamples.en: Okay, bye. Talk to you later.
extraExamples.zh: 好，掰，晚點聊。

units.title: 交朋友閒聊
units.scenario: Ian在地鐵上認識新朋友

sentences.en: Excuse me, do you know how to use this ticket machine?
sentences.zh: 不好意思，你知道怎麼用這個售票機嗎？

back.grammar: 「Do you know how to + V」是很自然、禮貌地請教做法的句型。
variations: Excuse me, do you know how this machine works?
Sorry, could you show me how to use this ticket machine?
Hey, do you happen to know how to buy a ticket on this thing?

keyPhrases.phrase: how to use this ticket machine

extraExamples.en: Do you know how to connect to the Wi-Fi here?
extraExamples.zh: 你知道怎麼連這裡的 Wi-Fi 嗎？

extraExamples.en: Excuse me, do you know how to get to Gate 12?
extraExamples.zh: 不好意思，你知道怎麼去 12 號登機門嗎？

sentences.en: Yeah, just put your card here, click here… and here. There you go!
sentences.zh: 嗯，只要把你的卡放在這裡，點一下這裡……再點一下這裡。好了！

back.grammar: 「just + 動詞」常用來表示步驟很簡單；「There you go」口語中表示「好了、完成了」。
variations: Sure, just tap your card here, then press this and this. All set!
Yeah, put your card right here, hit these buttons, and you’re good to go.
Yep, just place your card here, choose this, then that. There you go!

extraExamples.en: Just sign here and here. There you go!
extraExamples.zh: 只要在這裡和這裡簽名。好了！

extraExamples.en: Put the code in, hit enter, and you’re good to go.
extraExamples.zh: 輸入代碼，按 Enter，就可以了。

sentences.en: Wow, thanks!
sentences.zh: 哇，謝謝你！

back.grammar: 「Wow, thanks!」是簡短自然的口語感謝，wow 表示驚訝或感激。
variations: Oh wow, thank you!
Awesome, thanks a lot!
Great, I appreciate it!

extraExamples.en: Wow, thanks! That really helped.
extraExamples.zh: 哇，謝謝！那真的很有幫助。

extraExamples.en: Awesome, thanks a lot for the ride.
extraExamples.zh: 太好了，非常謝謝你載我。

sentences.en: No worries! Are you going to the main station?
sentences.zh: 不客氣！你是要去主站嗎？

back.grammar: 「No worries」是美式口語中回應感謝的說法，意思像「不客氣、沒事」。
variations: No problem! Are you headed to the main station?
Of course! Are you going to the central station?
No worries! You going to the main station?

extraExamples.en: No worries! Are you going downtown?
extraExamples.zh: 不客氣！你要去市中心嗎？

extraExamples.en: No problem. Are you headed to the airport?
extraExamples.zh: 沒問題。你是要去機場嗎？

sentences.en: Yeah. You’re going the same direction?
sentences.zh: 對啊。你也是往同一個方向嗎？

back.grammar: 「You’re going the same direction?」省略了 Are，是口語中常見的簡化問句。
variations: Yeah. Are you headed the same way?
Yep. You going that way too?
Yeah. Are we going in the same direction?

extraExamples.en: You’re going the same direction? Want to walk together?
extraExamples.zh: 你也是往同一個方向嗎？要一起走嗎？

extraExamples.en: Are you headed the same way, or are you taking a different train?
extraExamples.zh: 你也是往同一個方向，還是要搭別班車？

sentences.en: Yeah! So it’s your first time here in SF.
sentences.zh: 對！所以這是你第一次來舊金山吧。

back.grammar: 「It’s your first time here」用來表示「這是你第一次來這裡」；句尾語氣像是在確認。
variations: Nice! Is this your first time in SF?
Oh cool, so you’ve never been to San Francisco before?
Yeah? First time here in the city?

keyPhrases.zh: San Francisco 舊金山的縮寫

extraExamples.en: Is this your first time here in New York?
extraExamples.zh: 這是你第一次來紐約嗎？

extraExamples.en: So it’s your first time trying Korean food?
extraExamples.zh: 所以這是你第一次吃韓國菜嗎？

sentences.en: Yup, just in town for a conference tomorrow.
sentences.zh: 是啊，我來這裡參加一個明天的會議。

back.grammar: 「in town for...」是口語片語，表示「來這個城市是為了……」。
variations: Yep, I’m just here for a conference tomorrow.
Yeah, I’m in town for a work conference.
Yup, just visiting for a conference tomorrow.

extraExamples.en: I’m in town for a wedding this weekend.
extraExamples.zh: 我這週末來這裡參加婚禮。

extraExamples.en: She’s just in town for a few meetings.
extraExamples.zh: 她只是來這裡開幾個會。

sentences.en: Cool… What conference? What do you do?
sentences.zh: 很酷啊……什麼會議？你是做什麼工作的？

back.grammar: 「What do you do?」不是問你正在做什麼，而是問「你從事什麼工作？」
variations: Nice. What kind of conference? What do you do for work?
Oh cool. What’s the conference about? What’s your job?
That sounds interesting. What field are you in?

extraExamples.en: What do you do? I’m guessing you work in tech.
extraExamples.zh: 你做什麼工作？我猜你是在科技業。

extraExamples.en: What’s the conference about? Is it related to education?
extraExamples.zh: 那個會議是關於什麼的？跟教育有關嗎？

sentences.en: I am a teacher. You?
sentences.zh: 我是老師。你呢？

back.grammar: 回答職業用「I’m a/an + 職業」最自然；「You?」是「How about you?」的口語省略。
variations: I’m a teacher. How about you?
I teach. What about you?
I’m in education. You?

extraExamples.en: I’m a nurse. You?
extraExamples.zh: 我是護理師。你呢？

extraExamples.en: I work in marketing. How about you?
extraExamples.zh: 我做行銷。你呢？

sentences.en: Wow. I do import and export. Tea from Africa you know…
sentences.zh: 哇，我做進出口生意。是從非洲進口茶葉的…

back.grammar: 「I do import and export」口語中 do 可表示「從事某種工作或生意」。
variations: I’m in import-export, mostly tea from Africa.
I run an import-export business—African tea, mainly.
I import tea from Africa and sell it here.

extraExamples.en: I do real estate, mostly small apartments.
extraExamples.zh: 我做房地產，主要是小公寓。

extraExamples.en: She’s in import and export, mainly coffee beans.
extraExamples.zh: 她做進出口，主要是咖啡豆。

sentences.en: Interesting! What got you into that?
sentences.zh: 真有趣！是什麼讓你開始做這個的？

back.grammar: 「What got you into...?」是口語問法，意思是「是什麼讓你開始對……感興趣／進入這行？」
variations: That’s interesting! How did you get into that?
Cool! What made you start doing that?
Really? How’d you end up in that business?

keyPhrases.zh: 是什麼讓你開始做那個的？

extraExamples.en: What got you into photography?
extraExamples.zh: 是什麼讓你開始玩攝影的？

extraExamples.en: How did you get into teaching?
extraExamples.zh: 你是怎麼開始當老師的？

sentences.en: Well, I am vegan so I enjoy cooking a lot. I went to West Africa to visit family there and got so hooked on this tea that they have there.
sentences.zh: 嗯，我是素食主義者，所以我很喜歡煮飯。我去西非探親的時候，迷上了那邊的一種茶。

back.grammar: 「got hooked on」是口語片語，表示「迷上、上癮於」某事物。
variations: Well, I’m vegan, so I love cooking. I visited family in West Africa and got really into this local tea.
I’m vegan and I cook a lot. When I went to West Africa to see family, I fell in love with the tea there.
Since I’m vegan, I’m pretty into cooking. I tried this tea in West Africa and got hooked.

keyPhrases.zh: 我是純素者／素食主義者

extraExamples.en: I got hooked on that show after one episode.
extraExamples.zh: 我看了一集之後就迷上那部影集了。

extraExamples.en: She enjoys cooking a lot, especially on weekends.
extraExamples.zh: 她很喜歡煮飯，尤其是週末。

sentences.en: And you just decided to start bringing those teas back here in the US?
sentences.zh: 然後你就決定把那些茶帶回美國？

back.grammar: 「decided to + V」表示「決定做某事」；「bring ... back」是「把……帶回來」。
variations: So you decided to start bringing that tea back to the U.S.?
And then you started importing those teas here?
So that’s when you decided to bring the tea back home?

extraExamples.en: I decided to start working out after work.
extraExamples.zh: 我決定開始下班後運動。

extraExamples.en: She brought some snacks back from Japan.
extraExamples.zh: 她從日本帶了一些零食回來。

sentences.en: Yeah, my roommate at the time was vegan and really loves tea too. So I started to bring him and my other friends those teas and it just went from there.
sentences.zh: 是啊，那時我的室友也是素食主義者，而且他也非常愛茶。所以我開始帶這些茶給他和其他朋友，事情就這樣發展起來了。

back.grammar: 「at the time」表示「當時」；「it just went from there」是口語說法，意思是「事情就從那裡開始發展」。
variations: Yeah, my roommate back then was vegan and loved tea too, so I brought some back for him and my friends. It kind of took off from there.
Yeah, my roommate at the time was also vegan and really into tea. I started sharing it with friends, and things grew from there.
Right. I brought some tea back for my roommate and a few friends, and it just turned into a business.

keyPhrases.phrase: bring him and my other friends those teas
keyPhrases.zh: 帶那些茶給他和其他朋友
事情就這樣發展起來了

extraExamples.en: I was living in Boston at the time.
extraExamples.zh: 我當時住在波士頓。

extraExamples.en: I made cookies for a few coworkers, and it just went from there.
extraExamples.zh: 我做餅乾給幾個同事，事情就這樣發展起來了。

sentences.en: Oh my stop is next. What a story! And thanks for helping me again!
sentences.zh: 哦，我下一站就到了。真是個精彩的故事！再次謝謝你的幫忙！

back.grammar: 「My stop is next」表示「我下一站要下車」；「What a + 名詞!」是感嘆句，表示「真是……！」
variations: Oh, this is my next stop. What a great story! And thanks again for your help!
Looks like my stop’s coming up. Great story—and thanks again!
Oh, I get off at the next stop. That’s an amazing story. Thanks again for helping me!

extraExamples.en: My stop is next, so I should get ready.
extraExamples.zh: 我下一站就到了，所以我該準備一下。

extraExamples.en: What a day! I’m exhausted.
extraExamples.zh: 真是漫長的一天！我累壞了。

sentences.en: Oh wait, I’m gonna meet up with my friends to go rock climbing later. Do you wanna join us? I’ll text you.
sentences.zh: 哦，等等，我待會兒要和朋友一起去攀岩。你要不要加入我們？我可以等下訊息你（時間地點）。

back.grammar: 「I’m gonna...」是口語的未來式，等於「I’m going to...」；「meet up with」表示約人碰面。
variations: Oh wait, I’m meeting up with some friends for rock climbing later. Wanna come? I’ll text you the details.
Actually, I’m gonna go climbing with my friends later. Do you want to join? I’ll message you.
Hold on, I’m meeting some friends to go rock climbing later. You should come! I’ll send you the info.

keyPhrases.zh: 和朋友碰面／約朋友見面

extraExamples.en: I’m gonna meet up with my coworkers for dinner after work.
extraExamples.zh: 我下班後要和同事碰面吃晚餐。

extraExamples.en: We’re going rock climbing this Saturday. Do you wanna join us?
extraExamples.zh: 我們這週六要去攀岩。你要不要加入我們？

sentences.en: Sounds fun! Um you can take my instagram and I’ll see if I still have energy for that then.
sentences.zh: 聽起來不錯！嗯，你可以加我的 Instagram，到時看看我還有沒有精力去。

back.grammar: 「I’ll see if...」表示「我到時看看是否……」；「then」在這裡指「到那時候」。
variations: Sounds fun! You can add me on Instagram, and I’ll see how I feel later.
That sounds cool! Grab my Instagram, and I’ll let you know if I’m up for it then.
Sounds good! Add me on Instagram, and I’ll see if I have the energy later.

keyPhrases.zh: 加我的 Instagram／記一下我的 IG

keyPhrases.phrase: see if I still have energy
keyPhrases.zh: 看看我到時還有沒有精力

extraExamples.en: I’ll see if I still have energy to go out after work.
extraExamples.zh: 我會看看下班後還有沒有精力出門。

extraExamples.en: Add me on Instagram, and I’ll send you the photos later.
extraExamples.zh: 加我 Instagram，我晚點把照片傳給你。

sentences.en: Cool cool.
sentences.zh: 好喔好喔。

back.grammar: 「Cool cool」是很口語的回應，表示「好喔、了解、可以」，語氣輕鬆隨意。
variations: Sounds good.
Alright, cool.
Gotcha, cool.

extraExamples.en: Cool cool, just text me when you get there.
extraExamples.zh: 好喔好喔，你到了再傳訊息給我。

extraExamples.en: Cool cool, I’ll see you around 7 then.
extraExamples.zh: 好喔好喔，那我大概七點見你。

sentences.en: Alright. Take care and nice meeting you again!
sentences.zh: 好，那再見啦！很高興認識你！

back.grammar: 「Take care」是道別時常用語；「Nice meeting you」表示第一次或剛認識後說「很高興認識你」。
variations: Alright, take care. It was nice meeting you!
Okay, see you around. Nice meeting you!
Alright, have a good one. It was great meeting you!

extraExamples.en: Take care, and text me when you get home.
extraExamples.zh: 保重，到家後傳訊息給我。

extraExamples.en: It was nice meeting you. Hope we run into each other again soon.
extraExamples.zh: 很高興認識你。希望我們很快又能碰到。

units.title: 心靈對話
units.scenario: Cheryl室友說她想跟籃球隊男友分手

sentences.en: Hey Daisy! Why are you so quiet today? Is everything alright?
sentences.zh: 嘿，Daisy！你今天怎麼這麼安靜？一切都還好嗎？

back.grammar: 「Why are you so quiet today?」用現在式/現在進行感問對方當下狀態；「Is everything alright?」是關心對方是否還好的常用口語。
variations: Hey Daisy, you seem really quiet today. Everything okay?
You’re kind of quiet today. Is something wrong?
Hey, you good? You’ve been super quiet.

extraExamples.en: You seem so quiet at lunch today. Is everything alright?
extraExamples.zh: 你今天午餐時看起來好安靜。一切都還好嗎？

extraExamples.en: Hey, you’ve been quiet all morning. Everything okay?
extraExamples.zh: 嘿，你整個早上都很安靜。還好嗎？

sentences.en: My… my boyfriend and I just broke up.
sentences.zh: 我…我和我男朋友剛分手了。

back.grammar: 「just broke up」用過去式加 just，表示事情才剛發生；「break up」是分手的片語動詞。
variations: My boyfriend and I just split up.
We just ended things.
I just broke it off with my boyfriend.

extraExamples.en: They broke up last night after a long talk.
extraExamples.zh: 他們昨晚長談後分手了。

extraExamples.en: I heard Jenna and her boyfriend just split up.
extraExamples.zh: 我聽說 Jenna 和她男朋友剛分手。

sentences.en: You… you what? Michael?
sentences.zh: 你…你說什麼？Michael？

back.grammar: 「You what?」是口語中表示震驚、沒聽懂或不敢相信的反問，相當於「你說什麼？」
variations: Wait, what? Michael?
You did what? With Michael?
Hold on—Michael? Are you serious?

extraExamples.en: You what? You quit your job?
extraExamples.zh: 你說什麼？你辭職了？

extraExamples.en: Wait, what? You’re moving to New York?
extraExamples.zh: 等等，什麼？你要搬去紐約？

sentences.en: Yeah, Michael. I’m still processing everything too.
sentences.zh: 對，Michael。我也還在消化這一切。

back.grammar: 「I’m still processing everything」是很自然的口語，表示還在消化、整理情緒或資訊。
variations: Yeah, Michael. I’m still trying to wrap my head around it.
Yeah. I’m still taking it all in too.
Yeah, Michael. I haven’t fully processed it yet either.

extraExamples.en: I’m still processing what happened at the meeting.
extraExamples.zh: 我還在消化會議上發生的事。

extraExamples.en: Give me a minute—I’m still trying to wrap my head around this news.
extraExamples.zh: 給我一點時間，我還在消化這個消息。

sentences.en: I’m sorry to hear that. It will take some time but take all the time you need!
sentences.zh: 我很遺憾聽到這件事。這需要時間，但你可以慢慢來！

back.grammar: 「I’m sorry to hear that」是聽到壞消息時表達同情的固定說法；「take all the time you need」表示不用急、慢慢來。
variations: I’m really sorry. Take as much time as you need.
That’s so hard to hear. Don’t rush yourself.
I’m sorry you’re going through that. Give yourself time.

keyPhrases.phrase: take all the time you need

extraExamples.en: I’m sorry to hear about your grandma. Take all the time you need.
extraExamples.zh: 聽到你奶奶的事我很遺憾。你可以慢慢來。

extraExamples.en: Healing takes some time, so don’t be too hard on yourself.
extraExamples.zh: 療傷需要時間，所以別對自己太苛刻。

sentences.en: Thanks Cheryl!
sentences.zh: 謝謝你，Cheryl！

back.grammar: 「Thanks」比「Thank you」更口語、輕鬆；直接加名字可讓語氣更親近。
variations: Thanks, Cheryl. I appreciate it.
Thank you, Cheryl.
Thanks. That means a lot.

extraExamples.en: Thanks, Mia. I really needed to hear that.
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝你，Mia。我真的需要聽到那句話。

extraExamples.en: Thanks. That means a lot to me.
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝。這對我來說很重要。

sentences.en: But what happened exactly? I thought you guys just had a great date night out celebrating your anniversary last week.
sentences.zh: 到底發生了什麼？我還以為你們上週才度過一個很棒的周年慶約會夜。

back.grammar: 「What happened exactly?」用 exactly 追問細節；「I thought...」表示原本以為，後面接過去發生的事。
variations: So what actually happened? I thought you two had a great anniversary date last week.
Wait, what went wrong? Didn’t you guys just celebrate your anniversary?
What happened? I thought your date night last week went really well.

extraExamples.en: What happened exactly? I thought the interview went well.
extraExamples.zh: 到底發生什麼事？我以為面試很順利。

extraExamples.en: I thought you guys had a fun night out last weekend.
extraExamples.zh: 我以為你們上週末出去玩得很開心。

sentences.en: It’s complicated…
sentences.zh: 事情很複雜…

back.grammar: 「It’s complicated」是很常見的口語，用來表示情況複雜、不好三言兩語說清楚。
variations: It’s kind of complicated.
It’s a long story.
There’s a lot to it.

extraExamples.en: Why did you leave the company? It’s complicated.
extraExamples.zh: 你為什麼離開那家公司？說來複雜。

extraExamples.en: I can’t explain it in one sentence. It’s a long story.
extraExamples.zh: 我沒辦法一句話解釋。說來話長。

sentences.en: Did he break up with you or did you dump him?
sentences.zh: 是他和你分手，還是你甩了他？

back.grammar: 「break up with someone」是跟某人分手；「dump someone」是口語中「甩掉某人」，語氣較直接。
variations: Did he end things, or did you?
Was it his decision, or yours?
Did he break it off, or did you dump him?

extraExamples.en: She dumped him after she found out he lied.
extraExamples.zh: 她發現他說謊後就甩了他。

extraExamples.en: Did he break up with you over text?
extraExamples.zh: 他是用簡訊跟你分手的嗎？

sentences.en: I broke up with him. There is no specific reason… but lately I’ve just been thinking that we were not on the same page on a lot of things like we used to be.
sentences.zh: 我和他分手了。沒有特定的原因…但最近我一直覺得我們不像以前那樣在很多事情上有共同的想法。

back.grammar: 「lately I’ve just been thinking」是現在完成進行式，表示最近一段時間一直在想；「be on the same page」表示想法一致。
variations: I ended things with him. There wasn’t one big reason, but lately I’ve felt like we weren’t on the same page anymore.
I broke it off. Nothing specific happened, but we’ve been seeing things differently lately.
I’m the one who ended it. We just haven’t been aligned on a lot of things.

extraExamples.en: Lately I’ve been thinking about changing jobs.
extraExamples.zh: 最近我一直在想換工作。

extraExamples.en: We need to make sure we’re on the same page before the meeting.
extraExamples.zh: 開會前我們要確認彼此有共識。

sentences.en: Ah I see. Well, people change. And we change too. If you think it was the right thing to do, then I support that!
sentences.zh: 哦，我明白了。嗯，人是會改變的。我們自己也會改變。如果你認為這是正確的選擇，那我支持你！

back.grammar: 「If you think..., then I support that」是用 if 條件句表達尊重對方決定；「the right thing to do」指正確的選擇。
variations: I get it. People change. If it felt right to you, I’m behind you.
That makes sense. People grow apart. If you think it was best, I support you.
Yeah, people change. If you believe it was the right call, I’ve got your back.

extraExamples.en: If you think quitting is the right thing to do, I support that.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你覺得辭職是正確的選擇，我支持你。

extraExamples.en: People change, and sometimes friendships change too.
extraExamples.zh: 人會改變，有時友情也會變。

sentences.en: Thanks, girl! It was hard to have the conversation but I think it’s for the better.
sentences.zh: 謝謝你，女孩！進行那次對話很難，但我覺得這樣對彼此都更好。

back.grammar: 「Thanks, girl!」是女生朋友間親暱的口語；「for the better」表示長遠來看會更好。
variations: Thanks, girl. It was a tough talk, but I think it’s better this way.
Thanks. That conversation was hard, but I think it needed to happen.
Thanks, girl. It wasn’t easy, but I think it’s for the best.

keyPhrases.zh: 往好的方向、對未來更好

extraExamples.en: It was hard to have the conversation with my boss, but I feel better now.
extraExamples.zh: 跟老闆談那件事很難，但我現在感覺好多了。

extraExamples.en: Moving out was scary, but it was for the better.
extraExamples.zh: 搬出去很可怕，但長遠來看是好的。

sentences.en: Take your time to heal. Plus… there are plenty of fish in the sea! I’m here when you’re ready to get out and mingle! Haha.
sentences.zh: 妳慢慢來。還有啊…天涯何處無芳草！等妳準備好了再出去交朋友，我隨時都在！哈哈。

back.grammar: 「Take your time to heal」是安慰對方慢慢療傷；「There are plenty of fish in the sea」是俗語，意思是還有很多其他對象。
variations: Give yourself time to heal. And hey, there are plenty of other people out there.
Don’t rush your healing. When you’re ready to go out again, I’m here.
Take your time. When you feel like meeting people again, I’ve got you.

keyPhrases.phrase: plenty of fish in the sea

extraExamples.en: Take your time to heal after the breakup.
extraExamples.zh: 分手後慢慢療傷就好。

extraExamples.en: When you’re ready to get out and mingle, let me know.
extraExamples.zh: 等你準備好出去社交時，告訴我。

sentences.en: Not yet! I just want to focus on myself.
sentences.zh: 還沒啦！我現在只想專心在自己身上。

back.grammar: 「Not yet」表示還沒準備好；「focus on myself」是口語中指把重心放回自己身上。
variations: Not yet. I just need to focus on myself right now.
I’m not ready yet. I want to work on myself for a while.
Not right now. I just want some time for me.

extraExamples.en: I’m not ready to date yet. I want to focus on myself.
extraExamples.zh: 我還沒準備好約會。我想專注在自己身上。

extraExamples.en: After leaving that job, she took time to focus on herself.
extraExamples.zh: 離開那份工作後，她花時間專注在自己身上。

sentences.en: I’m here for you! And yeah, the silver lining is that it gives you time to focus on yourself now, as you’ve always wanted to!
sentences.zh: 我會一直在這裡支持妳！而且，正面來看，這樣妳終於可以好好專注在自己，像妳一直想的那樣！

back.grammar: 「I’m here for you」是表達支持的固定說法；「the silver lining」指壞事中的好處、正面的一面。
variations: I’m here for you. The bright side is, you finally have time to focus on yourself.
I’ve got your back. At least now you can put yourself first.
I’m always here. If there’s a silver lining, it’s that you get more time for yourself now.

keyPhrases.zh: 壞事中的一線希望／好的一面

extraExamples.en: I’m here for you if you need to talk.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你需要聊聊，我都在。

extraExamples.en: The silver lining is that we saved money by staying home this weekend.
extraExamples.zh: 好的一面是，我們這週末待在家省了錢。

sentences.en: Yeah, like what my mom always told me, sometimes endings are blessings in disguise.
sentences.zh: 對啊，就像我媽常跟我說的，有時候結束反而是隱藏的祝福。

back.grammar: "like what..." 是口語中用來引出別人說過的話；"blessings in disguise" 表示「偽裝成壞事的好事」。
variations: Yeah, like my mom always says, some endings turn out to be a good thing.
Totally. My mom always tells me that sometimes something bad is actually a blessing.
Yeah, sometimes when things end, it’s for the best—like my mom says.

keyPhrases.phrase: like what my mom always told me

extraExamples.en: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise because I found a better one.
extraExamples.zh: 失去那份工作反而是因禍得福，因為我找到更好的工作。

extraExamples.en: Like what my dad always told me, you learn the most from hard times.
extraExamples.zh: 就像我爸常跟我說的，你從困難時期學到最多。

sentences.en: Oh, how’s your mom lately?
sentences.zh: 喔對，妳媽最近怎麼樣?

back.grammar: "How’s... lately?" 是關心近況的自然問法，lately 表示「最近這段時間」。
variations: How has your mom been lately?
How’s your mom doing these days?
By the way, how’s your mom been?

extraExamples.en: How’s your brother doing lately?
extraExamples.zh: 你弟最近怎麼樣？

extraExamples.en: How have you been lately? You seem really busy.
extraExamples.zh: 你最近過得怎麼樣？你看起來很忙。

sentences.en: Well, my aunt just passed away last month so she is not in the best place.
sentences.zh: 嗯，我阿姨上個月過世了，她現在心情不是很好。

back.grammar: "passed away" 是 "died" 的委婉說法；"not in the best place" 常用來表示情緒或心理狀態不好。
variations: My aunt passed away last month, so my mom’s been having a hard time.
My aunt died last month, so she’s not doing great right now.
My aunt passed last month, and my mom’s been pretty down.

extraExamples.en: Her grandfather passed away last week, so she’s taking some time off.
extraExamples.zh: 她爺爺上週過世了，所以她請了一些假休息。

extraExamples.en: I’m not in the best place right now, but I’ll be okay.
extraExamples.zh: 我現在狀態不是很好，但我會沒事的。

sentences.en: Oh no, my condolences!
sentences.zh: 天啊，真的是很抱歉！

back.grammar: "My condolences" 是表達哀悼的正式但常見說法；口語中常搭配 "I’m so sorry"。
variations: I’m so sorry for your loss.
Oh no, I’m really sorry to hear that.
That’s awful. Please accept my condolences.

keyPhrases.zh: 請節哀；我致上哀悼之意
對你的喪親之痛感到遺憾

extraExamples.en: I’m so sorry for your loss. Let me know if you need anything.
extraExamples.zh: 對你的喪親之痛我很遺憾。如果你需要什麼，跟我說。

extraExamples.en: Please accept my condolences. Your father was a wonderful person.
extraExamples.zh: 請節哀。你父親是一位很好的人。

sentences.en: Thanks! She’ll come out of it, I’m sure.
sentences.zh: 謝謝妳！我相信她會挺過來的。

back.grammar: "come out of it" 是片語，表示從低潮、病痛或困境中恢復過來；"I’m sure" 表示有信心。
variations: Thanks. I’m sure she’ll get through it.
Thank you. She’ll be okay eventually, I know it.
Thanks. I think she’ll bounce back with time.

extraExamples.en: He’s been really stressed, but he’ll come out of it soon.
extraExamples.zh: 他最近壓力很大，但他很快會走出來的。

extraExamples.en: I’m sure you’ll get through this. You’re stronger than you think.
extraExamples.zh: 我相信你會撐過去的。你比你想的更堅強。

sentences.en: Is there anything I can do for you?
sentences.zh: 有什麼我可以幫忙的嗎？

back.grammar: "Is there anything I can do for you?" 是表達願意幫忙的禮貌句型，常用於安慰或支援別人。
variations: Is there anything I can do to help?
Let me know if you need anything.
How can I help?

keyPhrases.phrase: Is there anything I can do for you?
keyPhrases.zh: 有什麼我可以幫你的嗎？

extraExamples.en: Is there anything I can do for you while you’re sick?
extraExamples.zh: 你生病的時候，有什麼我可以幫你的嗎？

extraExamples.en: Let me know if you need a ride to the airport.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你需要人載你去機場，跟我說。

sentences.en: Maybe movie night tonight?
sentences.zh: 要不要今晚來個電影之夜？

back.grammar: "Maybe...?" 可以用來輕鬆提出建議；"movie night" 指在家或一起看電影的活動。
variations: How about a movie night tonight?
Wanna do movie night tonight?
Maybe we could watch a movie tonight?

keyPhrases.zh: 電影之夜；一起看電影的晚上

extraExamples.en: Maybe coffee tomorrow morning?
extraExamples.zh: 要不要明天早上喝咖啡？

extraExamples.en: How about a movie night this Friday?
extraExamples.zh: 這週五來個電影之夜怎麼樣？

sentences.en: I’ll get the popcorn ready!
sentences.zh: 我準備爆米花！

back.grammar: "I’ll..." 表示當下決定要做某事；"get something ready" 是「把某物準備好」。
variations: I’ll make the popcorn!
I’ll take care of the popcorn.
I’ve got the popcorn covered!

extraExamples.en: I’ll get the snacks ready before everyone comes over.
extraExamples.zh: 大家來之前，我會把零食準備好。

extraExamples.en: You pick the movie, and I’ll take care of the drinks.
extraExamples.zh: 你選電影，我來負責飲料。

sentences.en: You’re the best!
sentences.zh: 妳最棒了！

back.grammar: "You’re the best!" 是口語中稱讚、感謝對方的自然說法，比字面上的「你是最棒的」更像「你人真好」。
variations: You’re amazing!
You’re awesome!
I appreciate you so much!

extraExamples.en: You brought me coffee? You’re the best!
extraExamples.zh: 你幫我帶咖啡？你人也太好了！

extraExamples.en: Thanks for helping me move. I really appreciate you.
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝你幫我搬家。我真的很感激你。

units.title: 開玩笑幽默
units.scenario: Cheryl嘗試網路約會失敗

sentences.en: Hey! Cheryl right?
sentences.zh: 嗨！是Cheryl對吧？

back.grammar: 「名詞/名字 + right?」是口語中確認對方身分或資訊的簡短問法。
variations: Hey, you’re Cheryl, right?
Hi, Cheryl, isn’t it?
Hey, it’s Cheryl, right?

extraExamples.en: You’re the new intern, right?
extraExamples.zh: 你是新來的實習生，對吧？

extraExamples.en: This is our stop, right?
extraExamples.zh: 這是我們要下車的站，對吧？

sentences.en: Hi! Anthony?
sentences.zh: 嗨！是Anthony吧？

back.grammar: 只說名字加上升語調，是很自然的口語確認方式。
variations: Hi, are you Anthony?
Hey, Anthony, right?
You must be Anthony.

extraExamples.en: Sarah? I’m Mike from the app.
extraExamples.zh: Sarah？我是交友軟體上的 Mike。

extraExamples.en: Table for two?
extraExamples.zh: 兩位用餐嗎？

sentences.en: Yeah! Wow, you are taller than I imagined.
sentences.zh: 對！哇，妳比我想像的還高耶。

back.grammar: 「比較級 + than I imagined」表示「比我想像中更……」。
variations: Wow, you’re taller than I thought.
You’re way taller in person.
I didn’t realize you were this tall.

extraExamples.en: This apartment is bigger than I imagined.
extraExamples.zh: 這間公寓比我想像中還大。

extraExamples.en: The movie was funnier than I thought.
extraExamples.zh: 那部電影比我想像中更好笑。

sentences.en: Haha, thanks? You are… the same as I imagined?
sentences.zh: 哈哈，謝謝？你…跟我想像的一樣高？

back.grammar: 「the same as + 子句/名詞」表示「和……一樣」，句尾上揚可帶開玩笑或不確定語氣。
variations: Haha, thanks? You’re pretty much how I pictured you.
Thanks, I guess? You look just like I imagined.
Haha, I’ll take that. You’re exactly what I expected.

extraExamples.en: The restaurant looks the same as I remembered.
extraExamples.zh: 這間餐廳看起來跟我記得的一樣。

extraExamples.en: Your apartment is exactly as I imagined.
extraExamples.zh: 你的公寓跟我想像的一模一樣。

sentences.en: I’ll take that as a compliment! You’re ready for some good Chinese food? It has a 4.4 stars on Google so you know, if it’s bad, it’s on Google, not me.
sentences.zh: 這我就當是讚美了！妳準備好來點好吃的中餐了嗎？這間Google評價有4.4顆星喔，所以如果不好吃，怪Google不怪我。

back.grammar: 「I’ll take that as...」表示「我就把那當作……」；「it’s on someone/something」口語指「責任在……」。
variations: I’m gonna take that as a compliment.
Ready for some solid Chinese food?
If it sucks, blame Google, not me.

keyPhrases.phrase: ready for some good Chinese food
keyPhrases.zh: 準備好吃點好吃的中餐

extraExamples.en: You called me organized? I’ll take that as a compliment.
extraExamples.zh: 你說我很有條理？我就當作你在誇我。

extraExamples.en: If this place is terrible, it’s on my coworker, not me.
extraExamples.zh: 如果這地方很雷，怪我同事，不要怪我。

sentences.en: Don’t worry. Let’s see what we’ve got here. Hmm I think I’m ready to order.
sentences.zh: 別擔心，我們來看看有什麼好吃的。我應該準備點餐了。

back.grammar: 「Let’s see what we’ve got」是口語說「來看看有什麼」；「I’m ready to order」表示準備好點餐。
variations: No worries. Let’s check out the menu.
Let’s see what they have here.
I think I know what I want.

extraExamples.en: Let’s see what we’ve got in the fridge.
extraExamples.zh: 我們來看看冰箱裡有什麼。

extraExamples.en: I’m ready to order whenever you are.
extraExamples.zh: 你準備好了的話，我也可以點餐了。

sentences.en: Speedy, I like you already! Excuse me. We’re ready to order! I’ll get some chicken potstickers to start and a coke.
sentences.zh: 這麼快，我喜歡！不好意思，我們準備好點餐了！我想先來點雞肉鍋貼，再加一杯可樂。

back.grammar: 「I’ll get...」是點餐時很自然的說法；「to start」表示「先來……當開胃/第一道」。
variations: That was fast—I like that.
Excuse me, we’re ready.
I’ll start with the chicken potstickers and a Coke.

keyPhrases.zh: 我已經喜歡你了／你很對我胃口

extraExamples.en: I’ll get the burger and fries, please.
extraExamples.zh: 我要漢堡和薯條，謝謝。

extraExamples.en: Can we get the calamari to start?
extraExamples.zh: 我們可以先來一份炸花枝嗎？

sentences.en: I’ll get some ice tea and spring rolls.
sentences.zh: 那我要一杯冰茶和春捲。

back.grammar: 點餐用「I’ll get...」比「I want...」更自然、有禮貌。
variations: I’ll have an iced tea and spring rolls.
Can I get an iced tea and spring rolls?
I’m gonna do the iced tea and spring rolls.

extraExamples.en: I’ll get a latte and a blueberry muffin.
extraExamples.zh: 我要一杯拿鐵和一個藍莓馬芬。

extraExamples.en: Can I get a water with no ice?
extraExamples.zh: 可以給我一杯去冰的水嗎？

sentences.en: Alright… so where do we start?
sentences.zh: 好喔…那我們從哪開始聊？

back.grammar: 「Where do we start?」可用來開啟話題，意思是「我們從哪裡開始聊？」
variations: So, what should we talk about first?
Where should we begin?
Alright, first question—where do we start?

extraExamples.en: There’s so much to catch up on—where do we start?
extraExamples.zh: 好多事要聊，我們從哪開始？

extraExamples.en: This project is huge. Where do we start?
extraExamples.zh: 這個專案很大，我們從哪裡開始？

sentences.en: So you said you’re doing grad school at USC?
sentences.zh: 所以你說你在USC讀研究所嗎？

back.grammar: 「You said you’re...」用來確認對方之前提到的資訊；「doing grad school」是口語說「讀研究所」。
variations: You mentioned you’re in grad school at USC, right?
So you’re doing your master’s at USC?
Wait, you said USC for grad school?

extraExamples.en: You said you’re working downtown now?
extraExamples.zh: 你說你現在在市中心工作嗎？

extraExamples.en: She’s doing grad school in Boston.
extraExamples.zh: 她在波士頓讀研究所。

sentences.en: Yeah, neuroscience program. I hope I’m not too nerdy for wanting to talk about neurons all day.
sentences.zh: 對啊，神經科學的課程。我希望我講神經元講太多不會讓妳覺得我太書呆子…

back.grammar: 「I hope I’m not too... for...」表示擔心自己因為某件事太……；「all day」誇張表示「一直」。
variations: Yeah, I’m in neuroscience.
Hope I don’t sound too nerdy talking about neurons all the time.
I promise I won’t make the whole night about brain cells.

extraExamples.en: I hope I’m not too boring for talking about work so much.
extraExamples.zh: 我一直聊工作，希望不會太無聊。

extraExamples.en: My brother could talk about basketball all day.
extraExamples.zh: 我哥可以一整天都聊籃球。

sentences.en: No that’s totally cool! So does that make you an expert mind reader? If so I need to get prepared…
sentences.zh: 不會啊，這超酷的！那你會讀心術嗎？如果是的話，我得先準備一下…

back.grammar: 「Does that make you...?」表示「那是不是代表你是……？」；「If so」是「如果是這樣的話」。
variations: That’s actually really cool.
So does that mean you can read minds?
If that’s the case, I’d better prepare myself.

extraExamples.en: You bake every weekend? Does that make you the dessert expert?
extraExamples.zh: 你每週末都烘焙？那你就是甜點專家嗎？

extraExamples.en: If so, we should leave earlier.
extraExamples.zh: 如果是這樣的話，我們應該早點出發。

sentences.en: I wish haha. But seriously, what’s your favorite karaoke song? This is how I judge people.
