sentences.zh: 我希望是啦，哈哈。可是講真的，妳最愛的KTV歌曲是什麼？這是我判斷一個人的標準喔。

back.grammar: 「I wish」單獨使用表示「我也希望是這樣」；「This is how I...」表示「我就是這樣……的」。
variations: I wish I could.
For real though, what’s your go-to karaoke song?
That’s my way of judging someone’s character.

extraExamples.en: I wish. I can’t afford a vacation right now.
extraExamples.zh: 我也希望。但我現在負擔不起旅行。

extraExamples.en: What’s your go-to comfort food?
extraExamples.zh: 你最常吃的療癒食物是什麼？

sentences.en: Um… you wouldn’t want to hear me sing at karaoke. The mic would be all yours.
sentences.zh: 嗯…你可能不想聽我唱K，你可以霸佔麥克風。

back.grammar: 「wouldn’t want to」表示「不會想要……」，語氣比直接說不要更委婉；「all yours」指「都歸你」。
variations: Trust me, you don’t want to hear me sing.
You can have the mic the whole night.
I’ll leave the singing to you.

keyPhrases.phrase: wouldn’t want to hear me sing
The mic would be all yours

extraExamples.en: You wouldn’t want to see me dance.
extraExamples.zh: 你不會想看我跳舞的。

extraExamples.en: The last slice of pizza is all yours.
extraExamples.zh: 最後一片披薩給你吃。

sentences.en: What? You have a nice voice though. I can’t sing either.
sentences.zh: 什麼？妳聲音很好聽啊！我也唱不好。

back.grammar: 「though」放句尾可表示補充或轉折，意思接近「不過／其實」；「either」用在否定句表示「也不」。
variations: Really? Your voice is nice, though.
Come on, you sound good.
Don’t worry, I can’t sing either.

extraExamples.en: The place is small, but it’s cozy though.
extraExamples.zh: 這地方很小，不過其實很溫馨。

extraExamples.en: I don’t know how to cook either.
extraExamples.zh: 我也不會煮飯。

sentences.en: Thanks and thank goodness? Why did you even ask then? What about some embarrassing stories you weren’t planning to tell me tonight?
sentences.zh: 謝謝，幸好嗎？那為什麼還問我這個？那有沒有什麼丟臉的故事，是你今天晚上不打算告訴我的？

back.grammar: 「Why did you even…?」用 even 加強語氣，表示覺得對方的行為很矛盾或沒必要。
variations: Then why’d you ask in the first place?
So why even bring it up?
Got any embarrassing stories you were gonna keep from me tonight?

extraExamples.en: If you didn’t want my opinion, why did you even ask?
extraExamples.zh: 如果你不想聽我的意見，那你到底為什麼要問？

extraExamples.en: I wasn’t planning to tell anyone, but I accidentally blurted it out.
extraExamples.zh: 我原本不打算告訴任何人，但我不小心脫口而出。

sentences.en: Well… I almost canceled tonight because my cat really seemed to need emotional support... but I'm glad I came out because look at you…
sentences.zh: 嗯…我差點取消今晚的約會，因為我家貓真的看起來很需要我陪…但我很高興我還是出來了，因為妳…真不錯。

back.grammar: 「almost + 過去式」表示「差點做某事但最後沒做」；came out 在約會語境中可指「出門赴約」。
variations: I nearly bailed tonight because my cat looked like he needed me.
I was this close to canceling, but I’m really glad I showed up.
I almost stayed home with my cat, but honestly, I’m happy I came.

extraExamples.en: I almost canceled the dinner because I was so tired.
extraExamples.zh: 我因為太累差點取消晚餐約。

extraExamples.en: I’m glad I came out tonight; this place is really fun.
extraExamples.zh: 我很高興今晚有出來，這地方真的很好玩。

sentences.en: Shut up! You have a cat too? I got two!
sentences.zh: 閉嘴啦！妳也有貓？我有兩隻！

back.grammar: 口語中的「Shut up!」不一定是真的叫人閉嘴，也可表示驚訝：「真的假的！」
variations: No way! You have a cat too? I have two!
Seriously? You’re a cat person too? I’ve got two!
Get out! You have a cat too? I’ve got a pair.

keyPhrases.zh: 真的假的！少來！（驚訝用法）
我有兩隻（口語中 got = have）

extraExamples.en: Shut up! You went to the same high school as me?
extraExamples.zh: 真的假的！你跟我讀同一間高中？

extraExamples.en: I got three plants, and I still want more.
extraExamples.zh: 我有三盆植物，而且還想再買。

sentences.en: You do? Mine’s called Mochi. He’s a tuxedo. You should come and see him later!
sentences.zh: 妳有嗎？我家的貓叫Mochi，他是穿燕尾服的。妳應該來看看他！

back.grammar: 「Mine’s called…」是口語說法，意思是「我的叫…」；tuxedo 指黑白相間像穿燕尾服的貓。
variations: Really? My cat’s name is Mochi. He’s a tuxedo cat.
No way. Mine is Mochi—he’s black and white.
You should come meet him sometime.

extraExamples.en: Mine’s called Peanut, and she loves sleeping on my laptop.
extraExamples.zh: 我的叫 Peanut，她超愛睡在我的筆電上。

extraExamples.en: You should come and see my new apartment sometime.
extraExamples.zh: 你哪天應該來看看我的新公寓。

sentences.en: Mochi? What a name! I got two- this is Bento and this is Bella.
sentences.zh: Mochi？這名字好可愛！我有兩隻，這隻是Bento，那隻是Bella。

back.grammar: 「What a + 名詞!」是感嘆句，用來表示驚訝、稱讚或覺得有趣。
variations: Mochi? That’s such a cute name!
Mochi? I love that name!
I’ve got two—this one’s Bento, and that one’s Bella.

keyPhrases.zh: 這名字真有意思／真可愛！

extraExamples.en: What a view! You can see the whole city from here.
extraExamples.zh: 這景色太棒了！從這裡可以看到整座城市。

extraExamples.en: I’ve got two roommates—this is Jason, and that’s Mark.
extraExamples.zh: 我有兩個室友，這位是 Jason，那位是 Mark。

sentences.en: They are majestic! They should be cat models. And you should be a model too because wow look at you…you’re from Tai…Taiwan you said?
sentences.zh: 哇，牠們好威啊！牠們應該當貓模特兒！妳也應該當模特兒，妳看妳…妳是從台…台灣來的對吧？

back.grammar: 「should be」用來表示建議或誇張稱讚：「應該去當…」；look at you 是口語稱讚語氣。
variations: They’re gorgeous! They could totally be cat models.
Those cats are stunning. They belong in a magazine.
And honestly, you could be a model too—look at you.

keyPhrases.zh: 看看你，多厲害／多好看啊

extraExamples.en: Your dog is majestic. He should be in a commercial.
extraExamples.zh: 你的狗好威風，牠應該去拍廣告。

extraExamples.en: Look at you, all dressed up for the party!
extraExamples.zh: 看看你，為派對打扮得這麼漂亮！

sentences.en: Yup. I moved here from Taiwan five years ago but I was born and raised there.
sentences.zh: 對啊，我五年前從台灣搬來這裡，但我是在那裡出生長大的。

back.grammar: 「moved here from… + 時間 ago」表示多久前從某地搬來；「born and raised」是固定片語。
variations: Yeah. I came here from Taiwan five years ago, but I grew up there.
Yep. I’ve been here five years, but I was born and raised in Taiwan.
I moved from Taiwan five years back, but Taiwan is where I grew up.

extraExamples.en: I moved here from Seattle two years ago.
extraExamples.zh: 我兩年前從西雅圖搬來這裡。

extraExamples.en: She was born and raised in Tainan.
extraExamples.zh: 她是在台南出生長大的。

sentences.en: Ni hao! wo yeah hwei shou i dien chung wen.
sentences.zh: 妳好，我也會說一點中文。

back.grammar: 「I can speak a little…」是表達語言能力的常用句；這句故意用拼音呈現中文發音。
variations: Ni hao! I can speak a little Chinese too.
Hey, I know a little Mandarin.
I speak a tiny bit of Chinese.

extraExamples.en: I can speak a little Spanish, but I’m still learning.
extraExamples.zh: 我會說一點西班牙文，但還在學。

extraExamples.en: She knows a tiny bit of Japanese from watching anime.
extraExamples.zh: 她因為看動畫會一點點日文。

sentences.en: Wow! Impressive!
sentences.zh: 哇！很厲害耶！

back.grammar: 「Impressive!」是口語稱讚，意思是「很厲害、令人佩服」。
variations: Wow, that’s impressive!
Nice! I’m impressed.
That’s actually pretty impressive.

extraExamples.en: You made this cake yourself? Impressive!
extraExamples.zh: 這蛋糕是你自己做的？很厲害耶！

extraExamples.en: You finished the whole project in one day? I’m impressed.
extraExamples.zh: 你一天就完成整個專案？我很佩服。

sentences.en: Yeah I have been learning Chinese so I can impress my ex’s parents. She was from China but then I found out she was crazy after a year. But anyways, I think I can impress your parents too!
sentences.zh: 對啊，我一直在學中文，這樣可以讓我給我前女友的爸媽留下好印象。她是中國人，不過我發現她有點瘋，交往一年後就分手了。但無論如何，我覺得我也可以給妳爸媽留下好印象！

back.grammar: 「have been + V-ing」是現在完成進行式，表示從過去到現在一直在做某事。
variations: I’ve been studying Chinese to win over my ex’s parents.
I started learning Chinese because I wanted to impress my ex’s family.
Anyway, I bet I could impress your parents too.

extraExamples.en: I’ve been learning guitar so I can play at my friend’s wedding.
extraExamples.zh: 我一直在學吉他，這樣就能在朋友婚禮上表演。

extraExamples.en: I found out my coworker lives in the same building as me.
extraExamples.zh: 我發現我的同事跟我住同一棟。

sentences.en: Woah, we’re there already? What? Haha…
sentences.zh: 哇，我們聊到這了嗎？什麼？哈哈…

back.grammar: 「We’re there already?」口語意思是「我們已經聊到那一步了嗎？」there 代指某個進展或程度。
variations: Whoa, we’re already at that stage?
Wait, are we there already?
Wow, moving fast, huh?

keyPhrases.zh: 我們已經到那個階段了嗎

extraExamples.en: We just met, and you’re talking about moving in? We’re there already?
extraExamples.zh: 我們才剛認識，你就在聊同居？已經到那一步了嗎？

extraExamples.en: Whoa, moving fast—you already booked a trip together?
extraExamples.zh: 哇，進展很快耶，你們已經訂好一起旅行了？

sentences.en: Yeah, I just really like Chinese food and Asian culture. And I think Taiwanese girls are super hot. All my exes are Asians!
sentences.zh: 對啊，我真的很喜歡中餐和亞洲文化。我覺得台灣女孩超正的，我所有的前女友都是亞洲人！

back.grammar: 「super + 形容詞」是美式口語加強語氣；「All my exes are…」表示過去交往對象的共同特徵。
variations: I’m really into Chinese food and Asian culture.
I think Taiwanese girls are really attractive.
Honestly, all the people I’ve dated have been Asian.

extraExamples.en: I’m really into Korean food these days.
extraExamples.zh: 我最近真的很迷韓國料理。

extraExamples.en: All my exes lived in the same neighborhood, weirdly enough.
extraExamples.zh: 很奇怪的是，我所有前任都住在同一個區域。

sentences.en: Ah…
sentences.zh: 啊…

back.grammar: 「Ah…」在對話中常用來表示尷尬、遲疑、無言或不知道怎麼回。
variations: Oh…
Uh… okay.
Right…

keyPhrases.zh: 喔…好喔（有點尷尬或敷衍）

extraExamples.en: Ah… I didn’t know you felt that way.
extraExamples.zh: 啊…我不知道你是那樣想的。

extraExamples.en: Uh… okay, that’s kind of a lot to take in.
extraExamples.zh: 呃…好，這資訊量有點大。

sentences.en: So what other Taiwanese things about you should I know? Do you guys also have to live with your parents even after you’re married?
sentences.zh: 那還有什麼台灣的事情是我應該知道的？你們結婚後還是得跟爸媽住嗎？

back.grammar: 「What other… should I know?」用來詢問還有哪些相關資訊；「have to」表示必須。
variations: What else should I know about being Taiwanese?
Are there any other Taiwanese customs I should know about?
Do people in Taiwan usually live with their parents after getting married?

keyPhrases.phrase: What other things should I know?
keyPhrases.zh: 還有什麼我應該知道的事？

extraExamples.en: What other rules should I know before I start this job?
extraExamples.zh: 我開始這份工作前，還有什麼規則應該知道？

extraExamples.en: Do we have to dress up even if it’s just a small party?
extraExamples.zh: 就算只是小派對，我們也得穿正式一點嗎？

sentences.en: Um…
sentences.zh: 嗯…

back.grammar: 「Um…」是填補停頓的語助詞，表示正在思考、尷尬或不知道怎麼回答。
variations: Uh…
Well…
Hmm…

extraExamples.en: Um… I’m not sure how to answer that.
extraExamples.zh: 嗯…我不太確定要怎麼回答那個。

extraExamples.en: Well… that’s a little personal.
extraExamples.zh: 這個嘛…那有點私人。

sentences.en: Because I just want to put it out there so you know, alright, Cheryl? I am very open-minded and I think that is not a problem.
sentences.zh: 因為我只是想告訴妳，Cheryl，我是個開放的心態，我覺得這應該不算問題。

back.grammar: 「put it out there」是口語片語，意思是先把話說出來、表明立場；「so you know」用來表示『讓你知道一下』。
variations: I just wanted to say it upfront so you know, Cheryl.
Just putting it out there—I’m really open-minded, and I don’t think that’s an issue.
I figured I should mention it: I’m pretty open-minded, so that’s not a problem for me.

keyPhrases.zh: 思想開放的；願意接受不同想法的

extraExamples.en: I’m just putting it out there: I’m not looking for anything serious right now.
extraExamples.zh: 我只是先說清楚：我現在不想找太認真的關係。

extraExamples.en: She’s very open-minded about trying new food.
extraExamples.zh: 她對嘗試新食物很開放。

sentences.en: Oh um… that’s good to know I guess? Look, I had fun but I have to meet up with my friends in 30 minutes for a band performance, we’ll split the check?
sentences.zh: 喔，嗯…這樣知道了算是好嗎？聽著，我真的很開心，不過我30分鐘後要和朋友們見面，看他們的樂團表演，我們來各付各的吧？

back.grammar: 「I had fun but I have to...」是約會或社交場合常見的禮貌收尾；「meet up with」表示和某人碰面；「split the check」是各付各的。
variations: That’s good to know, I guess. I had a nice time, but I’m meeting my friends in half an hour. Want to split the bill?
Okay… thanks for telling me. I had fun, but I’ve gotta meet up with my friends soon. Let’s just split the check.
Uh, good to know. I had fun, but I need to head out in 30 minutes for my friends’ show. We can go Dutch, right?

extraExamples.en: I’m meeting up with Kevin after work for dinner.
extraExamples.zh: 我下班後要和 Kevin 碰面吃晚餐。

extraExamples.en: We split the check because neither of us wanted it to feel like a date.
extraExamples.zh: 我們各付各的，因為我們都不想讓它感覺像約會。

sentences.en: Alright. I promise I can be more interesting next time!
sentences.zh: 好啊，我保證下次我會更有趣！

back.grammar: 「I promise I can...」用來保證自己做得到；「more interesting next time」表示下次會更有趣、更會聊天。
variations: Okay. I swear I’ll be more fun next time!
Alright, I’ll do better next time—I promise.
Got it. Next time, I’ll be way more entertaining!

extraExamples.en: I promise I’ll text you when I get home.
extraExamples.zh: 我保證到家後會傳訊息給你。

extraExamples.en: Next time, we should pick a quieter restaurant.
extraExamples.zh: 下次我們應該選一家安靜一點的餐廳。

sentences.en: Enjoy your night!
sentences.zh: 祝你今晚愉快！

back.grammar: 「Enjoy your night!」是自然口語的告別祝福，常用於晚上分開時，語氣友善但也可帶有結束對話的感覺。
variations: Have a good night!
Hope you have a great night!
Enjoy the rest of your night!

extraExamples.en: Thanks for coming out. Enjoy the rest of your night!
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝你出來。祝你今晚接下來愉快！

extraExamples.en: I’m heading home now. Have a good night!
extraExamples.zh: 我現在要回家了。祝你晚上愉快！

units.title: 生活分享、社交邀約與拒絕
units.scenario: Oliver跟室友的週五閒聊

sentences.en: How was school?
sentences.zh: 今天上課如何？

back.grammar: 「How was + 名詞？」是很自然的寒暄問法，用來問某件事過得如何。
variations: How was your day at school?
How’d school go?
How was class today?

extraExamples.en: How was work today?
extraExamples.zh: 今天上班如何？

extraExamples.en: How was the movie last night?
extraExamples.zh: 昨晚那部電影怎麼樣？

sentences.en: It’s been alright. I had a late lunch because I was in a meeting with my professor.
sentences.zh: 還好啦。今天午餐吃得有點晚，因為我和教授開會。

back.grammar: 「It’s been alright」用現在完成式描述到目前為止的狀況；「had a late lunch」表示午餐吃得比較晚。
variations: It’s been okay. I ate lunch late because I had a meeting with my professor.
It was alright. I had lunch pretty late since I was meeting with my professor.
Not bad. I didn’t eat lunch until later because I was in a meeting with my professor.

keyPhrases.zh: 到目前為止還可以／還好

extraExamples.en: It’s been a busy week, but I’m doing okay.
extraExamples.zh: 這週一直很忙，但我還可以。

extraExamples.en: I had a late dinner because I got stuck at work.
extraExamples.zh: 我晚餐吃得很晚，因為被工作卡住了。

sentences.en: Oh how did the meeting go?
sentences.zh: 喔，會議還好嗎？

back.grammar: 「How did ... go?」是問活動、會議、考試等結果如何的常用口語句型。
variations: How’d the meeting go?
How was the meeting?
Did the meeting go okay?

extraExamples.en: How did your interview go?
extraExamples.zh: 你的面試如何？

extraExamples.en: How did the presentation go this morning?
extraExamples.zh: 今天早上的簡報還順利嗎？

sentences.en: I finally handed in my essays and the professor liked what I wrote so far. He said he’ll meet with me next week to go over them.
sentences.zh: 我終於交了我的論文，教授覺得我寫得不錯。他說下週會和我再見面討論。

back.grammar: 「hand in」是交作業的片語動詞；「so far」表示到目前為止；「go over」表示一起檢查或討論。
variations: I finally turned in my essays, and my professor liked what I have so far.
I finally submitted my essays, and he said the draft looks good so far.
I got my essays turned in, and the professor wants to go over them with me next week.

extraExamples.en: I need to hand in my homework before midnight.
extraExamples.zh: 我必須在午夜前交作業。

extraExamples.en: Can we go over the plan one more time?
extraExamples.zh: 我們可以再討論一次這個計畫嗎？

sentences.en: That’s a great start! You’ve been working really hard on this.
sentences.zh: 太棒了！你真的花了很多精力在這上面。

back.grammar: 「You’ve been working」是現在完成進行式，強調從過去到現在一直持續努力。
variations: That’s a really good start. You’ve put a lot of work into this.
That’s awesome. You’ve been working so hard on it.
Sounds like you’re off to a great start. You’ve really been putting in the effort.

keyPhrases.phrase: put a lot of work into

extraExamples.en: You’ve been working really hard on your English lately.
extraExamples.zh: 你最近真的很努力學英文。

extraExamples.en: This is a great start, but we still need to fix a few details.
extraExamples.zh: 這是很好的開始，但我們還需要修幾個細節。

sentences.en: Yeah, I know. And I didn’t get to talk to Sally in class today about our travel plans during the winter break because she went on a vacation.
sentences.zh: 對啊。而且今天在上課時我沒有和Sally聊到我們寒假旅行計畫，因為她去度假了。

back.grammar: 「didn’t get to + V」表示沒機會做某事；「go on a vacation」表示去度假。
variations: I didn’t get a chance to talk to Sally about our winter break trip because she’s on vacation.
I couldn’t talk to Sally in class today about our travel plans since she went away on vacation.
I never got to ask Sally about the trip because she’s out on vacation.

extraExamples.en: I didn’t get to call my mom yesterday because I was too busy.
extraExamples.zh: 我昨天太忙，沒機會打給我媽。

extraExamples.en: We’re trying to make travel plans for spring break.
extraExamples.zh: 我們正在規劃春假的旅行。

sentences.en: You still got a few weeks to figure that out.
sentences.zh: 還有幾個星期可以決定一下。

back.grammar: 口語中「You still got」常省略 have，完整是「You still have got / You still have」；「figure out」表示想出、弄清楚。
variations: You still have a few weeks to figure it out.
You’ve still got some time to decide.
There’s still a few weeks left to work it out.

keyPhrases.zh: 把那件事弄清楚／決定好

extraExamples.en: Don’t stress. You still have time to figure it out.
extraExamples.zh: 別太緊張，你還有時間弄清楚。

extraExamples.en: We need to figure out where to eat tonight.
extraExamples.zh: 我們得決定今晚要去哪裡吃。

sentences.en: Yeah, I guess you’re right. I just want to sort things out sooner.
sentences.zh: 對，我知道，我就是想快點弄好。

back.grammar: 「I guess you’re right」是口語中表示勉強同意；「sort things out」表示把事情整理好、處理好。
variations: Yeah, you’re probably right. I just want to get everything sorted out soon.
I guess so. I just want to deal with it sooner rather than later.
Yeah, true. I just don’t want to leave it until the last minute.

extraExamples.en: I guess you’re right. I should talk to her first.
extraExamples.zh: 我想你說得對。我應該先跟她談談。

extraExamples.en: I need to sort things out with my roommate this weekend.
extraExamples.zh: 我這週末需要跟室友把事情談清楚。

sentences.en: You will be okay. What did you have for lunch by the way?
sentences.zh: 你會沒問題的。對了，你今天午餐吃什麼？

back.grammar: 「by the way」用來轉換話題，中文常說「對了」。
variations: You’ll be fine. What’d you eat for lunch, by the way?
Don’t worry, you’ll be okay. By the way, what did you have for lunch?
You’re gonna be fine. Oh, what was for lunch today?

extraExamples.en: By the way, did you text Jason back?
extraExamples.zh: 對了，你有回 Jason 訊息嗎？

extraExamples.en: What did you have for dinner last night?
extraExamples.zh: 你昨晚晚餐吃了什麼？

sentences.en: I took the leftovers to school today to save some money and got some Chinese takeout for dinner on the way home. The train ran late so it took me extra long to get back.
sentences.zh: 我今天帶了剩菜去學校，省點錢。回家的時候順便買了點中式外賣當晚餐。可是火車誤點了，所以回家花了特別久。

back.grammar: 「to save some money」是不定詞表示目的；「run late」表示交通工具或行程誤點；「take extra long」表示花特別久。
variations: I brought leftovers to school to save money and picked up Chinese takeout on my way home.
I took some leftovers for lunch, then grabbed Chinese food on the way back.
The train was delayed, so it took forever to get home.

extraExamples.en: I brought coffee from home to save some money.
extraExamples.zh: 我從家裡帶咖啡，想省點錢。

extraExamples.en: My bus ran late, so I missed the beginning of the movie.
extraExamples.zh: 我的公車誤點，所以我錯過電影開頭。

sentences.en: Ugh… talk about saving money! I ran out of foundation and conditioner so I went to CVS to grab some. It was $50 just to get these two items.
sentences.zh: 唉…說到省錢！我今天買了粉底和護髮，結果光這兩樣就花了50塊。

back.grammar: 「Talk about ...!」是口語感嘆，意思像「說到……還真是！」；「run out of」表示用完。
variations: Ugh, speaking of saving money, I just spent $50 at CVS on foundation and conditioner.
Seriously, so much for saving money. I ran out of foundation and conditioner and had to buy more.
Ugh, I went to CVS for foundation and conditioner, and just those two things were fifty bucks.

extraExamples.en: I ran out of shampoo, so I need to stop by Target.
extraExamples.zh: 我的洗髮精用完了，所以我得去 Target 一趟。

extraExamples.en: Talk about bad timing—the elevator broke right when we were moving.
extraExamples.zh: 這時間點也太糟了吧——我們搬家的時候電梯剛好壞掉。

sentences.en: I know. I don’t like it when my makeup stuff runs out either. Oh I wanted to ask if you have plans for this weekend?
sentences.zh: 我知道，我也不喜歡化妝品用完的時候。對了，我想問妳這個週末有什麼計畫嗎？

back.grammar: 「I don’t like it when ...」是常見句型，用來說不喜歡某種情況；「I wanted to ask if ...」是較委婉的問法。
variations: Same. I hate when my makeup runs out too. Oh, do you have plans this weekend?
I know, it’s annoying when makeup runs out. By the way, are you doing anything this weekend?
Yeah, I hate that too. I was wondering if you’re busy this weekend.

extraExamples.en: I don’t like it when my phone dies in the middle of the day.
extraExamples.zh: 我不喜歡手機在一天中途沒電。

extraExamples.en: Do you have plans for Friday night?
extraExamples.zh: 你星期五晚上有安排嗎？

sentences.en: Oh! Why?
sentences.zh: 哦！為什麼？

back.grammar: 「Why?」單獨使用很口語；加上「Oh!」表示驚訝或好奇。
variations: Oh yeah? Why?
Oh! What’s up?
Oh? How come?

extraExamples.en: Oh? How come you’re leaving so early?
extraExamples.zh: 喔？你為什麼這麼早走？

extraExamples.en: What’s up? You look excited.
extraExamples.zh: 怎麼了？你看起來很興奮。

sentences.en: We’re going to the beach tomorrow. If you’re free, you should come hang with us!
sentences.zh: 我們明天要去海灘。如果你有空的話，你可以來和我們一起玩！

back.grammar: 「come hang with us」是非常口語的說法，意思是來跟我們一起玩／混；「If you’re free」表示如果你有空。
variations: We’re heading to the beach tomorrow. You should come with us if you’re free.
We’re going to the beach tomorrow—come hang out if you can.
A few of us are going to the beach tomorrow. You should join us!

extraExamples.en: If you’re free tonight, come hang out with us.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你今晚有空，來跟我們一起玩。

extraExamples.en: We’re going to the mall later. You should join us.
extraExamples.zh: 我們晚點要去購物中心。你應該一起來。

sentences.en: I would love to but I have to get this other essay turned in tomorrow so I’m going to have to take a rain check this time.
sentences.zh: 我很樂意，但我明天必須交另一篇論文，所以這次我必須拒絕了。

back.grammar: 「I would love to, but ...」是禮貌拒絕邀約；「take a rain check」表示這次先婉拒、下次再約；「get something turned in」表示把某物交出去。
variations: I’d love to, but I have another essay due tomorrow, so I’ll have to pass this time.
I wish I could, but I need to turn in another essay tomorrow. Can I take a rain check?
That sounds fun, but I’ve gotta finish and submit another essay tomorrow, so maybe next time.

extraExamples.en: I’d love to go to dinner, but I have to work late tonight.
extraExamples.zh: 我很想去吃晚餐，但我今晚得加班。

extraExamples.en: Can I take a rain check? I’m not feeling well today.
extraExamples.zh: 可以下次再約嗎？我今天不太舒服。

sentences.en: No worries! Also my sister is visiting next weekend. Would you want to join us for a play next Sunday?
sentences.zh: 不用擔心！我姐姐下週末也來拜訪。下週日你想跟我們一起看戲嗎？

back.grammar: 「is visiting」用現在進行式表示已安排好的近期未來行程；「Would you want to...?」是委婉邀請。
variations: No problem! My sister’s coming next weekend too. Wanna come see a play with us next Sunday?
Don’t worry about it! My sister will be in town next weekend. Do you want to join us for a show next Sunday?
All good! My sister’s visiting next weekend as well. Would you like to come with us to a play next Sunday?

extraExamples.en: No worries! We can just meet up another time.
extraExamples.zh: 不用擔心！我們可以改天再約。

extraExamples.en: My cousin is visiting next month, so I’m planning a small dinner.
extraExamples.zh: 我表哥下個月要來，所以我在安排一個小聚餐。

sentences.en: I wish I could join but I have to take care of a few things that day.
sentences.zh: 我希望我能加入，但那天我必須處理一些事情。

back.grammar: 「I wish I could...」表示可惜自己做不到；「have to」表示不得不、必須。
variations: I’d love to, but I’ve got a few things to deal with that day.
I wish I could make it, but I have some stuff to take care of then.
Sounds fun, but I can’t. I’ve got a couple things going on that day.

keyPhrases.zh: 我希望我可以，但實際上不行

keyPhrases.phrase: take care of a few things

extraExamples.en: I wish I could go to dinner, but I have to work late tonight.
extraExamples.zh: 我希望我能去吃晚餐，但我今晚得加班。

extraExamples.en: I need to take care of a few things before I leave for the airport.
extraExamples.zh: 我去機場前需要先處理一些事情。

sentences.en: Busy you! Alright, guess I’ll ask Jesicca if she can come then!
sentences.zh: 忙著呢！好吧，我想我會問傑西卡她是否可以來！

back.grammar: 「Busy you!」是口語打趣說法，意思像「你真忙！」；「I’ll ask... if...」表示我會問某人是否可以。
variations: Look at you, so busy! Okay, I’ll ask Jessica if she wants to come.
You’re busy, huh? Alright, I’ll see if Jessica can make it then.
Busy bee! No problem, I’ll ask Jessica if she’s free.

extraExamples.en: Alright, I’ll ask Mark if he can drive us there.
extraExamples.zh: 好吧，我會問 Mark 能不能開車載我們去。

extraExamples.en: You’re busy, huh? Let’s catch up next week instead.
extraExamples.zh: 你很忙喔？那我們改下週再聊吧。

sentences.en: Alright!
sentences.zh: 好吧！

back.grammar: 「Alright!」是常見口語回應，可表示同意、接受或結束話題，語氣比正式的「Okay」更隨興。
variations: Okay!
Sounds good!
Cool!

extraExamples.en: Alright, I’ll see you tomorrow morning.
extraExamples.zh: 好，那我明天早上見你。

extraExamples.en: Alright! Let me know what she says.
extraExamples.zh: 好！她怎麼說再告訴我。

units.title: 工作與面試
units.scenario: Ian去面試

sentences.en: Hi! Ian?
sentences.zh: 嗨！Ian嗎？

back.grammar: 用名字加升調可以表示確認對方身分，口語中很常見。
variations: Hi, are you Ian?
Ian? Hi, I’m Susan.
Hey, you must be Ian.

extraExamples.en: Hi! Mark? I’m here for the 2 p.m. interview.
extraExamples.zh: 嗨！是 Mark 嗎？我是來參加兩點面試的。

extraExamples.en: Jessica? Nice to meet you in person.
extraExamples.zh: Jessica 嗎？很高興終於見到本人。

sentences.en: Yes, you must be Susan!
sentences.zh: 對，你是Susan吧！

back.grammar: must be + 人名 用來推測對方身分，語氣自然像「你一定是……吧」。
variations: Yes, you’re Susan, right?
That’s me. You must be Susan.
Yep! I’m guessing you’re Susan.

extraExamples.en: You must be the new manager. Welcome!
extraExamples.zh: 你一定是新經理吧。歡迎！

extraExamples.en: You must be here for the interview.
extraExamples.zh: 你一定是來面試的吧。

sentences.en: Yes! Nice to finally meet you! First, we’ll learn more about your professional experience and the responsibilities of this position.
sentences.zh: 對呀！很高興終於見到你！首先，我們會更了解你的專業經歷以及這個職位的責任。.

back.grammar: Nice to finally meet you 表示「終於見到你很高興」；we’ll 用來說明接下來的流程。
variations: Great to finally meet you! We’ll start by going over your experience and the role.
Nice to meet you at last! First, let’s talk about your background and what this job involves.
I’m glad we finally get to meet. We’ll begin with your experience and the position’s responsibilities.

extraExamples.en: Nice to finally meet you after all our emails.
extraExamples.zh: 我們信件往來這麼久，終於見到你很高興。

extraExamples.en: First, we’ll learn more about your goals for this project.
extraExamples.zh: 首先，我們會更了解你對這個專案的目標。

sentences.en: Sounds good.
sentences.zh: 聽起來不錯。

back.grammar: Sounds good 是非常常用的口語回應，表示同意或覺得安排可以。
variations: That works for me.
Sounds great.
Sure, that’s fine.

extraExamples.en: Let’s meet at 10 tomorrow. Sounds good?
extraExamples.zh: 我們明天十點見。可以嗎？

extraExamples.en: Sounds good. I’ll send you the file tonight.
extraExamples.zh: 可以。我今晚會把檔案寄給你。

sentences.en: So you applied to the marketing representative role with us. Can you briefly tell me more about yourself?
sentences.zh: 你申請了我們的行銷代表職位，可以簡單介紹一下自己嗎？

back.grammar: applied to the role 表示「申請某職位」；Can you briefly tell me… 是面試中請人簡短自我介紹的自然問法。
variations: So you applied for our marketing representative position. Could you tell me a little about yourself?
I see you applied for the marketing rep role. Can you give me a quick overview of your background?
You’re here for the marketing representative opening. Could you briefly walk me through your background?

keyPhrases.phrase: briefly tell me more about yourself

extraExamples.en: I applied to the customer service role last week.
extraExamples.zh: 我上週申請了客服職位。

extraExamples.en: Could you briefly tell me more about your experience with social media?
extraExamples.zh: 你可以簡單說明一下你在社群媒體方面的經驗嗎？

sentences.en: For sure. My name is Ian and in the past 3 years, I have worked as a marketing manager for this electronics company that mainly sells baby monitors and the software subscription that goes along with it. My main responsibilities include monitoring paid ad campaigns on Amazon, Meta, and Google, reaching out to new distributors overseas, and planning for promotion campaigns on our website. The reason I left this job a month ago was because they moved office to a different location and it was too far from where I live. And before this company, I worked for 2 other companies also as a marketing rep.
sentences.zh: 當然，我叫Ian，在過去三年裡，我在一家電子公司擔任行銷經理，這家公司主要販售嬰兒監視器以及相關的軟體訂閱服務。我的主要工作包括監控亞馬遜、Meta和Google上的付費廣告活動，聯繫海外新經銷商，還有規劃網站上的促銷活動。我之所以在一個月前離開這份工作，是因為公司搬到了一個離我家太遠的地方。之前我也在兩家公司擔任行銷專員。

back.grammar: in the past 3 years 搭配現在完成式 I have worked，表示從過去到現在這段期間的經驗。
variations: Sure. I’m Ian, and for the past three years, I’ve worked as a marketing manager at an electronics company.
Of course. My name is Ian. I’ve spent the last three years working in marketing for an electronics company.
Absolutely. I’m Ian, and my recent experience has been as a marketing manager at an electronics company.

extraExamples.en: In the past two years, I have worked as a project coordinator.
extraExamples.zh: 在過去兩年裡，我擔任專案協調員。

extraExamples.en: My main responsibilities include scheduling meetings and tracking budgets.
extraExamples.zh: 我的主要職責包括安排會議和追蹤預算。

sentences.en: Thanks for the brief intro. I was gonna to ask you to walk me through your previous experience but you just did that. So can you share an example of a marketing campaign you worked on at your last job?
sentences.zh: 謝謝你的簡單介紹。我原本想讓你詳細說明過去的經歷，不過你剛才已經說了。所以可以分享一下你在上一份工作中參與的某個行銷活動嗎？

back.grammar: gonna 是 going to 的口語縮讀，後面直接接原形動詞；正確是 I was gonna ask，不說 gonna to ask。
variations: I was going to ask you to walk me through your experience, but you already covered it.
I planned to ask about your background, but you just explained it really well.
I was about to have you go over your past experience, but you already did.

extraExamples.en: I was gonna call you, but then I saw your message.
extraExamples.zh: 我原本要打給你，但後來看到你的訊息。

extraExamples.en: Can you walk me through how you handled that client issue?
extraExamples.zh: 你可以一步步說明你怎麼處理那個客戶問題嗎？

sentences.en: Absolutely. The last campaign I worked on was a Black Friday sale campaign on their website as well as on Amazon. Me and my two other colleagues designed the visual of the promotion page and wrote the sales copies. Our manager gave us the goal of selling 500 devices from that campaign and we hit that target with some help from the influencers we collaborated with!
sentences.zh: 當然可以。我參與的最後一個活動是黑色星期五的促銷活動，這個活動是在我們網站以及亞馬遜上進行的。我和另外兩位同事一起設計了促銷頁面的視覺效果，還寫了銷售文案。我們的經理給我們的目標是希望從這個活動中賣出500台設備，最終我們用了一些我們合作個網紅合作的幫助達成了目標！

back.grammar: The last campaign I worked on was… 用來介紹最近參與的專案；hit that target 表示達成目標。
variations: Definitely. The most recent campaign I worked on was a Black Friday sale for our website and Amazon.
Sure. My last campaign was a Black Friday promotion across our site and Amazon.
Absolutely. I recently worked on a Black Friday campaign that ran on both our website and Amazon.

keyPhrases.phrase: the last campaign I worked on

extraExamples.en: The last project I worked on was a website redesign.
extraExamples.zh: 我參與的上一個專案是網站改版。

extraExamples.en: Our goal was 1,000 sign-ups, and we hit that target in two weeks.
extraExamples.zh: 我們的目標是一千個註冊，結果兩週內就達成了。

sentences.en: Nice! So tell me, Ian, what made you want to look for a new job in the manufacturing industry since it is very different from your last job?
sentences.zh: 哇，真不錯！那我想問問，Ian，既然你之前的工作經驗跟製造業差異那麼大，當初是什麼讓你想轉職呢？

back.grammar: What made you want to… 是自然問法，意思是「是什麼讓你想……？」用來詢問動機。
variations: What made you interested in moving into manufacturing?
What led you to look for a job in the manufacturing industry?
Why are you looking to switch to manufacturing when your last role was so different?

extraExamples.en: What made you want to study abroad?
extraExamples.zh: 是什麼讓你想出國留學？

extraExamples.en: What led you to look for a new apartment?
extraExamples.zh: 是什麼讓你開始找新公寓？

sentences.en: My last company also handled manufacturing, so I’ve always had an interest in manufacturing businesses. I really got along with my team. I am not gonna lie, but I felt stagnant there. I really wanted to bring a different kind of value to a new company.
sentences.zh: 我上一家公司也是一家製造該設備的企業，所以我一直對製造業很感興趣。我和上一家公司的團隊相處得非常好。但說實話，我感覺在那裡有些停滯不前。我真的希望能為一家新公司帶來不同的價值。

back.grammar: I’m not gonna lie 是口語中引出誠實想法的說法；felt stagnant 表示覺得停滯、沒有成長。
variations: Honestly, I really liked my team, but I felt like I wasn’t growing anymore.
To be honest, I got along well with everyone, but I felt stuck there.
I won’t lie—I enjoyed the team, but I wanted a new challenge.

extraExamples.en: I got along with my roommates, but the apartment was too small.
extraExamples.zh: 我和室友相處得很好，但公寓太小了。

extraExamples.en: I’m not gonna lie, I felt stagnant in that role after a year.
extraExamples.zh: 老實說，做那個職位一年後我覺得有點停滯。

sentences.en: I’m glad to hear that! And how do you think you can add value to our company in the first 90 days if you are hired?
sentences.zh: 很高興聽到這一點！那麼，如果你被錄取，你認為在前90天內可以為我們公司帶來什麼價值呢？

back.grammar: if you are hired 是條件句；add value to… 是職場常用語，表示為公司帶來貢獻。
variations: How do you think you could contribute in your first 90 days if we hire you?
If you joined our team, what value could you bring in the first three months?
What impact do you think you could make during your first 90 days here?

extraExamples.en: I want to add value to the team by improving our social media strategy.
extraExamples.zh: 我想透過改善社群媒體策略為團隊帶來價值。

extraExamples.en: If you are hired, you’ll start with a two-week training program.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你被錄取，你會先進行兩週的訓練。

sentences.en: Before this interview, I looked over your website, and I thought I could bring my previous experience of working with influencers and looking for new distributors. I have emailed you a mock presentation of how I can contribute to the team. Would you like me to go over it?
sentences.zh: 在這次面試前，我查看了你們的網站，我認為可以將我在與網紅合作以及尋找新經銷商方面的經驗帶到貴公司。我今天早上已經發了一份 10 頁的簡報給您，裡面詳細說明了我可以為團隊做出的貢獻。您希望我快速講解一下嗎？

back.grammar: looked over 表示快速查看；Would you like me to… 是禮貌詢問對方是否要自己做某事。
variations: Before the interview, I reviewed your website and put together a mock presentation. Would you like me to walk you through it?
I took a look at your website before today and sent over a sample presentation. Should I go over it now?
I checked out your website beforehand and emailed you a mock deck. Would it be helpful if I explained it briefly?

extraExamples.en: I looked over the contract last night, and I have a few questions.
extraExamples.zh: 我昨晚看過合約了，有幾個問題。

extraExamples.en: Would you like me to go over the schedule with you?
extraExamples.zh: 你要我跟你講解一下行程嗎？

sentences.en: Awesome! We will leave that to the next meeting with our manager. For now, I would like to know if there is anything you did not do at your last position that you’d like to do? Or vice versa?
sentences.zh: 太棒了！我們可以把這部分留到下次和我們經理的會議再討論。目前我想知道，是否有什麼事情是你在上一份工作中沒機會做，但希望能嘗試的？或者反過來，有什麼是你不想再做的？

back.grammar: leave that to… 表示「把那件事留到……」；vice versa 表示「反過來也一樣／相反情況」。
variations: Great. We can save that for the next meeting with our manager.
Awesome. Let’s leave that for the manager meeting.
Nice. We’ll cover that in the next conversation with our manager.

keyPhrases.phrase: leave that to the next meeting
keyPhrases.zh: 把那件事留到下次會議

extraExamples.en: Let’s leave the budget discussion to next week’s meeting.
extraExamples.zh: 我們把預算討論留到下週會議。

extraExamples.en: Do you prefer working from home, or vice versa—do you prefer the office?
extraExamples.zh: 你比較喜歡在家工作，還是相反地比較喜歡辦公室？

sentences.en: Well, I would like to have a bit more responsibility with cross-department coordination. At my last company, I did have some of that, but it’s usually on very specific things like resolving a customer’s complaint with the engineers.
sentences.zh: 嗯，我希望能多一些跨部門協作的責任。在我上一家公司，我有參與一些這類協作，但通常是針對非常具體的事情，比如與工程師一起解決客戶的抱怨。

back.grammar: would like to have 表達較委婉的希望；cross-department coordination 是職場常見的「跨部門協調」。
variations: I’d like to take on more cross-functional coordination.
I’m hoping to get more involved in working across departments.
I’d love to have more responsibility coordinating with other teams.

extraExamples.en: I’d like to have a bit more responsibility in client communication.
extraExamples.zh: 我希望在客戶溝通方面能多一點責任。

extraExamples.en: This project requires a lot of cross-department coordination.
extraExamples.zh: 這個專案需要很多跨部門協調。

sentences.en: Since we’re running short on time, I gotta get the last few questions in. Have you ever had a disagreement with your coworker or your manager? What happened there, and how did you solve it?
sentences.zh: 由於時間不多了，我需要抓緊問最後幾個問題。你是否曾經和同事或經理有過意見不合？當時發生了什麼？你是如何解決的？

back.grammar: running short on time 表示時間快不夠；gotta 是 have got to 的口語縮讀，意思是「必須」。
variations: Since we’re almost out of time, I need to ask the last few questions.
We’re running a little short on time, so let me fit in the final questions.
We don’t have much time left, so I want to get through a few more questions.

keyPhrases.phrase: I gotta get the last few questions in
keyPhrases.zh: 我得趕快問完最後幾個問題

extraExamples.en: We’re running short on time, so let’s skip the small talk.
extraExamples.zh: 我們時間快不夠了，所以先跳過寒暄吧。

extraExamples.en: I had a disagreement with my coworker about the deadline, but we talked it through.
extraExamples.zh: 我和同事對截止日期有意見不合，但我們溝通後解決了。

sentences.en: Yes, I have actually. It was a discussion between me and my manager about an in-person event. I won’t go into details, but it was about how we set up our booth at an influencer event to best attract influencers’ attention. I designed a bear cardboard for our photobooth, but my manager suggested a stork cardboard instead, as the bird is often viewed as the bringer of newborn babies. Initially, I thought a bear would be cuter, but then I quickly came to the realization that her idea was better because it aligned with the brand image more.
sentences.zh: 是的，我的確遇到過。當時是我和經理之間針對一場線下活動進行的討論。我不會講太多細節，但主要是關於我們如何在一場網紅活動中佈置展位，以便更好地吸引網紅的注意力。我設計了一個用於拍照亭的熊形立牌，但我的經理建議用鸛形硬紙板，因為鸛通常被視為新生兒的帶來者。一開始我認為熊更可愛，但我很快地意識到她的想法更好，因為它與品牌形象更加契合。

back.grammar: 「have actually」是現在完成式的簡短回答，表示「確實有過這樣的經驗」；另外「came to the realization that...」是較正式的說法，意思是「逐漸意識到……」。
variations: Yes, I actually have.
Yeah, I’ve been in that situation before.
At first I liked my idea better, but I realized hers fit the brand more.

extraExamples.en: I won’t go into details, but the project changed a lot at the last minute.
extraExamples.zh: 我不會講太多細節，但那個專案在最後一刻改了很多。

extraExamples.en: The new packaging really aligns with our brand image.
extraExamples.zh: 新的包裝真的很符合我們的品牌形象。

sentences.en: Thanks for sharing that with me! And finally, what are your salary expectations?
sentences.zh: 感謝你的分享！最後，你對薪資的期待是什麼？

back.grammar: 「Thanks for sharing that with me」是面試中常見的禮貌回應；「salary expectations」指薪資期待，通常用複數。
variations: Thanks for walking me through that.
I appreciate you sharing that.
To wrap up, what salary range are you looking for?

keyPhrases.phrase: Thanks for sharing that with me
keyPhrases.zh: 謝謝你跟我分享這件事

extraExamples.en: Thanks for sharing that with me. It helps me understand your background better.
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝你跟我分享，這讓我更了解你的背景。

extraExamples.en: What are your salary expectations for this position?
extraExamples.zh: 你對這個職位的薪資期待是什麼？

sentences.en: Based on my last job and the responsibilities of this role, I am looking at a salary of $85,000. I'm open to discussion but I would like to stay near that number since I do have 2 other offers on the table right now.
sentences.zh: 根據我上一份工作的薪資以及這個職位的職責，我希望薪資能在 85,000 美元左右。我對這個數字持開放態度，可以進一步討論，但希望薪資能接近這個範圍，因為我目前還有兩個其他的工作邀約在手上。

back.grammar: 「I am looking at a salary of...」是委婉表達薪資期待；「offers on the table」是口語片語，表示目前已有可考慮的工作邀約。
variations: I’m hoping for something around $85,000.
Given my experience, I’m targeting around $85K.
I’m flexible, but I’d like to stay close to that number.

extraExamples.en: Based on the market rate, I’m looking for something in the low 80s.
extraExamples.zh: 根據市場行情，我希望薪資大約在八萬出頭。

extraExamples.en: I’m open to discussion, but I’d prefer not to go too far below that range.
extraExamples.zh: 我願意討論，但希望不要低於那個範圍太多。

sentences.en: Ok, I will note that down for our manager to discuss with you in the next interview. What I can do here is to go over all the benefits and compensation we offer.
sentences.zh: 好的，我會把這個數字記下來，並交給我們的經理在下一次面試時進一步討論。我現在可以和你介紹我們提供的福利和薪酬方案。

back.grammar: 「note that down」是片語動詞，意思是「把那件事記下來」；「go over」在職場口語中常指「說明、逐項確認」。
variations: I’ll make a note of that for the manager.
I’ll pass that along to our manager for the next round.
For now, I can walk you through our benefits package.

extraExamples.en: Let me note that down so I don’t forget.
extraExamples.zh: 我先把那個記下來，免得忘記。

extraExamples.en: Can we go over the benefits before I make a decision?
extraExamples.zh: 在我做決定前，我們可以先看一下福利內容嗎？

sentences.en: Yes, please do!
sentences.zh: 好的，請說！

back.grammar: 「please do」是簡短又禮貌的回應，用來表示「好的，請你這麼做」。
variations: Yes, please.
That would be great.
Sure, I’d appreciate that.

extraExamples.en: If you can send me the details, please do.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你可以把細節寄給我，麻煩你了。

extraExamples.en: Would you like me to explain the next steps? Yes, please do.
extraExamples.zh: 你想要我說明下一步嗎？好的，請說。

sentences.en: So our benefits include the 3 insurances, you know… medical, dental, and eye care. We have our 401k and match up the employee contributions up to 3%. We also include life insurance and other security insurances like identity protection. At the end of the year, we give out bonuses based on how profitable the company was as well as based on the employee performance. And I can send you a full list of all the insurance options with all the details but those are the main ones I want to point out.
sentences.zh: 我們的福利包括三種保險，你知道的……醫療、牙科和眼科保險。我們有 401k 福利，並且會跟員工自付額同額付出到至多 3% 。此外，我們還提供人壽保險和其他安全相關的保險，比如身份保護服務。到了年底，我們會根據公司的盈利情況和員工的表現發放獎金。我也可以發給你一份包含所有保險選項詳細信息的完整清單，但這些是我想特別強調的主要福利。

back.grammar: 「include」用來列舉包含的福利；「match up to 3%」表示公司會依員工提撥金額配對補助，最高到 3%。
variations: Our benefits cover medical, dental, and vision insurance.
We match employee 401(k) contributions up to 3%.
I can send over the full benefits list with all the details.

extraExamples.en: The company matches employee contributions up to 5%.
extraExamples.zh: 公司會配對補助員工提撥金額，最高到 5%。

extraExamples.en: I just want to point out that this role also comes with a yearly bonus.
extraExamples.zh: 我想特別提一下，這個職位也有年終獎金。

sentences.en: Yes, please do send the list. I am also curious if the company provides professional development opportunities?
sentences.zh: 好的，請發給我清單。我還想知道，公司是否提供專業進修的機會？

back.grammar: 「please do send」中的 do 是加強語氣，讓請求更明確；「I am curious if...」是禮貌詢問的自然說法。
variations: Yes, please send the list over.
I’d appreciate it if you could send me the full list.
I was also wondering if you offer professional development opportunities.

extraExamples.en: Please do send me the schedule when you have a chance.
extraExamples.zh: 你方便時，請務必把行程表寄給我。

extraExamples.en: I’m curious if the company offers tuition reimbursement.
extraExamples.zh: 我想知道公司是否提供學費補助。

sentences.en: We do! Every Tuesday, we have Lunch and Learn where an outside presenter or a consultant visits our office to share about latest industry trends and tips. Occasionally, we send key employees to conferences or tradeshows too.
sentences.zh: 我們有！每週二，我們會舉辦「午餐與學習」活動，會邀請外部講師或顧問來辦公室分享最新的行業趨勢和技巧。有時候，我們也會不定期派遣中藥員工參加研討會或展覽。

back.grammar: 「We do!」是用助動詞 do 強調肯定回答；「where」可用來補充說明活動內容或地點。
variations: Yes, we definitely do.
We have a weekly Lunch and Learn with guest speakers.
We sometimes send employees to industry conferences as well.

keyPhrases.zh: 午餐學習會；邊吃午餐邊聽講座的活動

extraExamples.en: Every Friday, we have a Lunch and Learn where team members share useful tools.
extraExamples.zh: 每週五我們有午餐學習會，團隊成員會分享實用工具。

extraExamples.en: Our manager sends a few employees to conferences every year.
extraExamples.zh: 我們主管每年會派幾位員工去參加研討會。

sentences.en: Got it. Everything sounds good to me so far.
sentences.zh: 明白了。目前為止一切聽起來都很不錯。

back.grammar: 「Got it」是口語中表示「了解」的自然說法；「so far」表示「到目前為止」。
variations: That makes sense. Everything sounds good so far.
Understood. I like what I’m hearing so far.
Sounds good to me up to this point.

extraExamples.en: Got it. I’ll send the file by the end of the day.
extraExamples.zh: 了解。我會在今天結束前把檔案寄出。

extraExamples.en: The plan sounds good to me so far.
extraExamples.zh: 到目前為止，我覺得這個計畫聽起來不錯。

sentences.en: Alrighty, Ian. Thank you for your time today and we will be in touch through email!
sentences.zh: 好的，Ian。感謝你今天的時間，我們之後會通過電子郵件與你聯繫！

back.grammar: 「Alrighty」是非常口語、輕鬆的「Alright」；「be in touch」表示之後會聯絡。
variations: Alright, Ian. Thanks for your time today.
Thanks for speaking with me today. We’ll follow up by email.
We’ll be in touch soon via email.

extraExamples.en: Thanks for your time today. We’ll be in touch next week.
extraExamples.zh: 感謝你今天抽空。我們下週會再聯絡。

extraExamples.en: I’ll be in touch by email once I have an update.
extraExamples.zh: 一有最新消息，我會用 email 跟你聯絡。

sentences.en: Thank you for your time and it was a pleasure meeting you!
sentences.zh: 感謝您的時間，今天很高興能與您會面！

back.grammar: 「It was a pleasure meeting you」是面試或商務場合常見的禮貌結尾，意思是「很高興見到你」。
variations: Thank you for your time. It was great meeting you.
I really appreciate your time today.
It was nice speaking with you today.

keyPhrases.phrase: It was a pleasure meeting you

extraExamples.en: Thank you for your time today. I look forward to hearing from you.
extraExamples.zh: 感謝您今天抽空。我期待收到您的消息。

extraExamples.en: It was a pleasure meeting you and learning more about the team.
extraExamples.zh: 很高興與您見面，也更了解這個團隊。

units.title: 工作閒聊
units.scenario: Oliver跟同事洗手間閒聊

sentences.en: Hey, Karen! Good to see you! How’s your weekend?
sentences.zh: 嗨，Karen！很高興見到你！週末過得怎麼樣？

back.grammar: How’s = How is；用 How’s your weekend? 輕鬆寒暄，問對方週末過得如何。
variations: Hey, Karen! Great to see you. How was your weekend?
Hi, Karen! Nice seeing you. Did you have a good weekend?
Hey! Good to run into you. How’d your weekend go?

extraExamples.en: Good to see you! How’s your week going?
extraExamples.zh: 很高興見到你！你這週過得怎麼樣？

extraExamples.en: Hey, Mike! How was your weekend?
extraExamples.zh: 嗨，Mike！你週末過得如何？

sentences.en: Hey, Oliver! It’s been alright! Can you believe how cold it is outside today? I’ve been a little under the weather so mostly staying in over the weekend!
sentences.zh: 嗨，Oliver！還可以啦！你能相信今天外面有多冷嗎？我有點小感冒、不舒服，所以週末基本上都待在家裡！

back.grammar: It’s been alright 是現在完成式口語回應；under the weather 表示身體不太舒服。
variations: Hey, Oliver! It’s been okay. It’s freezing out today, right? I’ve been feeling a little sick, so I mostly stayed home this weekend.
Hi, Oliver! Not bad. Can you believe this cold? I’ve been under the weather, so I just stayed in most of the weekend.
Hey! It’s been fine. It’s so cold outside today. I haven’t been feeling great, so I kept it low-key at home.

extraExamples.en: I’m a little under the weather, so I’m going to stay in tonight.
extraExamples.zh: 我有點不舒服，所以今晚打算待在家裡。

extraExamples.en: It’s been alright, just a little busy with work.
extraExamples.zh: 還可以，只是工作有點忙。

sentences.en: Oh, I’m sorry to hear that. Did you at least do anything fun with your kids?
sentences.zh: 哦，聽到這個真遺憾。你有至少帶孩子做點有趣的事情嗎？

back.grammar: I’m sorry to hear that 是聽到壞消息時的自然安慰語；Did you at least...? 表示「至少有沒有……？」
variations: Aw, sorry to hear that. Did you get to do anything fun with the kids at least?
Oh no, I’m sorry. Were you able to do anything fun with your kids?
That’s too bad. At least you got to hang out with your kids, right?

extraExamples.en: I’m sorry to hear that. Did you at least get some rest?
extraExamples.zh: 聽到這件事我很遺憾。你至少有休息一下嗎？

extraExamples.en: Did you do anything fun over the long weekend?
extraExamples.zh: 你連假有做什麼有趣的事嗎？

sentences.en: Kind of, I took my youngest two to a pumpkin patch towards Calabasas but they were not behaving well so most of the trip was just me yelling at them and them being mad at me…
sentences.zh: 算是吧，我帶了最小的兩個孩子去卡拉巴薩斯附近的南瓜園，但是他們不太聽話，所以大部分時間就是我在吼他們，而他們對我生氣……

back.grammar: Kind of 表示「算是、有一點」；me yelling at them and them being mad at me 是口語中用受詞 + V-ing 描述狀況。
variations: Sort of. I took my two youngest to a pumpkin patch near Calabasas, but they were acting up, so I spent most of the trip yelling and they spent it being mad at me.
Kind of. I brought the younger two to a pumpkin patch out by Calabasas, but they weren’t listening, so it was mostly me scolding them the whole time.
A little. I took my youngest two kids to a pumpkin patch around Calabasas, but they were misbehaving, so the trip was pretty stressful.

extraExamples.en: Kind of. We went to the beach, but it was too windy to enjoy it.
extraExamples.zh: 算是吧。我們去了海邊，但風太大，沒辦法好好玩。

extraExamples.en: The kids weren’t behaving well at the restaurant, so we left early.
extraExamples.zh: 孩子們在餐廳不太守規矩，所以我們提早離開了。

sentences.en: Oh no! You were doing your best I’m sure. I can’t imagine having to keep two little humans with infinite energy quiet …
sentences.zh: 哦天啊！我相信你已經盡力了。我無法想像要讓兩個精力無限的小傢伙安靜下來……

back.grammar: I can’t imagine + V-ing 表示「我無法想像做某事」；having to 表示「不得不、必須」。
variations: Oh no! I’m sure you did the best you could. I can’t imagine trying to keep two energetic little kids quiet.
Yikes! I bet you were doing your best. Keeping two tiny humans with endless energy quiet sounds impossible.
Oh man! I’m sure you handled it as well as you could. I can’t even imagine managing two little kids all day.

extraExamples.en: You did your best, and that’s all you can do.
extraExamples.zh: 你已經盡力了，這樣就夠了。

extraExamples.en: I can’t imagine working full-time and taking care of three kids.
extraExamples.zh: 我無法想像一邊全職工作一邊照顧三個孩子。

sentences.en: Yeah, I know. What about you? Did you catch the Dodgers game last night?
sentences.zh: 是啊，我知道。那你呢？你昨晚有看道奇隊的比賽嗎？

back.grammar: What about you? 用來把話題轉回對方；catch the game 是口語「看比賽」。
variations: Yeah, I know. How about you? Did you watch the Dodgers game last night?
Right. What about you? Did you see the Dodgers play last night?
Yeah. Anyway, did you catch last night’s Dodgers game?

extraExamples.en: Did you catch the Lakers game yesterday?
extraExamples.zh: 你昨天有看湖人隊的比賽嗎？

extraExamples.en: I’m doing okay. What about you?
extraExamples.zh: 我還可以。那你呢？

sentences.en: No, not a big baseball fan but I saw online that they’re making it to the championship!
sentences.zh: 沒有啦，我不算棒球迷，不過我看到網上說他們進入總冠軍賽了！

back.grammar: not a big fan of... 口語表示「不是很喜歡／不算粉絲」；make it to... 表示「成功進入」。
variations: No, I’m not really into baseball, but I saw online that they made it to the championship!
Nah, I’m not much of a baseball fan, but I saw they’re going to the championship!
No, baseball’s not really my thing, but I heard they made the championship.

extraExamples.en: I’m not a big coffee person, but this latte is really good.
extraExamples.zh: 我不算很愛喝咖啡的人，但這杯拿鐵真的很好喝。

extraExamples.en: Our team made it to the finals for the first time.
extraExamples.zh: 我們隊第一次進入決賽。

sentences.en: Yeah. Crazy huh! Any vacation plans coming up for Christmas?
sentences.zh: 是啊，瘋狂吧！聖誕節有什麼休假期間的計畫嗎？

back.grammar: coming up 表示「即將到來」；Any...? 是省略句，口語中常用來快速詢問。
variations: Yeah, wild, right? Do you have any Christmas vacation plans?
Yeah, pretty crazy. Got any plans for Christmas break?
I know, right? Are you doing anything for Christmas?

extraExamples.en: Do you have any trips coming up?
extraExamples.zh: 你最近有什麼旅行計畫嗎？

extraExamples.en: Any plans for the long weekend?
extraExamples.zh: 連假有什麼計畫嗎？

sentences.en: Not really! I am saving my vacation days for visiting my family in Taiwan in the next few months!
sentences.zh: 沒有什麼特別的！我正在累積可休假天數，準備幾個月後去台灣看家人！

back.grammar: be saving... for... 表示「正在為了某目的保留／存」；vacation days 指可請假的天數。
variations: Not really. I’m saving my days off to visit my family in Taiwan in a few months.
No big plans. I’m using my vacation days later to go see my family in Taiwan.
Not much. I’m trying to save my PTO for a trip to Taiwan to visit family.

extraExamples.en: I’m saving my vacation days for my sister’s wedding.
extraExamples.zh: 我正在保留休假天數，準備參加我姊姊的婚禮。

extraExamples.en: Not really. I’ll probably just stay home and relax.
extraExamples.zh: 沒有什麼特別的。我可能就待在家放鬆。

sentences.en: Oh, that’s right! How’s your family?
sentences.zh: 哦，對了！你家人還好嗎？

back.grammar: That’s right! 在口語中可表示「對喔，我想起來了！」；How’s your family? 是問候家人的自然說法。
variations: Oh, right! How’s your family doing?
That’s right, you mentioned that. How’s everyone back home?
Oh yeah! How are your folks doing?

extraExamples.en: Oh, that’s right! You’re going to Japan next month.
extraExamples.zh: 喔，對耶！你下個月要去日本。

extraExamples.en: How’s your family doing these days?
extraExamples.zh: 你家人最近還好嗎？

sentences.en: They’re doing well. Or at least from what they told me haha.
sentences.zh: 他們很好啦。至少據他們說是這樣的，哈哈。

back.grammar: They’re doing well 是自然回答「他們過得很好」；from what they told me 表示「根據他們告訴我的」。
variations: They’re doing well—at least that’s what they say, haha.
They’re good, from what I hear, haha.
Everyone seems to be doing fine, or at least that’s what they tell me.

extraExamples.en: My parents are doing well, but they’re always busy.
extraExamples.zh: 我爸媽過得很好，但他們總是很忙。

extraExamples.en: From what she told me, the meeting went pretty smoothly.
extraExamples.zh: 根據她告訴我的，會議進行得很順利。

sentences.en: Now, THAT’S tough. I am not complaining about my kids no more. At least I get to see them every day after work.
sentences.zh: 嗯，那真的挺難的。我再也不抱怨我的孩子了。至少我每天下班後都能見到他們。

back.grammar: Now, THAT’S tough 用重音強調「那才真的難」；no more 是口語用法，標準文法常說 anymore。
variations: Wow, that’s really tough. I’m not complaining about my kids anymore. At least I get to see them after work every day.
Man, that’s hard. I’ll stop complaining about my kids. At least I see them every day after work.
Okay, that puts things in perspective. I shouldn’t complain about my kids anymore since I get to see them daily.

extraExamples.en: That’s tough. I hope things get better soon.
extraExamples.zh: 那真的很辛苦。希望事情很快好轉。

extraExamples.en: I shouldn’t complain about my commute. At least I can work from home twice a week.
extraExamples.zh: 我不該抱怨通勤。至少我一週可以在家工作兩天。

sentences.en: Yeah, you shouldn’t! I mean, parenting is a blessing, right? Even if at times it’s chaotic.
sentences.zh: 是啊，你當然不該這樣想！我是說，養育孩子是一種祝福，對吧？即使有時候會很混亂。

back.grammar: Even if 表示「即使」；at times 是較自然的說法，意思是「有時候」。
variations: Yeah, don’t! I mean, being a parent is a blessing, right? Even when it gets chaotic.
Exactly! Parenting is a gift, even if it’s crazy sometimes.
Yeah, you shouldn’t. Having kids is a blessing, even on the chaotic days.

extraExamples.en: Living with roommates can be fun, even if it’s chaotic at times.
extraExamples.zh: 跟室友住可以很有趣，即使有時候很混亂。

extraExamples.en: Parenting is exhausting, but it’s also a blessing.
extraExamples.zh: 養小孩很累，但也是一種祝福。

sentences.en: Yeah, but honestly, sometimes I wonder if I should’ve just gotten another dog instead of having kids.
sentences.zh: 是啊，但說實話，有時候我會想，也許當初我早知道應該再養一隻狗，而不是生小孩。

back.grammar: should’ve + p.p. 表示「當初應該……卻沒有」，常用來半開玩笑地後悔或假設。
variations: Yeah, but honestly, sometimes I think I should’ve just gotten another dog instead of having kids.
True, but some days I wonder if another dog would’ve been easier than kids.
Yeah, but I’m not gonna lie—sometimes I think a second dog might’ve been the better choice.

extraExamples.en: Honestly, I wonder if I should’ve ordered pizza instead of cooking.
extraExamples.zh: 老實說，我在想我是不是當初應該叫披薩，而不是自己煮。

extraExamples.en: She should’ve taken a taxi instead of walking in the rain.
extraExamples.zh: 她當初應該搭計程車，而不是在雨中走路。

sentences.en: Oh, uh… wow, Karen. That’s… something.
sentences.zh: 哦，呃……哇，Karen，這個……還真是個特別的想法。

back.grammar: That’s... something 是委婉又尷尬的回應，表示「這還真特別／我不知道該怎麼接」。
variations: Oh… wow, Karen. That’s quite a take.
Uh… okay, Karen. That’s definitely one way to look at it.
Oh wow. That’s… a lot to process.

extraExamples.en: You want to quit your job and become a magician? That’s… something.
extraExamples.zh: 你想辭掉工作去當魔術師？這還真特別。

extraExamples.en: He said pineapple belongs on every pizza. That’s quite a take.
extraExamples.zh: 他說每種披薩都該加鳳梨。這看法很特別。

sentences.en: Don’t get me wrong! I love them. But dogs won’t talk back to me or call me at work 5 times just to ask where I put their cereals or Lunchables.
sentences.zh: 別誤會！我當然愛他們。但狗不會跟我頂嘴，也不會在我上班時打五通電話來問我麥片或午餐點心放在哪裡。

back.grammar: Don’t get me wrong 是很常見的口語開場，用來先澄清「別誤會我的意思」；won’t 在這裡表示「不會／不可能會」的習慣或特性。
variations: I’m not saying I don’t love them, but dogs don’t talk back.
Don’t take this the wrong way—I love my kids, but dogs are easier.
I love them, obviously. It’s just that dogs don’t call me five times at work.

keyPhrases.zh: 在我上班時打電話給我

extraExamples.en: Don’t get me wrong—I like my job, but the meetings are exhausting.
extraExamples.zh: 別誤會，我喜歡我的工作，但那些會議真的很累人。

extraExamples.en: My teenager talks back to me whenever I ask him to clean his room.
extraExamples.zh: 我叫我家青少年整理房間時，他總是跟我頂嘴。

sentences.en: Hey, before you know it, they’ll be all grown up and out of the house for college!
sentences.zh: 嘿，轉眼之間，他們就長大離家去上大學了！

back.grammar: before you know it 是口語片語，意思是「轉眼間／不知不覺」；they’ll be 是 they will be 的縮寫，表示未來會發生的事。
variations: They’ll be off to college before you even realize it.
One day you’ll blink, and they’ll be grown and gone.
Trust me, it goes fast—they’ll be out of the house before you know it.

extraExamples.en: Enjoy the weekend—before you know it, Monday will be here.
extraExamples.zh: 好好享受週末吧，轉眼間星期一就到了。

extraExamples.en: My little cousin is all grown up and starting her first job next week.
extraExamples.zh: 我的小表妹已經長大了，下週要開始她的第一份工作。

sentences.en: All grown up? Ha! Clearly you’ve never had an adult kid ask for money or a ride at 2 a.m.
sentences.zh: 長大？哈！你顯然沒遇過已經成年的小孩凌晨兩點還打電話來要錢或要接送吧。

back.grammar: Clearly you’ve never... 用現在完成式表示「你顯然從沒經歷過」；have an adult kid ask... 是 have + 受詞 + 原形動詞，表示「遇到／經歷某人做某事」。
variations: Clearly, you’ve never had a grown kid call you for cash at 2 a.m.
You say that like you’ve never dealt with an adult child needing a ride in the middle of the night.
Ha! Wait until your adult kid asks you for money at two in the morning.

extraExamples.en: Clearly, you’ve never tried to find parking downtown on a Friday night.
extraExamples.zh: 你顯然從沒試過週五晚上在市中心找停車位。

extraExamples.en: My brother called me at midnight to ask for a ride home.
extraExamples.zh: 我弟半夜打給我，要我載他回家。

sentences.en: Uh, well… I suppose not.
sentences.zh: 呃……嗯，我想沒有吧。

back.grammar: I suppose not 是委婉、不太肯定的說法，意思是「我想沒有吧／大概不是」，比直接說 no 更柔和。
variations: Yeah, I guess not.
Probably not, no.
I don’t think I have, actually.

keyPhrases.zh: 呃……嗯……；用來拖延或表示尷尬

extraExamples.en: Do you think we need to leave right now? — I suppose not.
extraExamples.zh: 你覺得我們現在需要走嗎？——我想不用吧。

extraExamples.en: Uh, well... I guess I forgot to reply to her message.
extraExamples.zh: 呃……嗯……我想我忘了回她訊息。

sentences.en: You know, Oliver, enjoy it while you can. No kids, no stress. Just you and your fancy trips to Taiwan.
sentences.zh: 你知道嗎，Oliver，好好享受吧，趁你還可以的時候。沒小孩，沒壓力，就只有你和你那些「高級」的台灣旅行。

back.grammar: enjoy it while you can 是口語勸告，意思是「趁還可以的時候好好享受」；No kids, no stress 是省略句，強調「沒有小孩就沒有壓力」。
variations: Enjoy the freedom while it lasts.
You should soak it up—no kids, no chaos.
Make the most of it while you can: no kids, no stress, just travel.

extraExamples.en: Enjoy sleeping in while you can—once the baby comes, it’s over.
extraExamples.zh: 趁現在還能睡到自然醒就好好享受吧，寶寶出生後就沒了。

extraExamples.en: No homework, no meetings—just a quiet Saturday at home.
extraExamples.zh: 沒有作業，沒有會議——只有安靜待在家的週六。

sentences.en: It’s not exactly “fancy”…
sentences.zh: 也稱不上「高級」啦……

back.grammar: not exactly 是口語中委婉否定的說法，表示「也不完全是／稱不上」，比直接說 it’s not fancy 更含蓄。
variations: I wouldn’t exactly call it fancy.
It’s not really that fancy.
Fancy is kind of a stretch.

extraExamples.en: The hotel was nice, but not exactly luxury.
extraExamples.zh: 那間飯店不錯，但也稱不上豪華。

extraExamples.en: I wouldn’t exactly call him friendly, but he’s polite.
extraExamples.zh: 我不會說他很友善，但他很有禮貌。

sentences.en: Oh, come on. You probably sip apple juice on the plane and laugh at us poor parents stuck at home.
sentences.zh: 哎呀，別這樣！你大概坐在飛機上喝著蘋果汁，然後嘲笑我們這些被困在家的可憐爸媽吧。

back.grammar: Oh, come on 是口語反應，表示「拜託／少來了」；probably + 動詞用來猜測對方可能在做的事，帶有開玩笑或挖苦語氣。
variations: Oh please, you’re probably relaxing on the plane while we’re stuck here.
Come on, you’re probably living your best life in the air.
Yeah right—you’re probably sipping juice at 30,000 feet and laughing at us.

extraExamples.en: Oh, come on, you know that movie was terrible.
extraExamples.zh: 拜託，你也知道那部電影很爛。

extraExamples.en: I’m stuck at home waiting for the repair guy all afternoon.
extraExamples.zh: 我整個下午都被困在家等維修人員。

sentences.en: Karen, that’s not what I …
sentences.zh: Karen，我不是這個意思……

back.grammar: That’s not what I... 是被打斷時常見的未完成句，完整可說 That’s not what I meant，意思是「我不是那個意思」。
variations: That’s not what I meant...
I didn’t mean it like that...
Wait, that came out wrong...

keyPhrases.zh: 我不是那個……；話說到一半被打斷

extraExamples.en: That’s not what I meant—I was just trying to help.
extraExamples.zh: 我不是那個意思，我只是想幫忙。

extraExamples.en: I didn’t mean it like that. I’m sorry if it sounded rude.
extraExamples.zh: 我不是那樣的意思。如果聽起來很沒禮貌，我很抱歉。

sentences.en: Oh, don’t mind me! I’m just jealous. Anyway, I should go before I say anything else regrettable.
sentences.zh: 哎，別介意！我只是嫉妒而已。總之，我該走了，免得又說出什麼會後悔的話。

back.grammar: Don’t mind me 是口語中表示「別理我／別在意我」；I should go before... 用 should 表示「我該走了」，before 後接句子表示「免得／在……之前」。
variations: Ignore me—I’m just being jealous.
Don’t pay attention to me. I’m just jealous.
I’d better get going before I say something I regret.

extraExamples.en: Don’t mind me—I’m just tired and cranky today.
extraExamples.zh: 別理我，我今天只是累又暴躁。

extraExamples.en: I should go before I buy everything in this store.
extraExamples.zh: 我該走了，免得把這間店的東西都買下來。

sentences.en: Uh… sure. See you later?
sentences.zh: 呃……好吧，那回頭見？

back.grammar: See you later? 加問號語氣表示不太確定、尷尬或試探性的道別，不像直述句 See you later 那麼肯定。
variations: Okay... see you around?
Uh, sure. Talk to you later?
Alright, I guess I’ll see you later?

extraExamples.en: Uh... sure, we can talk about it tomorrow.
extraExamples.zh: 呃……好吧，我們明天可以談這件事。

extraExamples.en: Okay, I’ll see you later after class.
extraExamples.zh: 好，那我下課後再見你。

units.title: 新聞時事探討
units.scenario: Ian跟室友看電視聊新聞

sentences.en: Hey Marry, how are you?
sentences.zh: 嘿，Marry，你好嗎？

back.grammar: How are you? 是最常見的問候語；熟人之間也常用 Hey 開頭，語氣自然親切。
variations: Hey Mary, how’s it going?
Hi Mary, how’ve you been?
Hey Mary, what’s up?

extraExamples.en: Hey Jason, how are you?
extraExamples.zh: 嘿，Jason，你好嗎？

extraExamples.en: Hey, how’s it going?
extraExamples.zh: 嘿，最近怎麼樣？

sentences.en: Ok I guess…just a little down in the dumps…
sentences.zh: 還行吧……只是有點情緒低落……

back.grammar: down in the dumps 是口語慣用語，表示心情低落、悶悶不樂。
variations: I’m okay, I guess… just feeling kind of down.
I’m all right… just a little bummed out.
I’m fine, sort of… just not in the best mood.

extraExamples.en: She’s been down in the dumps since she failed the test.
extraExamples.zh: 她考試沒過之後一直很沮喪。

extraExamples.en: I’m okay, I guess, just a little tired.
extraExamples.zh: 我還行吧，只是有點累。

sentences.en: Oh no, what’s wrong?
sentences.zh: 哎呀，怎麼了？

back.grammar: What’s wrong? 是詢問對方發生什麼事的自然口語，比 What happened? 更偏關心狀態。
variations: Oh no, what happened?
Uh-oh, are you okay?
Oh no, what’s going on?

extraExamples.en: You look upset. What’s wrong?
extraExamples.zh: 你看起來不太開心，怎麼了？

extraExamples.en: Oh no, what happened to your phone?
extraExamples.zh: 哎呀，你的手機怎麼了？

sentences.en: I just have been thinking a lot about the economy lately. I know…I know…I should not do it to myself. But think about it Ian. By the time we graduate from college, the job market will be so bad that we will be lucky to get poverty wages at best! No wonder everyone has so much debt nowadays.
sentences.zh: 我最近一直在想經濟的問題。我知道……我知道……我不該這樣折磨自己。但 Ian 你想想看，等我們從大學畢業時，就業市場會糟糕到一個程度是能拿到微薄工資就已經算幸運的了！難怪現在大家都有這麼多債務。

back.grammar: have been thinking 是現在完成進行式，表示從過去到現在一直在想；by the time + 現在式，主句常用未來式。
variations: I’ve been thinking a lot about the economy lately.
The economy’s been on my mind a lot recently.
I can’t stop worrying about the economy these days.

extraExamples.en: I’ve been thinking a lot about changing jobs lately.
extraExamples.zh: 我最近一直在想換工作的事。

extraExamples.en: By the time we get there, the store will probably be closed.
extraExamples.zh: 等我們到那裡時，店可能已經關了。

sentences.en: You are right, and prices have been skyrocketing, haven't they? Particularly gas prices. I don’t drive a car myself but I was chatting with my parents last week and they told me that it used to cost 20 dollars to fill up, but now, it costs 80!
sentences.zh: 你說得對，而且物價一直在飆升，對吧？尤其是燃油價格。我自己沒有開車，但上週和我爸媽聊天時，他們告訴我，以前加滿油箱只要 20 美元，現在竟然要 80 美元！

back.grammar: have been skyrocketing 是現在完成進行式，強調價格最近持續快速上漲；haven’t they? 是附加問句，用來確認對方同意。
variations: Prices have been going through the roof, haven’t they?
Everything’s gotten so expensive lately, right?
Prices keep shooting up, especially gas.

extraExamples.en: Rent has been skyrocketing in this city.
extraExamples.zh: 這個城市的房租一直在飆升。

extraExamples.en: It used to cost five dollars, but now it’s almost fifteen.
extraExamples.zh: 以前只要五美元，現在快十五美元了。

sentences.en: This is what I am talking about! Today I was looking at the polls and it seems that the candidate that is set to win the elections will be passing all these new laws that will have an even bigger negative impact on the economy! I can’t believe they have announced all these decisions, even before the elections have begun!
sentences.zh: 我說的就是這個！我剛剛在看民調，好像那位很有可能當選的候選人打算推行一堆新法律，這些法律會對經濟產生更大的負面影響！我簡直不敢相信，他們居然在選舉還沒開始之前就已經宣布這些決定了！

back.grammar: This is what I’m talking about! 是口語中強調「這就是我說的！」；be set to 表示「預計／很可能會」。
variations: That’s exactly what I mean!
See? That’s what I’m saying!
This is exactly my point!

keyPhrases.phrase: This is what I’m talking about

extraExamples.en: The team is set to announce the new coach tomorrow.
extraExamples.zh: 球隊預計明天宣布新教練。

extraExamples.en: This decision could have a big impact on small businesses.
extraExamples.zh: 這個決定可能會對小企業有很大的影響。

sentences.en: Wait a minute, you were looking at the polls? Marry….have you been watching the news again? Is this why you have been feeling so run down?
sentences.zh: 等等，你在看民調？Marry……你又在看新聞了嗎？這就是你最近感覺這麼疲憊不堪的原因嗎？

back.grammar: have you been watching...? 是現在完成進行式，用來問最近是否一直在做某事；run down 形容人疲憊、沒精神。
variations: Hold on, have you been watching the news again?
Wait, is the news making you feel so drained?
Have you been checking the polls again? Is that why you’re exhausted?

keyPhrases.zh: 疲憊不堪；身體或精神很差

extraExamples.en: Have you been staying up late again? You look run down.
extraExamples.zh: 你是不是又一直熬夜？你看起來很累。

extraExamples.en: Wait a minute, did you forget your wallet?
extraExamples.zh: 等等，你是不是忘了帶錢包？

sentences.en: Maybe…
sentences.zh: 也許吧……

back.grammar: Maybe 單字回答在口語中常用來表示不確定、心虛，或不太想承認。
variations: Maybe a little…
Kind of…
I guess so…

extraExamples.en: Are you nervous about the interview? Maybe.
extraExamples.zh: 你對面試很緊張嗎？也許吧。

extraExamples.en: Do you miss him? Kind of.
extraExamples.zh: 你想他嗎？有點。

sentences.en: We have spoken about this. Watching the news will only make you depressed! Most of it is not even objective news reporting, it's just sensationalism.
sentences.zh: 我們之前已經談過這件事了。天天看新聞只會讓你更沮喪！而且大多數根本不是客觀的新聞報導，只是煽情報導罷了。

back.grammar: We have spoken about this. 用現在完成式，表示「之前談過，而且現在仍相關」；sensationalism 指誇大、煽情的報導方式。
variations: We’ve talked about this before.
We’ve been over this already.
Watching the news is just going to make you feel worse.

extraExamples.en: We’ve talked about this before—you need to take breaks from social media.
extraExamples.zh: 我們之前談過這件事——你需要暫時遠離社群媒體。

extraExamples.en: That article isn’t real journalism; it’s pure sensationalism.
extraExamples.zh: 那篇文章不是真正的新聞報導，根本是煽情炒作。

sentences.en: I know, but one thing led to another. First I went online to check the weather for our picnic tomorrow. The forecast said the weather’s gonna be good by the way. But because I typed in my browser “weather forecast”, I had a number of things pop up. Climate change forecast, financial forecast, election forecast…I got all the forecasts.
sentences.zh: 我知道，可是一件事接著一件事。起初我只是上網查明天野餐的天氣預報，順道一提天氣預報說天氣會不錯喔。但因為我輸入了「天氣預報」，結果一堆東西都跳出來了，氣候變遷預測、財務預測、選舉預測……所有的預測都來了。

back.grammar: one thing led to another 是常見口語片語，表示「一件事接著一件事發生」；gonna 是 going to 的口語縮讀。
variations: I know, but one click led to another.
I know, but I kind of fell down an internet rabbit hole.
I only meant to check the weather, but then all these other things popped up.

keyPhrases.zh: going to 的口語縮讀；將要

extraExamples.en: I went online to buy socks, and one thing led to another—I bought a jacket too.
extraExamples.zh: 我上網本來要買襪子，結果一件事接著一件事，連外套也買了。

extraExamples.en: A message popped up on my screen during the meeting.
extraExamples.zh: 會議時我的螢幕上跳出一則訊息。

sentences.en: Shame on you. You are only bringing yourself down by reading up on this stuff.
sentences.zh: 你這樣可不應該。你光是讀這些東西，就把自己搞得心情更差了。

back.grammar: Shame on you. 是半開玩笑或責備用語；bring yourself down 表示讓自己心情變差；read up on 表示深入閱讀、查資料。
variations: You’re just making yourself feel worse by reading that stuff.
Come on, you’re only dragging yourself down with all that news.
You shouldn’t keep reading about things that upset you.

keyPhrases.zh: 你這樣不應該；真不像話

extraExamples.en: Don’t bring yourself down by comparing your life to people online.
extraExamples.zh: 不要拿自己的人生跟網路上的人比較，讓自己心情變差。

extraExamples.en: I need to read up on the company before my interview.
extraExamples.zh: 我面試前需要查一下那間公司的資料。

sentences.en: I do it to myself really. I have spent the whole day being depressed about things I can’t change.
sentences.zh: 說真的，這都是我自己折磨自己。我整天都在為一些無法改變的事情感到沮喪。

back.grammar: have spent + 時間 + V-ing 表示「花了多少時間做某事」；do it to myself 是口語說法，表示自己害自己。
variations: Honestly, I’m doing this to myself.
I spent the whole day stressing over things I can’t control.
I basically made myself miserable over stuff I can’t change.

keyPhrases.zh: 自己折磨自己；自己害自己

extraExamples.en: I spent the whole night worrying about the exam.
extraExamples.zh: 我整晚都在擔心考試。

extraExamples.en: Stop doing this to yourself. You need some rest.
extraExamples.zh: 別再這樣折磨自己了。你需要休息。

sentences.en: Exactly! If you wanted a financial forecast, I could have given you one for free. Things are getting more and more expensive because of inflation. And there has always been inflation! I don’t think they are going to solve it anytime soon.
sentences.zh: 沒錯！如果你想要財務預測，我免費就能給你一個。東西越來越貴，都是因為通貨膨脹。而且通膨一直都存在！我不覺得他們短期內能解決這個問題。

back.grammar: could have + 過去分詞 表示「本來可以……但實際上沒有」；more and more 表示越來越。
variations: If you wanted a money prediction, I could’ve given you one for free.
Everything’s getting more expensive because of inflation.
I don’t think they’ll fix it anytime soon.

extraExamples.en: I could’ve picked you up if you had called me.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你打給我，我本來可以去接你。

extraExamples.en: Groceries are getting more and more expensive.
extraExamples.zh: 雜貨食品變得越來越貴。

sentences.en: You are right…I wonder what all those people at Wall Street do all day? Every time I have met a person working in finance, they are wearing an expensive suit and they tell me they are successful. How are they successful if the economy is doing so bad?
sentences.zh: 你說得對……我真的很好奇，華爾街那些人整天都在做什麼？我每次遇到金融業的人，他們不是穿著昂貴的西裝，就是告訴我他們很成功。如果經濟這麼糟，他們怎麼可能還這麼成功？

back.grammar: I wonder... 用來表達「我很好奇／不知道」；Every time + 子句 表示「每次……都……」。
variations: I wonder what people on Wall Street actually do all day.
What do finance people even do all day?
How are they doing so well when the economy is doing so badly?

extraExamples.en: I wonder why this café is always so crowded.
extraExamples.zh: 我很好奇為什麼這間咖啡店總是這麼多人。

extraExamples.en: Every time I see him, he’s wearing a baseball cap.
extraExamples.zh: 我每次看到他，他都戴著棒球帽。

sentences.en: I know right! I think that even though guys in finance are doing well, this doesn’t mean anything for the rest of the world. I mean look at the housing market. The price of houses and apartments has gone up three times since the last decade.
sentences.zh: 我也覺得！那些金融圈的人過得不錯，但這不代表其他人也一樣好過。你看看房市就知道了，房屋和公寓的價格在過去十年已經漲了三倍。

back.grammar: I know, right? 是美式口語，表示強烈同意；has gone up 用現在完成式表示價格從過去到現在已上漲。
variations: Exactly! Finance guys may be doing fine, but that doesn’t help everyone else.
I know, right? Just look at housing prices.
Sure, people in finance are doing well, but what about the rest of us?

extraExamples.en: I know, right? This restaurant used to be so much cheaper.
extraExamples.zh: 對吧！這間餐廳以前便宜多了。

extraExamples.en: The price of coffee has gone up again.
extraExamples.zh: 咖啡的價格又漲了。

sentences.en: Oh trust me, I will not be buying a house anytime soon. I have to deal with my student loans first.
sentences.zh: 相信我，我短期內絕對不會買房的。我得先解決我的學生貸款。

back.grammar: 「will not be + V-ing」可用來強調未來一段時間內「不會在做某事」；anytime soon 表示「短期內」。
variations: Believe me, I’m not buying a house anytime soon.
Trust me, a house is not happening for me anytime soon.
Yeah, no way I’m buying a place until my student loans are handled.

keyPhrases.zh: 處理／解決我的學生貸款

extraExamples.en: Trust me, I’m not quitting my job anytime soon.
extraExamples.zh: 相信我，我短期內不會辭職。

extraExamples.en: I need to deal with my credit card debt first.
extraExamples.zh: 我得先處理我的信用卡債務。

sentences.en: Buying a house is one thing, but then you have to pay for heating too! And every time the price of oil goes up, we get told that it is because of some war in the Middle East. To be honest, it feels more like an excuse than a reason.
sentences.zh: 買房還只是其中一件事，之後你還得付暖氣費！而且每次油漲價，他們就說是因為中東哪裡又打仗了。說真的，聽起來更像是藉口，而不是真正的原因。

back.grammar: 「A is one thing, but...」用來表達「A 已經是一回事，但後面還有更麻煩的事」；more like A than B 表示「與其說是 B，不如說是 A」。
variations: Buying the house is only part of it—you still have to pay for heat.
The house itself is one cost, but heating is another whole thing.
Honestly, that sounds more like an excuse than an actual reason.

keyPhrases.phrase: more like an excuse than a reason
keyPhrases.zh: 更像藉口，而不是真正理由

extraExamples.en: Rent is one thing, but parking downtown is expensive too.
extraExamples.zh: 房租是一回事，但市中心停車也很貴。

extraExamples.en: That sounds more like a complaint than a real suggestion.
extraExamples.zh: 那聽起來更像抱怨，不像真正的建議。

sentences.en: You seem to know a lot about this stuff, Ian. If I did not know better, I would say you have also been watching the news…
sentences.zh: Ian，看起來你對這些事很了解嘛。如果我不了解你的話，我還以為你也一直在看新聞呢……

back.grammar: 「If I didn’t know better, I would say...」是假設語氣，用來幽默地表示「要不是我知道情況，我還會以為……」。
variations: You sound pretty informed, Ian. I’d almost think you’ve been watching the news too.
Ian, you know a lot about this. If I didn’t know you, I’d say you’ve been keeping up with the news.
Wow, Ian, you’re pretty up to date. Makes me think you’ve been watching the news.

keyPhrases.phrase: know a lot about this stuff

keyPhrases.zh: 如果我不是更清楚情況的話

extraExamples.en: If I didn’t know better, I’d think you practiced this speech all night.
extraExamples.zh: 如果我不了解情況，我會以為你整晚都在練這段演講。

extraExamples.en: You know a lot about coffee. Have you been watching barista videos?
extraExamples.zh: 你對咖啡很懂欸。你是不是一直在看咖啡師影片？

sentences.en: Oops…caught red-handed.
sentences.zh: 哎呀……被你當場抓包了。

back.grammar: 「caught red-handed」是口語慣用語，意思是「當場被抓包／被逮個正著」。
variations: Oops, you got me.
Okay, you caught me.
Yep, busted.

keyPhrases.zh: 被當場抓包；被逮個正著

extraExamples.en: I was eating the last cookie when my sister walked in—caught red-handed.
extraExamples.zh: 我正在吃最後一片餅乾時我妹走進來——當場被抓包。

extraExamples.en: He said he wasn’t checking his phone, but he got caught red-handed.
extraExamples.zh: 他說他沒在看手機，但被當場抓包了。

sentences.en: I totally get you. I also cannot keep away from the news sometimes. It's just so compelling, you want to continue watching.
sentences.zh: 我完全懂你的感受。我有時也忍不住會一直看新聞，因為實在太吸引人了，讓人停不下來。

back.grammar: 「I totally get you」是口語中表示「我完全懂你」；cannot keep away from 表示「忍不住遠離不了」。
variations: I totally know what you mean. Sometimes I can’t stop watching the news either.
I get it. The news can be really hard to turn off.
Same here. Sometimes the news just pulls me in.

keyPhrases.zh: 非常吸引人；讓人停不下來

extraExamples.en: I totally get you. I can’t keep away from online shopping sometimes.
extraExamples.zh: 我完全懂你。我有時候也忍不住一直逛網購。

extraExamples.en: That show is so compelling, I watched five episodes in one night.
extraExamples.zh: 那部影集太吸引人了，我一晚看了五集。

sentences.en: I think we have managed to make ourselves quite depressed today. Tell you what, how about we head out and grab a coffee? My treat!
sentences.zh: 我覺得我們今天真的把自己搞得很沮喪。這樣吧，我們出去喝杯咖啡怎麼樣？我請客！

back.grammar: 「managed to + V」可表示「成功做到某事」，有時帶點自嘲；Tell you what 是口語中提出建議前常用的「這樣吧」。
variations: I think we’ve officially made ourselves miserable today. How about we go get coffee? I’ll buy.
We’ve bummed ourselves out enough for one day. Let’s go grab a coffee—my treat.
Okay, enough depressing news. Want to head out for coffee? It’s on me.

extraExamples.en: We managed to get lost again, even with GPS.
extraExamples.zh: 我們就算有 GPS，還是成功又迷路了。

extraExamples.en: Tell you what, let’s order pizza tonight. My treat.
extraExamples.zh: 這樣吧，今晚我們叫披薩。我請客。

sentences.en: Really? Wow, thank you, Ian! You’re the best!
sentences.zh: 真的嗎？哇，謝謝你，Ian！你真是太棒了！

back.grammar: 「You’re the best!」是美國口語中表達感謝、稱讚對方很貼心的自然說法。
variations: Seriously? Thank you so much—you’re awesome!
Really? That’s so nice of you. You’re the best.
No way, thanks! You’re amazing.

extraExamples.en: You saved me a seat? You’re the best!
extraExamples.zh: 你幫我留位子？你人真好！

extraExamples.en: Really? You can drive me home? Thank you so much!
extraExamples.zh: 真的嗎？你可以載我回家？太謝謝你了！

sentences.en: But promise me you’ll forget everything you read in the news today.
sentences.zh: 但你得答應我，忘掉今天看過的所有新聞。

back.grammar: 「Promise me you’ll...」是口語中要求對方答應做某事的句型；you’ll 是 you will 的縮寫。
variations: Just promise me you’ll stop thinking about the news for today.
Promise me you’ll let all that news go for now.
But you have to promise you’ll forget about everything you read today.

extraExamples.en: Promise me you’ll text me when you get home.
extraExamples.zh: 答應我，你到家後傳訊息給我。

extraExamples.en: Promise me you’ll take a real break this weekend.
extraExamples.zh: 答應我，你這週末要好好休息。

sentences.en: Promise! It’s already behind me!
sentences.zh: 答應你！我已經拋到腦後啦！

back.grammar: 「It’s behind me」是慣用說法，表示「那件事已經過去了／我已經不放在心上了」。
variations: Promise! I’m already over it.
You got it. I’ve already moved on.
Promise. I’m not thinking about it anymore.

extraExamples.en: The argument is behind me now. I just want to move on.
extraExamples.zh: 那場爭吵我已經放下了。我只想繼續往前。

extraExamples.en: I failed the test, but it’s behind me. I’ll do better next time.
extraExamples.zh: 我考試沒考好，但已經過去了。下次我會更好。

units.title: 價值觀傳遞
units.scenario: Oliver跟室友聊種族歧視問題

sentences.en: You know what Oliver, I think I am getting too old for all of this…
sentences.zh: Oliver，你知道嗎？我覺得自己好像真的老了……

back.grammar: 「I’m getting too old for...」是口語中表示「我越來越不適合／受不了某事」的說法，帶有自嘲感。
variations: You know what, Oliver? I think I’m too old for this stuff.
Honestly, Oliver, I feel like I’m getting too old for all this.
Oliver, I swear I’m not cut out for this anymore.

keyPhrases.zh: 變得太老而不適合／受不了某事

extraExamples.en: I’m getting too old for staying out until 2 a.m.
extraExamples.zh: 我越來越受不了在外面待到凌晨兩點了。

extraExamples.en: You know what, I think I’m done arguing about this.
extraExamples.zh: 你知道嗎，我覺得我不想再為這件事吵了。

sentences.en: Haha what has brought this on, Joanna? What are you talking about?
sentences.zh: 哈哈哈，Joanna，怎麼突然這麼說？你在講什麼呢？

back.grammar: 「What has brought this on?」是現在完成式，意思是「是什麼讓你突然這樣？」常用來問對方情緒或反應的原因。
variations: Haha, where is this coming from, Joanna?
What made you say that, Joanna?
Wait, what are you talking about?

keyPhrases.zh: 引發這件事／讓你突然這樣

extraExamples.en: You’re suddenly so quiet. What brought this on?
extraExamples.zh: 你突然變得好安靜。是發生什麼事了？

extraExamples.en: Where is this coming from? You seemed fine earlier.
extraExamples.zh: 怎麼突然這樣？你剛剛看起來還好好的。

sentences.en: You know, when I am feeling gloomy I like to put on an old cartoon movie and just lie back and pretend I am a kid and have not a worry in the world.
sentences.zh: 你知道的，當我心情低落的時候，我喜歡放一部老卡通，躺在那裡，假裝自己還是個小孩，沒有任何一點煩惱。

back.grammar: 「when I’m feeling gloomy」用現在進行式表達一時的情緒狀態；「put on」在這裡是「播放」電影或音樂。
variations: When I’m feeling down, I like to turn on an old cartoon and zone out.
Whenever I’m in a bad mood, I put on an old animated movie and relax.
When I feel gloomy, I just watch an old cartoon and pretend I’m a kid again.

keyPhrases.phrase: not have a worry in the world

extraExamples.en: When I’m feeling gloomy, I put on music and clean my room.
extraExamples.zh: 我心情低落的時候，會放音樂然後打掃房間。

extraExamples.en: On weekends, I like to lie back and not have a worry in the world.
extraExamples.zh: 週末我喜歡躺著放鬆，什麼煩惱都沒有。
