sentences.en: That sounds lovely. I also like to do that. Re-watch those old princess movies. They seem so simple now but when I was a young kid, those movies were my world!
sentences.zh: 聽起來很棒！我也喜歡這樣做，重溫那些老公主電影。現在看起來很簡單，但當我還是小孩子的時候，那些電影就是我的全世界！

back.grammar: 「used to / when I was...」這句雖沒用 used to，但用過去式描述童年回憶；「re-watch」是「重看」。
variations: That sounds really nice. I do that too—I rewatch old princess movies.
I love that. I still go back and watch those princess movies sometimes.
Same here. Those old princess movies felt like everything to me when I was little.

keyPhrases.zh: 是我的全世界；對我非常重要

extraExamples.en: I like to re-watch old sitcoms when I need comfort.
extraExamples.zh: 我需要安慰的時候，喜歡重看以前的情境喜劇。

extraExamples.en: When I was a kid, that video game was my world.
extraExamples.zh: 我小時候，那款電玩就是我的全世界。

sentences.en: Me too! So anyway, I was having a bit of a hard day recently so I put on one of my favourites. The problem is that before the movie began, there was a warning…
sentences.zh: 我也是！所以呢，最近有一天我過得不太順利，就放了一部最愛的老電影來看。但問題是，電影開始前出現了一個警告……

back.grammar: 「I was having a hard day」用過去進行式描述當時正在經歷的不順；「a bit of a...」讓語氣更委婉。
variations: I’d been having a rough day, so I put on one of my favorites.
I was having kind of a bad day, so I watched an old favorite.
I had a pretty rough day recently, so I turned on one of my go-to movies.

keyPhrases.zh: 過得很不順／很辛苦的一天

keyPhrases.phrase: put on one of my favorites

extraExamples.en: I was having a bit of a hard day, so I ordered comfort food.
extraExamples.zh: 那天我過得有點不順，所以點了療癒系食物。

extraExamples.en: The problem is that I forgot my wallet at home.
extraExamples.zh: 問題是我把錢包忘在家裡了。

sentences.en: A warning? For a kids movie?
sentences.zh: 警告？一部兒童電影？

back.grammar: 省略句「A warning? For a kids movie?」是口語中表示驚訝或不解的方式，完整句可理解為「There was a warning for a kids movie?」
variations: A warning? On a children’s movie?
Wait, a warning for a kids’ movie?
Seriously? A warning before a cartoon?

extraExamples.en: A dress code? For a backyard barbecue?
extraExamples.zh: 服裝規定？只是一個後院烤肉耶？

extraExamples.en: A fee? For just canceling the appointment?
extraExamples.zh: 要收費？只是取消預約而已？

sentences.en: I thought it was strange too, right? It read something like this: "This program contains disrespectful depictions of culture because it was created at a time when cultural sensitivity was not a social issue. These ideas were wrong then, and they are wrong now. We want you to also recognize the negative impact these opinions have but we still want to continue providing this movie to our audience". I was so shocked. I just turned it off right away.
sentences.zh: 我也覺得很奇怪，對吧？它上面寫著類似這樣的話：「本節目包含對文化不尊重的描繪，因為它是在文化敏感性還未成為社會議題的時代創作的。這些觀念當時是錯的，現在仍然是錯的。我們希望觀眾能夠理解到這些觀點的負面影響，但仍希望提供這部電影給大家觀看。」我太震驚了，直接關掉不看了。

back.grammar: 「It read something like this」表示「上面大概是這樣寫的」；「turn it off」是片語動詞，意思是「把它關掉」。
variations: I thought that was weird too. The message said something along these lines.
It seemed strange to me too. It basically said the movie had outdated stereotypes.
I was honestly shocked, so I just shut it off right away.

extraExamples.en: The sign read something like, “Please wait to be seated.”
extraExamples.zh: 牌子上大概寫著：「請等候帶位。」

extraExamples.en: The show got too violent, so I turned it off right away.
extraExamples.zh: 那個節目變得太暴力，所以我馬上關掉了。

sentences.en: That is so strange. What does that even mean?
sentences.zh: 這也太奇怪了吧？這到底是什麼意思？

back.grammar: 「What does that even mean?」中的 even 加強語氣，表示「這到底／究竟是什麼意思？」常帶有困惑或不滿。
variations: That’s so weird. What is that supposed to mean?
That’s really strange. What does that even refer to?
That’s odd. What are they even trying to say?

extraExamples.en: He said I was “too nice.” What does that even mean?
extraExamples.zh: 他說我「太好」。那到底是什麼意思？

extraExamples.en: The instructions say “activate the device.” What is that supposed to mean?
extraExamples.zh: 說明寫「啟動裝置」。那到底是什麼意思？

sentences.en: As far as I know this message is added for kid movies that show stereotypes of people. For example, if the movie shows Native Americans it might be flagged up for promoting the wrong image.
sentences.zh: 據我所知，這種警告是針對某些對特定群體有刻板印象的兒童電影加上的。比如，如果電影裡有美洲原住民的角色，可能會被標註為塑造了錯誤形象。

back.grammar: 「As far as I know」是口語中表示「據我所知」的常用開場；「be flagged up」表示「被標記／被提醒注意」。
variations: From what I understand, they add that message to movies with stereotypes.
As far as I can tell, it’s for kids’ movies that portray groups in a stereotypical way.
I think they flag movies when they show people in an outdated or offensive way.

keyPhrases.zh: 呈現對某些人的刻板印象
被標記出來；被提醒注意

extraExamples.en: As far as I know, the meeting is still at three.
extraExamples.zh: 據我所知，會議還是三點。

extraExamples.en: The video was flagged for inappropriate language.
extraExamples.zh: 那支影片因為不當語言被標記了。

sentences.en: Isn’t that a little bit over the top?
sentences.zh: 這是不是有點太誇張了？

back.grammar: 「over the top」是口語片語，意思是「太誇張、過頭了」。前面加「a little bit」讓語氣比較委婉。
variations: Isn’t that kind of over the top?
Doesn’t that seem a bit much?
Isn’t that taking it a little too far?

extraExamples.en: Decorating the whole office for one birthday seems a little over the top.
extraExamples.zh: 為了一個生日把整間辦公室都裝飾起來，好像有點太誇張。

extraExamples.en: Charging ten dollars for water is a bit much.
extraExamples.zh: 一瓶水收十美元有點太誇張了。

sentences.en: I thought so too. The reason I like to watch old cartoons is because it takes me away from everything that is happening. But I guess…..not anymore. Even cartoons have an agenda now!
sentences.zh: 我也這麼覺得。我之所以喜歡看老卡通，是因為它們能讓我暫時逃離現實世界的一切。但現在……看來這不再可能了。連卡通都有自己的政治立場了！

back.grammar: 「The reason... is because...」是口語常見句型，用來解釋原因；「take me away from」表示讓人暫時逃離現實或壓力。
variations: That’s what I thought too. I watch old cartoons to escape from everything.
I felt the same way. Old cartoons used to help me tune out the real world.
Exactly. I watch them to get away from all the stress, but I guess not anymore.

extraExamples.en: Running helps take me away from work stress for a while.
extraExamples.zh: 跑步能讓我暫時逃離工作壓力。

extraExamples.en: I used to enjoy that app, but not anymore.
extraExamples.zh: 我以前喜歡那個 app，但現在不喜歡了。

sentences.en: Yes, I think you are right. Even cartoons have a political meaning now. Where is the fun in that?
sentences.zh: 對啊，我覺得你說得沒錯。現在連卡通都要有政治含義了，這還有什麼樂趣可言呢？

back.grammar: 「Where is the fun in that?」是反問句，意思是「那還有什麼樂趣？」用來表達不認同或失望。
variations: Yeah, I think you’re right. That kind of takes the fun out of it.
Exactly. If even cartoons are political now, what’s the point?
I agree. Where’s the joy in watching them if everything has a message?

keyPhrases.phrase: Where is the fun in that?
take the fun out of it

extraExamples.en: If we have to schedule every minute of the trip, where’s the fun in that?
extraExamples.zh: 如果旅行每一分鐘都要排好，那還有什麼樂趣？

extraExamples.en: Checking work emails on vacation really takes the fun out of it.
extraExamples.zh: 度假時還要看工作信，真的會讓度假失去樂趣。

sentences.en: I think the part of it that bothers me the most is that I am essentially being told that I did the wrong thing even though I have done nothing wrong! I grew up with some cartoon movies that I loved and now I am made to feel guilty about it.
sentences.zh: 最讓我困擾的是，我明明什麼都沒做錯，卻好像被告知自己做錯了！我從小看著這些卡通長大，曾經那麼喜歡它們，現在卻被迫感到內疚。

back.grammar: 「be made to feel guilty」是被動語態，表示「被迫／被弄得覺得內疚」；「even though」表示「即使、雖然」。
variations: What bothers me most is feeling like I’m being blamed for liking something as a kid.
The part that gets to me is that I’m being made to feel guilty for something harmless.
It bugs me that I loved these movies growing up, and now I’m supposed to feel bad about it.

extraExamples.en: What bothers me the most is that no one told me the plan changed.
extraExamples.zh: 最讓我困擾的是，沒有人告訴我計畫變了。

extraExamples.en: She was made to feel guilty for taking a day off.
extraExamples.zh: 她因為請一天假而被弄得很有罪惡感。

sentences.en: Yes, I think children are too young to understand such concepts. Did you laugh at Indians when you were growing up and then think less of them?
sentences.zh: 對啊，孩子們根本太小，無法理解這些概念。你小時候看那些卡通的時候，真的會因此嘲笑印第安人，或覺得他們比較低等嗎？

back.grammar: 「too...to...」表示「太……以至於無法……」；「think less of someone」是片語，意思是「看不起某人／對某人評價降低」。
variations: Kids are too young to really understand ideas like that.
Do you really think kids watched those scenes and looked down on people?
When you were little, did that actually make you respect them less?

keyPhrases.zh: 看輕某人；對某人評價降低

extraExamples.en: My nephew is too young to understand why adults worry about money.
extraExamples.zh: 我姪子年紀太小，還不懂大人為什麼擔心錢。

extraExamples.en: I don’t think less of you because you made one mistake.
extraExamples.zh: 我不會因為你犯了一個錯就看輕你。

sentences.en: No, I did not! Also, the very term "being politically correct" started in America. In the rest of the world, we don’t need a term for it. It is merely known as "being nice to people" and "being polite".
sentences.zh: 當然沒有！而且「政治正確」這個概念本來就是從美國開始的。在世界其他地方，我們根本不需要這個詞，因為它本來就只是「待人友善」和「有禮貌」而已。

back.grammar: 「the very term」中的 very 用來強調「正是這個詞本身」；「be known as」表示「被稱為」。
variations: No, I didn’t. And honestly, outside the U.S., people might just call that being polite.
Not at all. In many places, that’s not called political correctness—it’s just basic kindness.
Nope. To me, it’s simply about being nice and respectful to people.

extraExamples.en: In our family, saying thank you is just known as good manners.
extraExamples.zh: 在我們家，說謝謝就只是被認為是有禮貌。

extraExamples.en: Being on time isn’t being strict; it’s being respectful.
extraExamples.zh: 準時不是嚴格，而是尊重別人。

sentences.en: I guess for the rest of the world, politeness is still pretty important, right?
sentences.zh: 我想，在英國，禮貌應該是一件很重要的事情，對吧？

back.grammar: I guess 用來表示「我想／我猜」，語氣較保留；句尾 right? 是口語中用來確認對方是否同意。
variations: I guess politeness still matters a lot to most people, right?
For the rest of the world, being polite is still a pretty big deal, isn’t it?
I mean, people still care about manners in most places, right?

extraExamples.en: I guess being on time is still pretty important, right?
extraExamples.zh: 我想準時還是很重要的，對吧？

extraExamples.en: For the rest of the team, the deadline is a pretty big deal.
extraExamples.zh: 對團隊其他人來說，截止日期是很重要的事。

sentences.en: It sure is! Manners are everything! And speaking about manners, I do not need a corporate warning in a kid’s movie to tell me not to be rude to people!
sentences.zh: 當然！禮貌就是一切！但說到禮貌，我真的不需要一個企業警告標語來提醒我，在看兒童電影時該怎麼做人！

back.grammar: It sure is! 是強調版的「的確是！」；speaking about/of... 用來自然轉到相關話題。
variations: Absolutely! Manners matter a lot. And speaking of manners, I don’t need some company warning to teach me not to be rude.
They definitely are! Manners are a big deal. And honestly, I don’t need a corporate message in a kids’ movie telling me how to behave.
For sure! Manners matter. But I don’t need a warning label from a company to remind me not to be mean to people.

keyPhrases.zh: 禮貌非常重要；禮貌就是一切

extraExamples.en: It sure is cold today! I should’ve brought a jacket.
extraExamples.zh: 今天真的很冷！我應該帶外套的。

extraExamples.en: Speaking of movies, did you watch that new one on Netflix?
extraExamples.zh: 說到電影，你有看 Netflix 上那部新的嗎？

sentences.en: Joanna, you seem to be very upset about all this.
sentences.zh: Joanna，看起來你對這件事很生氣。

back.grammar: seem to be + 形容詞 表示「看起來似乎……」，比直接說 you are upset 更委婉。
variations: Joanna, you seem really upset about this whole thing.
Joanna, it sounds like this is really bothering you.
Joanna, you look pretty frustrated with all of this.

extraExamples.en: You seem to be worried about the meeting tomorrow.
extraExamples.zh: 你看起來好像很擔心明天的會議。

extraExamples.en: She was upset about what her coworker said.
extraExamples.zh: 她對同事說的話感到不高興。

sentences.en: I am upset because I think nobody likes it, but we can’t turn it off. Also, every day there is a new thing you are not allowed to say. Last week, I was talking to a friend about how I visited a minority group in this hidden far away place in Asia and she burst out laughing. She told me that I can’t say the word "minority" anymore. So what am I supposed to call them?
sentences.zh: 當然會生氣，因為我覺得沒有人喜歡這種情況，但我們又沒辦法關掉它。而且，每天都會冒出一個「現在不可以說」的新詞。上週，我跟朋友聊到自己去了一個亞洲某個偏遠地區，拜訪當地的「少數族群」，結果她直接大笑，然後告訴我：「你不能再用 minority 這個詞了！」那我到底應該怎麼稱呼他們？

back.grammar: be allowed to + 原形動詞 表示「被允許做……」；be supposed to + 原形動詞 表示「應該／到底要……」，常用來表達困惑或不滿。
variations: I’m upset because I feel like nobody wants this, but we can’t shut it off. And every day there’s some new word we’re not supposed to say.
I’m annoyed because I don’t think anyone actually likes it, but we’re stuck with it. Plus, there’s always a new rule about what you can’t say.
I’m frustrated because it feels like no one likes this, but we can’t escape it. Every day, there’s another word that’s suddenly off-limits.

extraExamples.en: The music was so loud, but we couldn’t turn it off.
extraExamples.zh: 音樂很大聲，但我們沒辦法把它關掉。

extraExamples.en: When I showed him my old haircut, he burst out laughing.
extraExamples.zh: 我給他看我以前的髮型時，他突然大笑。

sentences.en: I didn’t know that was a thing either.
sentences.zh: 我也不知道這居然是一回事。

back.grammar: I didn’t know that was a thing. 是很口語的說法，意思是「我不知道那居然算一回事／有人在意這個」。either 放句尾表示「也」。
variations: I had no idea that was a thing either.
I didn’t realize people cared about that either.
Yeah, I didn’t know that was an issue either.

keyPhrases.zh: 那居然是一回事；那居然有人在意

extraExamples.en: I didn’t know putting pineapple on pizza was such a big thing.
extraExamples.zh: 我不知道披薩放鳳梨居然是這麼有爭議的事。

extraExamples.en: I had no idea that app was still a thing.
extraExamples.zh: 我完全不知道那個 app 居然還有人在用。

sentences.en: My point exactly. Why are we coming up with new language problems when the rest of the world does not care about skin politics? I think there are real problems and the world is hiding its head in the sand, choosing to deal with such matters instead.
sentences.zh: 這就是我的重點！為什麼我們要不斷製造新的語言問題，而世界其他地方根本不在乎這種「膚色政治」？明明有更重要的社會問題要解決，但世界卻選擇視而不見，把焦點放在這些事情上。

back.grammar: My point exactly. 是口語中表示「這正是我的重點」；come up with 表示「想出／提出」，hide one’s head in the sand 表示「逃避現實」。
variations: That’s exactly my point. Why are we inventing new language problems when there are bigger issues out there?
Exactly. Why are we creating all these new things to argue about while ignoring real problems?
That’s what I’m saying. We’re focusing on word games instead of dealing with actual problems.

keyPhrases.phrase: hide one’s head in the sand
keyPhrases.zh: 逃避現實；裝作沒看見問題

extraExamples.en: My point exactly—we need to fix the schedule before we talk about anything else.
extraExamples.zh: 這正是我的重點——我們得先修正時程，再談其他事。

extraExamples.en: You can’t just hide your head in the sand and pretend the bill isn’t due.
extraExamples.zh: 你不能逃避現實，假裝帳單不用繳。

units.title: 電話溝通與預約
units.scenario: Cheryl 早上打了幾通電話，先訂晚餐餐廳，再預約診所，最後打客服詢問帳戶扣款。

sentences.en: Hi, this is Cheryl. I'm calling to make a reservation for tonight.
sentences.zh: 嗨，我是 Cheryl。我打來想訂今晚的位子。

back.grammar: 電話開場常用 “This is + 名字” 表示「我是…」；“I’m calling to…” 說明打電話目的。
variations: Hi, this is Cheryl. I’d like to book a table for tonight.
Hi, Cheryl Chen here. I’m calling about a reservation for tonight.
Hi, I wanted to make a dinner reservation for tonight.

keyPhrases.zh: 我是 Cheryl（電話中自我介紹）

extraExamples.en: Hi, this is Mark. I’m calling to confirm my appointment.
extraExamples.zh: 嗨，我是 Mark。我打來確認我的預約。

extraExamples.en: I’d like to make a reservation for two this Friday.
extraExamples.zh: 我想訂這週五兩個人的位子。

sentences.en: Sure, what time were you hoping to come in?
sentences.zh: 好的，妳希望幾點過來呢？

back.grammar: “were you hoping to…” 用過去進行式讓問題更委婉，比 “What time do you want…” 更有禮貌。
variations: Sure, what time would you like to come in?
Of course. What time are you thinking?
Sure thing. Around what time were you looking for?

keyPhrases.zh: 你希望…嗎／想要…嗎（委婉問法）

extraExamples.en: What day were you hoping to come in?
extraExamples.zh: 你希望哪一天過來呢？

extraExamples.en: What time were you looking for on Saturday?
extraExamples.zh: 你星期六想約幾點呢？

sentences.en: Around seven, if you have anything available for four people.
sentences.zh: 大概七點，如果你們還有四個人的位子的話。

back.grammar: “if you have anything available” 是詢問是否有空位／時段的自然說法。
variations: Around seven, if you’ve got a table for four.
Maybe around seven, if there’s anything open for four.
Around 7, if you can fit four people in.

extraExamples.en: Do you have anything available this afternoon?
extraExamples.zh: 你們今天下午有任何空檔嗎？

extraExamples.en: We’re looking for something around six for three people.
extraExamples.zh: 我們想找大約六點、三個人的位子。

sentences.en: Let me take a quick look... I can do 7:15 or 8:00.
sentences.zh: 我幫妳快速看一下……我們可以安排 7:15 或 8:00。

back.grammar: “Let me…” 是服務業常用的口語句型，表示「我來幫你…」；“I can do…” 表示「我可以安排」。
variations: Let me check real quick... I have 7:15 or 8 available.
Give me one second... We can do 7:15 or 8:00.
Let me take a look... I’ve got 7:15 or 8.

extraExamples.en: Let me take a quick look at the schedule.
extraExamples.zh: 我快速看一下時程表。

extraExamples.en: I can do Monday morning or Tuesday afternoon.
extraExamples.zh: 我可以安排星期一早上或星期二下午。

sentences.en: 7:15 is perfect. Can you put it under Cheryl Chen?
sentences.zh: 7:15 很剛好。可以幫我用 Cheryl Chen 的名字訂嗎？

back.grammar: “put it under + 名字” 是訂位／預約時表示「登記在某人名下」的自然口語。
variations: 7:15 works great. Can you put it under Cheryl Chen?
7:15 is great. Could you make it under Cheryl Chen?
That time is perfect. The name is Cheryl Chen.

keyPhrases.zh: 用 Cheryl Chen 的名字

extraExamples.en: Can you put the reservation under David Lin?
extraExamples.zh: 可以把訂位登記在 David Lin 名下嗎？

extraExamples.en: Friday at noon is perfect for me.
extraExamples.zh: 星期五中午對我來說很剛好。

sentences.en: Absolutely. What's the best number to reach you?
sentences.zh: 當然可以。請問哪支電話最方便聯絡妳？

back.grammar: “What’s the best number to reach you?” 是詢問聯絡電話的禮貌說法，比 “What’s your phone number?” 更自然。
variations: Of course. What’s the best phone number for you?
Absolutely. What number should we use to reach you?
Sure. What’s a good number to contact you at?

extraExamples.en: What’s the best email to reach you?
extraExamples.zh: 哪個 email 最方便聯絡你？

extraExamples.en: You can reach me at this number after 5.
extraExamples.zh: 五點後可以用這支電話聯絡我。

back.grammar: 報電話號碼常用 “It’s…” 開頭；數字可一個一個念，0 常念 “oh” 或 “zero”。
variations: You can reach me at 415-555-0188.
My number is 415-555-0188.

extraExamples.en: My number is 212-555-0943.
You can reach me at 650-555-7721.

sentences.en: Great, you're all set, and we'll see you tonight.
sentences.zh: 太好了，都幫妳訂好了，今晚見。

back.grammar: “You’re all set” 是美式口語，表示「都好了、都辦妥了」。
variations: Great, you’re all set. We’ll see you tonight.
Perfect, your reservation is confirmed. See you tonight.
All right, we’ve got you down for tonight.

extraExamples.en: You’re all set for your 3 p.m. appointment.
extraExamples.zh: 你下午三點的預約都安排好了。

extraExamples.en: We’ll see you tomorrow morning.
extraExamples.zh: 我們明天早上見。

sentences.en: Hi, I was hoping to schedule an appointment with Dr. Miller.
sentences.zh: 嗨，我想預約 Miller 醫師的門診。

back.grammar: “I was hoping to…” 用過去進行式表達委婉請求，常用於預約、詢問幫忙。
variations: Hi, I’d like to make an appointment with Dr. Miller.
Hi, I’m calling to schedule a visit with Dr. Miller.
Hi, I wanted to see if I could book an appointment with Dr. Miller.

extraExamples.en: I was hoping to reschedule my appointment.
extraExamples.zh: 我想改我的預約時間。

extraExamples.en: I’d like to schedule an appointment for next week.
extraExamples.zh: 我想預約下週的時間。

sentences.en: Are you a new patient, or have you been seen here before?
sentences.zh: 請問妳是初診，還是之前有來看過？

back.grammar: “have you been seen here before?” 是現在完成式被動，意思是「你以前有在這裡被看診過嗎？」
variations: Is this your first time here, or have you been here before?
Are you a new patient, or have we seen you before?
Have you visited our office before, or is this your first appointment?

keyPhrases.phrase: have you been seen here before
keyPhrases.zh: 你以前有在這裡看過診嗎

extraExamples.en: Have you been seen at this clinic before?
extraExamples.zh: 你以前有在這間診所看過嗎？

extraExamples.en: Are you a new patient, or do you already have an account with us?
extraExamples.zh: 你是新病人，還是已經有我們的資料了？

sentences.en: I've been there before, but it's been a while.
sentences.zh: 我之前有去過，不過已經有一陣子了。

back.grammar: “I’ve been there before” 用現在完成式表示過去經驗；“it’s been a while” 表示「已經一段時間了」。
variations: I’ve been there before, but not recently.
I’ve seen him before, but it’s been a long time.
I’m an existing patient, but I haven’t been in for a while.

extraExamples.en: I’ve been to that restaurant before, but it’s been a while.
extraExamples.zh: 我以前去過那間餐廳，但已經有一陣子了。

extraExamples.en: I haven’t seen my dentist in a while.
extraExamples.zh: 我有一陣子沒看牙醫了。

sentences.en: No problem. Can you hold for just a second while I pull up your chart?
sentences.zh: 沒問題。我幫妳調病歷時，可以請妳稍等一下嗎？

back.grammar: “Can you hold…?” 是電話中請對方稍等；“pull up” 指在電腦系統中調出資料。
variations: No problem. Could you hold for a moment while I look up your chart?
Sure. Give me just a second while I pull up your file.
No problem. Please hold while I bring up your records.

extraExamples.en: Can you hold for a minute while I check that?
extraExamples.zh: 我查一下時，可以請你稍等一分鐘嗎？

extraExamples.en: Let me pull up your account real quick.
extraExamples.zh: 我快速調出你的帳戶資料。

sentences.en: Sure, take your time.
sentences.zh: 可以，不急。

back.grammar: “Take your time” 是常用口語，表示「慢慢來、不急」。
variations: Sure, no rush.
Of course, take your time.
No problem. I can wait.

extraExamples.en: Take your time. I’m not in a hurry.
extraExamples.zh: 慢慢來，我不趕時間。

extraExamples.en: No rush—just send it when you can.
extraExamples.zh: 不急，你方便的時候再寄給我就好。

sentences.en: Thanks for holding. The earliest opening I have is Thursday at 10:30.
sentences.zh: 謝謝妳等候。目前最早的空檔是星期四 10:30。

back.grammar: “Thanks for holding” 是電話中對方等待後的禮貌用語；“opening” 指可預約的空檔。
variations: Thanks for waiting. My earliest opening is Thursday at 10:30.
Thanks for holding. The first available time is Thursday at 10:30.
I appreciate you holding. The soonest I can do is Thursday at 10:30.

extraExamples.en: The earliest opening we have is Monday afternoon.
extraExamples.zh: 我們最早的空檔是星期一下午。

extraExamples.en: Thanks for holding. I found your appointment.
extraExamples.zh: 謝謝你等候。我找到你的預約了。

sentences.en: That works for me. Do I need to bring my insurance card?
sentences.zh: 這個時間我可以。需要帶我的保險卡嗎？

back.grammar: “That works for me” 表示「那個時間／安排我可以」；“Do I need to…?” 詢問是否需要做某事。
variations: That works for me. Should I bring my insurance card?
That time is good. Do I need my insurance card?
Perfect. Should I bring my insurance information with me?

extraExamples.en: Tuesday morning works for me.
extraExamples.zh: 星期二早上我可以。

extraExamples.en: Do I need to bring any paperwork?
extraExamples.zh: 我需要帶任何文件嗎？

sentences.en: Yes, and please arrive about fifteen minutes early to fill out some paperwork.
sentences.zh: 需要，另外請提早大約十五分鐘到，填一些資料。

back.grammar: 祈使句 please arrive... 用來禮貌提出要求；to fill out... 表示「為了填寫……」的目的。
variations: Sure, and please get here about fifteen minutes early to fill out a few forms.
Yes, and try to arrive around fifteen minutes early so you can complete some paperwork.
Yep, please come in about fifteen minutes early to take care of the paperwork.

extraExamples.en: Please arrive ten minutes early to check in at the front desk.
extraExamples.zh: 請提早十分鐘到櫃台報到。

extraExamples.en: I need to fill out some paperwork before my appointment.
extraExamples.zh: 我預約前需要填一些資料。

sentences.en: Hi, I'm calling about a charge on my account that I don't recognize.
sentences.zh: 嗨，我打來是想問我帳戶上有一筆我不認得的扣款。

back.grammar: I'm calling about... 是打電話詢問事情的常用開場；that I don't recognize 是關係子句，補充說明 charge。
variations: Hi, I'm calling because there's a charge on my account I don't recognize.
Hi, I wanted to ask about a charge on my account that doesn't look familiar.
Hello, I'm calling to check on a charge I don't remember making.

extraExamples.en: I'm calling about an appointment I need to reschedule.
extraExamples.zh: 我打來是想改約時間。

extraExamples.en: There's a charge on my account that I don't recognize.
extraExamples.zh: 我的帳戶上有一筆我不認得的扣款。

sentences.en: I can help with that. Can you verify your name and billing ZIP code?
sentences.zh: 我可以幫妳處理。可以請妳確認姓名和帳單郵遞區號嗎？

back.grammar: Can you verify...? 是客服常用的禮貌確認身分說法；billing ZIP code 指帳單地址的郵遞區號。
variations: I can help you with that. Could you verify your name and billing ZIP code?
Sure, I can take a look. Can I have your name and billing ZIP code to verify the account?
No problem. First, can you confirm your name and billing ZIP code?

keyPhrases.zh: 我可以幫忙處理那件事

extraExamples.en: Before we continue, can you verify your email address?
extraExamples.zh: 在我們繼續之前，可以請你確認你的電子信箱嗎？

extraExamples.en: I can help with that, but I’ll need your order number first.
extraExamples.zh: 我可以幫你處理，但我會先需要你的訂單號碼。

sentences.en: Yeah, it's Cheryl Chen, and the ZIP code is 94103.
sentences.zh: 可以，是 Cheryl Chen，郵遞區號是 94103。

back.grammar: Yeah 是口語中的「可以／對」；it's... 用來回答身分資訊，the ZIP code is... 用來清楚提供郵遞區號。
variations: Sure, it's Cheryl Chen, and the ZIP code is 94103.
Yes, my name is Cheryl Chen, and my ZIP code is 94103.
Yeah, this is Cheryl Chen, and the billing ZIP code is 94103.

extraExamples.en: Sure, it's Daniel Wang, and the phone number is 555-0198.
extraExamples.zh: 可以，是 Daniel Wang，電話號碼是 555-0198。

extraExamples.en: Yeah, this is Lisa Lin, and the ZIP code is 10001.
extraExamples.zh: 可以，這裡是 Lisa Lin，郵遞區號是 10001。

sentences.en: Thanks, I found your account. Let me look into that charge for you.
sentences.zh: 謝謝，我找到妳的帳戶了。我來幫妳查一下那筆扣款。

back.grammar: I found your account 用一般過去式表示剛完成的動作；Let me look into... 是「讓我查一下／深入了解」的客服常用語。
variations: Thanks, I pulled up your account. Let me check that charge for you.
Great, I found your account. I’ll take a look at that charge now.
Thanks, I’ve got your account here. Let me look into the charge for you.

extraExamples.en: Let me look into the issue and get back to you.
extraExamples.zh: 讓我查一下這個問題，再回覆你。

extraExamples.en: I found your reservation. Let me check the room details for you.
extraExamples.zh: 我找到你的訂房了。我來幫你確認房間資訊。

units.title: 問路與大眾運輸
units.scenario: Ian 在美國市中心迷路，向路人和站務人員問路，改搭地鐵、公車，最後叫 Uber 去目的地。

sentences.en: Excuse me, do you know how to get to Union Station from here?
sentences.zh: 不好意思，你知道從這裡怎麼去聯合車站嗎？

back.grammar: 「Do you know how to + V」是很自然、有禮貌地問路／問方法的句型。
variations: Excuse me, how do I get to Union Station from here?
Hi, could you tell me how to get to Union Station?
Do you happen to know the way to Union Station?

extraExamples.en: Excuse me, do you know how to get to the nearest pharmacy?
extraExamples.zh: 不好意思，你知道怎麼去最近的藥局嗎？

extraExamples.en: Do you know how to get to the airport from here?
extraExamples.zh: 你知道從這裡怎麼去機場嗎？

sentences.en: Yeah, it's about a ten-minute walk, but the subway's faster if you're in a hurry.
sentences.zh: 知道，大概走路十分鐘，但如果你趕時間，搭地鐵比較快。

back.grammar: 「about a ten-minute walk」表示「大約走路十分鐘」；「if you're in a hurry」是假設你趕時間的口語說法。
variations: Yeah, it's around a ten-minute walk, but the subway would be quicker if you're rushed.
Sure. You can walk there in about ten minutes, but the subway's faster.
It's not far—maybe ten minutes on foot—but take the subway if you're in a hurry.

extraExamples.en: It's about a five-minute walk from my apartment to the grocery store.
extraExamples.zh: 從我公寓走到超市大約五分鐘。

extraExamples.en: If you're in a hurry, just grab a taxi.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你趕時間，就直接搭計程車吧。

sentences.en: Which line should I take?
sentences.zh: 我應該搭哪一線？

back.grammar: 「Which + 名詞 + should I + V?」用來詢問建議，語氣自然又實用。
variations: What line should I take?
Which subway line do I need?
What train should I get on?

extraExamples.en: Which bus should I take to get to the mall?
extraExamples.zh: 我要搭哪班公車去購物中心？

extraExamples.en: Which exit should I use?
extraExamples.zh: 我應該走哪個出口？

sentences.en: Take the Red Line toward Downtown and get off at Union Station.
sentences.zh: 搭往市中心方向的紅線，在聯合車站下車。

back.grammar: 祈使句「Take... and get off...」常用在給交通指示，簡潔直接。
variations: Get on the Red Line toward Downtown and get off at Union Station.
Take the Red Line heading downtown, then hop off at Union Station.
You’ll want the Red Line toward Downtown. Get off at Union Station.

extraExamples.en: Take the Green Line toward the airport and get off at Terminal 2.
extraExamples.zh: 搭往機場方向的綠線，在第二航廈下車。

extraExamples.en: Get off at the next stop and walk two blocks north.
extraExamples.zh: 在下一站下車，往北走兩個街口。

sentences.en: Got it, thanks. Is the station entrance around here?
sentences.zh: 了解，謝謝。地鐵入口在這附近嗎？

back.grammar: 「Got it」是口語的「了解」；「around here」表示「在這附近」。
variations: Gotcha, thanks. Is there an entrance nearby?
Okay, thanks. Is the station entrance close by?
That makes sense, thanks. Where’s the entrance around here?

extraExamples.en: Got it, thanks. I’ll meet you at the front door.
extraExamples.zh: 了解，謝謝。我會在前門跟你碰面。

extraExamples.en: Is there a restroom around here?
extraExamples.zh: 這附近有洗手間嗎？

sentences.en: Yep, just go straight for two blocks, then make a left at the coffee shop.
sentences.zh: 對，直走兩個街口，然後在咖啡店那邊左轉。

back.grammar: 「go straight for two blocks」和「make a left」都是美國人很常用的指路口語。
variations: Yeah, go straight two blocks, then turn left at the coffee shop.
Yep, head straight for a couple blocks and take a left by the coffee shop.
Sure, just keep going straight for two blocks, then make a left when you see the coffee shop.

extraExamples.en: Go straight for three blocks, and the bank will be on your right.
extraExamples.zh: 直走三個街口，銀行會在你的右手邊。

extraExamples.en: Make a left at the gas station, then go down the hill.
extraExamples.zh: 在加油站左轉，然後往下坡走。

sentences.en: Do I need a transit card, or can I just tap my credit card?
sentences.zh: 我需要交通卡嗎，還是可以直接刷信用卡？

back.grammar: 「Do I need... or can I...?」用來詢問兩種選項；「tap my credit card」是感應刷信用卡。
variations: Do I have to buy a transit card, or can I tap my credit card?
Can I use my credit card, or do I need a metro card?
Do I need a pass, or can I just tap to pay?

extraExamples.en: Do I need cash, or can I just use Apple Pay?
extraExamples.zh: 我需要現金嗎，還是可以直接用 Apple Pay？

extraExamples.en: You can tap your credit card to get on the bus.
extraExamples.zh: 你可以感應刷信用卡上公車。

sentences.en: You can just tap your card at the gate.
sentences.zh: 你直接在閘門刷卡就可以了。

back.grammar: 「You can just + V」表示「你直接……就可以了」，語氣輕鬆、簡單。
variations: You can just tap your card when you go through the gate.
Just tap your card at the gate.
Yeah, just use your card at the turnstile.

extraExamples.en: You can just tap your phone to pay.
extraExamples.zh: 你直接用手機感應付款就可以了。

extraExamples.en: Just tap your card at the entrance and walk in.
extraExamples.zh: 在入口刷卡，然後走進去就可以了。

sentences.en: Sorry, is this train going downtown?
sentences.zh: 不好意思，這班車是往市中心嗎？

back.grammar: 「Is this train going...?」用現在進行式詢問交通工具的行進方向。
variations: Sorry, does this train go downtown?
Excuse me, is this the train to downtown?
Hey, is this one headed downtown?

extraExamples.en: Is this bus going to the airport?
extraExamples.zh: 這班公車是去機場的嗎？

extraExamples.en: Is this train headed toward Brooklyn?
extraExamples.zh: 這班車是往布魯克林方向嗎？

sentences.en: No, this one's going the other way.
sentences.zh: 不是，這班是往反方向。

back.grammar: 「this one」用來代替前面提到的 train；「the other way」表示反方向。
variations: No, this one’s headed the opposite direction.
No, it’s going the other way.
Nope, you need the train on the other platform.

extraExamples.en: This one’s too small. Do you have a larger size?
extraExamples.zh: 這件太小了。你們有大一點的尺寸嗎？

extraExamples.en: We’re going the other way, so we need to turn around.
extraExamples.zh: 我們走反方向了，所以需要折返。

sentences.en: Oh, okay. So I should cross over to the other platform?
sentences.zh: 喔好，所以我應該到對面的月台嗎？

back.grammar: 「So I should...?」用來確認自己理解是否正確；「cross over」表示穿越到另一邊。
variations: Oh, okay. So I need to go to the other platform?
Got it. Should I cross over to the platform across the tracks?
Okay, so the other platform is the one I want?

extraExamples.en: So I should wait here until my name is called?
extraExamples.zh: 所以我應該在這裡等到叫到我的名字嗎？

extraExamples.en: Cross over to the other side of the street and you’ll see the bakery.
extraExamples.zh: 穿到街對面，你就會看到那家麵包店。

sentences.en: Exactly, and the next train should be here in about five minutes.
sentences.zh: 沒錯，下一班大概五分鐘後會到。

back.grammar: 「should be here」這裡的 should 表示合理推測，不是「應該做」。
variations: Exactly. The next train should arrive in about five minutes.
Yep, and the next one should be here in around five minutes.
That’s right. You’ve got about five minutes until the next train.

keyPhrases.phrase: should be here in about five minutes

extraExamples.en: The food should be ready in about ten minutes.
extraExamples.zh: 餐點大概十分鐘後會好。

extraExamples.en: She should be here soon; she just texted me.
extraExamples.zh: 她應該快到了；她剛傳訊息給我。

sentences.en: Looks like there's a delay on the Red Line.
sentences.zh: 看起來紅線有延誤。

back.grammar: 「Looks like...」是口語的「看起來好像……」，後面可接完整句子。
variations: Looks like the Red Line is delayed.
It seems like there’s a delay on the Red Line.
I think the Red Line’s running late.

extraExamples.en: Looks like it’s going to rain later.
extraExamples.zh: 看起來晚點會下雨。

extraExamples.en: There’s a delay on my flight, so I’ll be late.
extraExamples.zh: 我的班機延誤了，所以我會晚到。

sentences.en: You could take the 42 bus instead; it stops right outside.
sentences.zh: 你也可以改搭 42 號公車；它就在外面停。

back.grammar: 「You could...」是給建議的委婉說法；「instead」表示改為、替代。
variations: You could grab the 42 bus instead; it stops right out front.
Maybe take the 42 bus instead—it stops just outside.
The 42 bus is another option. It stops right outside.

extraExamples.en: You could order takeout instead if you’re too tired to cook.
extraExamples.zh: 如果你太累不想煮飯，可以改叫外送。

extraExamples.en: The bus stops right outside my apartment building.
extraExamples.zh: 公車就停在我公寓大樓外面。

sentences.en: How often does that bus come?
sentences.zh: 那班公車多久來一班？

back.grammar: 「How often does... come?」用來問交通工具或事件的頻率：多久一次。
variations: How often does that bus run?
How frequently does the 42 come?
Does that bus come pretty often?

extraExamples.en: How often does the train come on weekends?
extraExamples.zh: 週末火車多久來一班？

extraExamples.en: How often do you go grocery shopping?
extraExamples.zh: 你多久去買一次菜？

sentences.en: Usually every fifteen minutes, but traffic can mess things up.
sentences.zh: 通常每十五分鐘一班，但塞車可能會打亂時間。

back.grammar: 省略句：前面省略了主詞和動詞（It comes / The bus comes），口語中常用；「mess things up」是口語片語，表示把事情搞亂。
variations: Usually every fifteen minutes, but traffic can throw it off.
It usually comes every fifteen minutes, but traffic can mess with the schedule.
Most of the time it’s every fifteen minutes, unless traffic screws things up.

extraExamples.en: The train usually runs every ten minutes, but bad weather can mess things up.
extraExamples.zh: 火車通常每十分鐘一班，但壞天氣可能會打亂時間。

extraExamples.en: I had everything planned, but one phone call messed things up.
extraExamples.zh: 我什麼都計畫好了，但一通電話把事情打亂了。

sentences.en: I'm running late, so I might just call an Uber.
sentences.zh: 我快遲到了，所以我可能直接叫 Uber。

back.grammar: 現在進行式「I'm running late」表示目前狀態：快遲到；「might just」表示『可能乾脆就……』，語氣很口語。
variations: I’m gonna be late, so I might just grab an Uber.
I’m running behind, so I’ll probably just call an Uber.
I’m cutting it close, so maybe I’ll just Uber there.

extraExamples.en: I’m running late for work, so I’ll skip coffee this morning.
extraExamples.zh: 我上班快遲到了，所以今天早上就不買咖啡了。

extraExamples.en: It’s starting to rain, so we might just take a taxi home.
extraExamples.zh: 開始下雨了，所以我們可能乾脆搭計程車回家。

sentences.en: Good call. The pickup spot is across the street by the hotel.
sentences.zh: 這樣也好。上車點在街對面飯店旁邊。

back.grammar: 「Good call」是口語用法，表示『好主意／這樣做是對的』；「across the street」表示在街對面。
variations: Smart move. The pickup spot is across the street next to the hotel.
Good idea. You’ll get picked up across the street by the hotel.
That works. The pickup point is just across the street near the hotel.

extraExamples.en: Good call bringing an umbrella—it’s pouring now.
extraExamples.zh: 你帶傘是對的，現在雨下超大。

extraExamples.en: The coffee shop is across the street from the bank.
extraExamples.zh: 那間咖啡店在銀行的街對面。

sentences.en: My driver says he'll be here in three minutes.
sentences.zh: 我的司機說他三分鐘後會到。

back.grammar: 「says he'll」是間接引述現在資訊；「in three minutes」表示從現在開始算『三分鐘後』。
variations: My driver says he’ll arrive in three minutes.
The driver says he’s three minutes away.
My Uber says the driver will be here in about three minutes.

extraExamples.en: My friend says she’ll be here in five minutes.
extraExamples.zh: 我朋友說她五分鐘後會到。

extraExamples.en: The app says the food will arrive in ten minutes.
extraExamples.zh: App 顯示餐點十分鐘後會送到。

sentences.en: Perfect, just double-check the license plate before you get in.
sentences.zh: 太好了，上車前記得再確認一下車牌。

back.grammar: 祈使句「double-check」表示提醒對方『再確認一次』；「before you get in」用 before + 主詞 + 動詞，表示做某事之前。
variations: Great, just make sure the license plate matches before you get in.
Perfect. Check the plate one more time before hopping in.
Nice, just confirm the license plate before you get in the car.

extraExamples.en: Double-check the address before you send the package.
extraExamples.zh: 寄包裹前再確認一下地址。

extraExamples.en: Make sure you check the license plate before getting into the Uber.
extraExamples.zh: 上 Uber 前一定要確認車牌。

units.title: 咖啡廳點餐與外送
units.scenario: Oliver 早上先在星巴克現場點咖啡，到了辦公室又幫同事用 App 叫外送，最後確認取餐和外送狀況。

sentences.en: Hey, what can I get started for you today?
sentences.zh: 嗨，今天想先幫你點什麼呢？

back.grammar: 咖啡店點餐常用句型：What can I get started for you? 比 What do you want? 更禮貌自然，get started 表示「先幫你開始點」。
variations: What can I get for you today?
What are we having today?
What can I start for you?

extraExamples.en: What can I get started for you this morning?
extraExamples.zh: 今天早上想先幫你點什麼呢？

extraExamples.en: I can get that started for you right away.
extraExamples.zh: 我可以馬上幫你開始處理。

sentences.en: Hi, can I get a grande iced latte, please?
sentences.zh: 嗨，我想要一杯中杯冰拿鐵，謝謝。

back.grammar: Can I get...? 是美國口語點餐超常用說法，比 I want... 更客氣自然。
variations: Could I get a grande iced latte, please?
I'll have a grande iced latte, please.
Can I do a grande iced latte?

keyPhrases.zh: 請；謝謝，用來讓語氣更客氣

extraExamples.en: Can I get a medium coffee, please?
extraExamples.zh: 我可以要一杯中杯咖啡嗎？謝謝。

extraExamples.en: Can I get two bagels to go?
extraExamples.zh: 我可以要兩個貝果外帶嗎？

sentences.en: Sure, 2% is standard—did you wanna switch it up?
sentences.zh: 可以，標準是用低脂牛奶，你想換別的奶嗎？

back.grammar: wanna 是 want to 的口語縮讀；switch it up 表示「換一下、改成不一樣的」。
variations: Sure, it normally comes with 2%. Did you want a different milk?
Yep, 2% is the default. Wanna change it?
Sure thing. It comes with 2%, unless you want to switch it up.

keyPhrases.zh: 低脂牛奶，2% 脂肪牛奶

extraExamples.en: We usually use whole milk, but you can switch it up.
extraExamples.zh: 我們通常用全脂牛奶，但你可以換成別的。

extraExamples.en: I always order the same drink, so today I wanna switch it up.
extraExamples.zh: 我每次都點一樣的飲料，所以今天想換一下。

sentences.en: Can you make that oat milk?
sentences.zh: 可以幫我改成燕麥奶嗎？

back.grammar: Can you make that...? 點餐時可用來要求「把那個改成……」，that 指前面點的飲料。
variations: Could you make it with oat milk?
Can I do oat milk instead?
Could I get that with oat milk?

extraExamples.en: Can you make that decaf?
extraExamples.zh: 可以把那個改成低咖啡因嗎？

extraExamples.en: Can you make that no ice?
extraExamples.zh: 可以把那個做成去冰嗎？

sentences.en: Of course. Do you want it sweetened at all?
sentences.zh: 當然。需要加糖嗎？

back.grammar: at all 用在問句中加強「有任何程度嗎？」Do you want it sweetened at all? 就是「要不要加一點甜？」
variations: Do you want any sweetener in it?
Would you like that sweetened?
Do you want it sweet at all?

keyPhrases.zh: 任何一點；到底，用於問句或否定句

extraExamples.en: Do you want your tea sweetened at all?
extraExamples.zh: 你的茶需要加糖嗎？

extraExamples.en: Is it spicy at all?
extraExamples.zh: 它會辣嗎？

sentences.en: Just half sweet, if that's okay.
sentences.zh: 半糖就好，可以的話。

back.grammar: Just 表示「只要……就好」；if that's okay 是客氣補充，讓要求聽起來更柔和。
variations: Half sweet is fine, please.
Just half sweet, please.
Can you do half sweet?

extraExamples.en: Just a little ice, if that's okay.
extraExamples.zh: 少冰就好，如果可以的話。

extraExamples.en: Can you make it half sweet, please?
extraExamples.zh: 可以幫我做半糖嗎？

sentences.en: No problem. Anything else for you?
sentences.zh: 沒問題。還需要其他的嗎？

back.grammar: Anything else for you? 是服務業常見收尾問句，意思是「還需要別的嗎？」
variations: Anything else today?
Will that be all for you?
Can I get you anything else?

extraExamples.en: No problem. Anything else you need?
extraExamples.zh: 沒問題。你還需要其他東西嗎？

extraExamples.en: Would you like anything else with that?
extraExamples.zh: 你還想搭配其他東西嗎？

sentences.en: Yeah, can I also get a bacon, Gouda, and egg sandwich?
sentences.zh: 有，可以再給我一份培根高達起司蛋三明治嗎？

back.grammar: Can I also get...? 表示「我也可以再點……嗎？」also 放在 get 前，表示追加品項。
variations: Yeah, could I also get a bacon, Gouda, and egg sandwich?
Can I add a bacon, Gouda, and egg sandwich?
I'll also take a bacon, Gouda, and egg sandwich.

keyPhrases.zh: 培根、高達起司和蛋三明治

extraExamples.en: Can I also get a bottle of water?
extraExamples.zh: 我也可以再要一瓶水嗎？

extraExamples.en: I'll also take a chocolate chip cookie.
extraExamples.zh: 我還要一片巧克力豆餅乾。

sentences.en: You got it. Is that for here or to go?
sentences.zh: 好喔。這邊用還是外帶？

back.grammar: You got it. 是美國口語中表示「好，沒問題」；for here or to go 是內用／外帶固定問法。
variations: Sure thing. Is that for here or to go?
Got it. Are you staying or taking it to go?
No problem. For here or to go?

extraExamples.en: You got it. I'll bring that out in a minute.
extraExamples.zh: 好喔。我等一下拿出來。

extraExamples.en: Is your coffee for here or to go?
extraExamples.zh: 你的咖啡是內用還是外帶？

sentences.en: To go, please.
sentences.zh: 外帶，謝謝。

back.grammar: To go 是「外帶」的自然口語說法；回答時可省略完整句，只說 To go, please.。
variations: To go, thanks.
I'll take it to go.
It's to go.

extraExamples.en: Can I get this to go?
extraExamples.zh: 這個可以幫我外帶嗎？

extraExamples.en: I'll take the sandwich to go, please.
extraExamples.zh: 這個三明治我要外帶，謝謝。

sentences.en: Can I get a name for the order?
sentences.zh: 可以給我訂單名字嗎？

back.grammar: Can I get a name for the order? 是店員詢問訂單名字的固定說法；a name 不是問全名，通常給名字即可。
variations: Can I have a name for the order?
What's the name for the order?
What name should I put on that?

keyPhrases.zh: 寫在那上面；登記在那筆訂單

extraExamples.en: Can I get a phone number for the reservation?
extraExamples.zh: 可以給我預約用的電話號碼嗎？

extraExamples.en: What name should I put on the table?
extraExamples.zh: 我要把桌位登記在什麼名字下？

sentences.en: Oliver.
sentences.zh: Oliver。

back.grammar: 回答名字時，英文口語通常直接說名字即可，不需要完整句 My name is...。
variations: It's Oliver.
Oliver, with an E.
The name is Oliver.

extraExamples.en: It's David.
extraExamples.zh: 名字是 David。

extraExamples.en: Megan, with one G.
extraExamples.zh: Megan，一個 G。

sentences.en: Awesome, we'll have that down at the end of the bar.
sentences.zh: 好，等等會放在吧台那端。

back.grammar: we'll have that... 使用未來式 will，表示「我們等一下會把它……」；down at the end of the bar 是店內取餐位置的說法。
variations: Great, we'll set that at the end of the bar.
Perfect, it'll be waiting at the end of the counter.
Awesome, we'll call your name when it's ready.

keyPhrases.phrase: at the end of the bar

extraExamples.en: We'll have your drinks ready in just a minute.
extraExamples.zh: 你的飲料很快就會好了。

extraExamples.en: You can pick it up at the end of the counter.
extraExamples.zh: 你可以在櫃檯那端取餐。

sentences.en: Thanks, I'll just wait over here.
sentences.zh: 謝謝，我在這邊等。

back.grammar: I'll just... 表示「我就……」，語氣輕鬆自然；over here 指「這邊這裡」，常搭配 wait、sit、stand。
variations: Thanks, I'll wait right over here.
Cool, I'll hang out over here.
Thanks, I'll be right here.

extraExamples.en: I'll just wait outside.
extraExamples.zh: 我就在外面等。

extraExamples.en: You can sit over here while you wait.
extraExamples.zh: 你等的時候可以坐這邊。

sentences.en: Oliver, your iced latte and sandwich are ready!
sentences.zh: Oliver，你的冰拿鐵和三明治好了！

back.grammar: Your ... is/are ready! 是通知餐點好了的固定句型；主詞有兩樣東西，所以用 are ready。
variations: Oliver, your order is ready!
Oliver, your latte and sandwich are good to go!
Oliver, we have your iced latte and sandwich ready!

extraExamples.en: Your table is ready.
extraExamples.zh: 你的桌位準備好了。

extraExamples.en: Your drinks are ready at the counter.
extraExamples.zh: 你的飲料在櫃檯好了。

sentences.en: Thanks. Could I grab a straw and some napkins?
sentences.zh: 謝謝。可以給我一支吸管和一些餐巾紙嗎？

back.grammar: Could I...? 是很有禮貌的請求說法；grab 在口語中常用來表示「拿、要」。
variations: Could I get a straw and a few napkins?
Can I have a straw and some napkins, please?
Could you throw in a straw and some napkins?

extraExamples.en: Could I grab a lid for this drink?
extraExamples.zh: 可以給我這杯飲料的杯蓋嗎？

extraExamples.en: Can I have some napkins? I spilled a little coffee.
extraExamples.zh: 可以給我一些餐巾紙嗎？我灑了一點咖啡。

sentences.en: Hey, I'm gonna place a delivery order—does anyone want Starbucks?
sentences.zh: 欸，我要叫外送，有人要星巴克嗎？

back.grammar: I'm gonna 是 I am going to 的口語縮讀，表示「我打算／要……」。place an order 是「下訂單」。
variations: Hey, I'm ordering delivery—anyone want Starbucks?
I'm about to put in a Starbucks order. Anyone want anything?
I'm gonna order Starbucks for delivery. Does anybody want something?

extraExamples.en: I'm gonna place a pizza order—do you want anything?
extraExamples.zh: 我要訂披薩外送，你要吃什麼嗎？

extraExamples.en: Does anyone want coffee? I'm heading out now.
extraExamples.zh: 有人要咖啡嗎？我現在要出門。

sentences.en: Can you add a cold brew for me and make it no ice?
sentences.zh: 可以幫我加一杯冷萃，然後去冰嗎？

back.grammar: Can you add...? 是請別人「幫我加點某物」；make it no ice 是點飲料時很自然的口語說法，意思是「做成去冰」。
variations: Can you add a cold brew for me with no ice?
Could you get me a cold brew, no ice?
Can you add one cold brew to the order and make it without ice?

extraExamples.en: Can you add a latte for me and make it oat milk?
extraExamples.zh: 可以幫我加一杯拿鐵，然後換成燕麥奶嗎？

extraExamples.en: Could you make it no ice? I don't want it watered down.
extraExamples.zh: 可以做成去冰嗎？我不想讓它被稀釋。

sentences.en: The app says the driver is about five minutes away, so I'll meet them downstairs.
sentences.zh: App 顯示外送員大概五分鐘後到，我下樓去等他。

back.grammar: The app says... 是口語中表示「App 上顯示／寫著……」；five minutes away 表示「距離抵達還有五分鐘」。them 可用來指性別不明的單數對象。
variations: The app says the driver is about five minutes out, so I'm gonna meet them downstairs.
Looks like the driver will be here in five minutes. I'll go wait downstairs.
The driver is around five minutes away, according to the app, so I'll head down.

extraExamples.en: The app says my food is ten minutes away.
extraExamples.zh: App 顯示我的餐點大約十分鐘後到。

extraExamples.en: I'll meet you downstairs when you get here.
extraExamples.zh: 你到的時候我會下樓去接你。

sentences.en: Hi, I'm picking up a delivery order for Oliver.
sentences.zh: 嗨，我來取 Oliver 的外送訂單。

back.grammar: I'm picking up... 是現在進行式表達「我現在來取……」；for Oliver 表示「替 Oliver／Oliver 的」。
variations: Hi, I'm here to pick up a delivery order for Oliver.
Hi, I have a pickup for Oliver.
I'm picking up Oliver's delivery order.

extraExamples.en: Hi, I'm picking up an order for Jason.
extraExamples.zh: 嗨，我來取 Jason 的訂單。

extraExamples.en: I'm here to pick up a delivery order from the lobby.
extraExamples.zh: 我來大廳取外送訂單。

units.title: 天氣閒聊與週末計畫
units.scenario: Ian 週五早上在公司茶水間跟同事聊最近的天氣，順便閒聊週末計畫。

sentences.en: Morning, Ian, can you believe how nice it is out today?
sentences.zh: 早啊，Ian，你敢相信今天外面天氣這麼好嗎？

back.grammar: 「Can you believe...？」是口語中用來表達驚訝、開話題的句型；「it is out」指外面天氣狀況。
variations: Morning, Ian. Isn't it gorgeous out today?
Hey Ian, can you believe this weather?
Good morning! It's so nice out today, right?

extraExamples.en: Can you believe how warm it is in January?
extraExamples.zh: 你敢相信一月竟然這麼暖嗎？

extraExamples.en: It's so nice out that I might eat lunch outside.
extraExamples.zh: 外面天氣太好了，我可能會去戶外吃午餐。

sentences.en: I know, right? It finally feels like spring.
sentences.zh: 對啊，真的欸，終於有春天的感覺了。

back.grammar: 「I know, right?」是美式口語中表示強烈同意的說法；「feels like」表示「感覺像」。
variations: Right? It finally feels like spring.
I know! It's finally starting to feel like spring.
Totally. Spring finally showed up.

extraExamples.en: I know, right? This coffee is actually really good.
extraExamples.zh: 對啊，真的欸？這咖啡其實很好喝。

extraExamples.en: It finally feels like summer now that school is out.
extraExamples.zh: 學校放假了，現在終於有夏天的感覺。

sentences.en: It was freezing just a couple days ago, so this is kind of amazing.
sentences.zh: 前幾天還冷得要命，所以現在這樣真的有點誇張地好。

back.grammar: 「It was freezing」用過去式描述前幾天的狀態；「just a couple days ago」表示時間其實很近。
variations: It was freezing a few days ago, so this feels crazy.
Just the other day it was freezing, and now it's gorgeous.
It was so cold earlier this week, so this is pretty wild.

keyPhrases.zh: 有點不可思議；滿驚人的

extraExamples.en: It was freezing last night, so I turned the heat back on.
extraExamples.zh: 昨晚冷得要命，所以我又把暖氣打開了。

extraExamples.en: I just saw her a couple days ago, and she looked great.
extraExamples.zh: 我幾天前才看到她，她看起來很不錯。

sentences.en: Yeah, the weather's been all over the place lately.
sentences.zh: 對啊，最近天氣真的變來變去。

back.grammar: 「has been」現在完成式表示最近一段時間持續到現在；「all over the place」口語指混亂、變來變去。
variations: Yeah, the weather's been so unpredictable lately.
Totally, it's been changing nonstop lately.
Yeah, the weather can't seem to make up its mind.

extraExamples.en: My schedule has been all over the place lately.
extraExamples.zh: 我最近的行程亂七八糟、一直變。

extraExamples.en: The Wi-Fi has been really slow lately.
extraExamples.zh: 最近 Wi-Fi 一直很慢。

sentences.en: I almost wore a heavy coat this morning, but I'm glad I didn't.
sentences.zh: 我今天早上差點穿厚外套，還好沒有。

back.grammar: 「almost + 過去式」表示「差點做某事」；「I'm glad I didn't」是省略說法，完整為「I'm glad I didn't wear it」。
variations: I nearly wore a heavy coat this morning, but thank God I didn't.
I almost put on my winter coat today, but I'm glad I skipped it.
I was about to wear a heavy coat, but luckily I didn't.

extraExamples.en: I almost missed the bus, but it was running late.
extraExamples.zh: 我差點錯過公車，但它剛好誤點了。

extraExamples.en: I was going to bring an umbrella, but I'm glad I didn't.
extraExamples.zh: 我本來要帶傘，還好我沒有。

sentences.en: Same here, I checked the weather app like three times before leaving.
sentences.zh: 我也是，出門前看了天氣 app 大概三次。

back.grammar: 「Same here」是口語中表示「我也是」；「like」在這裡是口語填充/約略用法，表示「大概」。
variations: Same here. I checked the forecast a bunch of times before I left.
Me too. I looked at the weather app like three times.
Same. I kept checking the weather before heading out.

extraExamples.en: Same here, I couldn't sleep at all last night.
extraExamples.zh: 我也是，昨晚完全睡不著。

extraExamples.en: I checked my email like five times before the meeting.
extraExamples.zh: 開會前我大概看了五次 email。

sentences.en: Are you doing anything fun this weekend if the weather holds up?
sentences.zh: 如果天氣維持這樣，你週末有要做什麼好玩的事嗎？

back.grammar: 「Are you doing anything...？」用現在進行式詢問已安排或打算做的事；「if the weather holds up」表示如果天氣維持不變。
variations: Got any fun plans this weekend if the weather stays nice?
Are you up to anything fun this weekend if the weather holds?
Any fun plans this weekend if it stays like this?

keyPhrases.zh: 如果天氣撐住／維持好天氣

extraExamples.en: Are you doing anything fun after work?
extraExamples.zh: 你下班後有要做什麼好玩的事嗎？

extraExamples.en: We'll have a barbecue if the weather holds up.
extraExamples.zh: 如果天氣維持不錯，我們就烤肉。

sentences.en: I'm thinking about going for a hike, but I don't wanna jinx it.
sentences.zh: 我在想要不要去健行，但我不想太早說，免得破功。

back.grammar: 「I'm thinking about + V-ing」表示正在考慮做某事；「wanna」是「want to」的口語縮讀。
variations: I'm thinking of going hiking, but I don't want to jinx it.
I might go for a hike, but I don't wanna get my hopes up.
I was thinking about a hike, but I don't want to speak too soon.

extraExamples.en: I'm thinking about trying that new ramen place tonight.
extraExamples.zh: 我在想今晚要不要去試那家新的拉麵店。

extraExamples.en: Don't jinx it—we're actually on time for once.
extraExamples.zh: 別說破，我們難得一次準時。

sentences.en: That sounds perfect, as long as it doesn't rain out of nowhere.
sentences.zh: 聽起來超適合，只要不要突然下雨就好。

back.grammar: 「as long as」表示條件「只要」；「out of nowhere」是口語片語，表示突然、毫無預警。
variations: That sounds awesome, as long as it doesn't randomly rain.
That'd be perfect, unless it starts raining out of nowhere.
Sounds great, as long as the rain doesn't come out of nowhere.

extraExamples.en: We can walk there as long as it's not too hot.
extraExamples.zh: 只要不會太熱，我們可以走過去。

extraExamples.en: A dog ran out of nowhere and scared me.
extraExamples.zh: 一隻狗突然衝出來，嚇到我。

sentences.en: Exactly, because around here, the forecast changes every five minutes.
sentences.zh: 沒錯，因為這裡的天氣預報每五分鐘就變一次。

back.grammar: 「every five minutes」常用來誇張形容變化很頻繁；「around here」表示在這附近／這一帶。
variations: Exactly. Around here, the forecast changes constantly.
Yep, the forecast here changes every two seconds.
For real. The weather app around here is never consistent.

extraExamples.en: Around here, traffic gets bad after four.
extraExamples.zh: 這一帶四點後交通就會變糟。

extraExamples.en: My plans change every five minutes these days.
extraExamples.zh: 我最近計畫一直變來變去。

sentences.en: I might just keep it low-key and grab brunch with a friend.
sentences.zh: 我可能就輕鬆一點，跟朋友去吃早午餐。

back.grammar: 「might just」表示「可能乾脆就……」；「keep it low-key」是口語，表示低調、輕鬆不要太盛大。
variations: I might just take it easy and get brunch with a friend.
I’ll probably keep it low-key and meet a friend for brunch.
Maybe I'll just do something chill and grab brunch.

extraExamples.en: I might just stay home and watch a movie tonight.
extraExamples.zh: 我今晚可能乾脆待在家看電影。

extraExamples.en: Let's keep it low-key and just order pizza.
extraExamples.zh: 我們簡單一點，叫披薩就好。

sentences.en: Oh nice, patio brunch weather is the best.
sentences.zh: 喔不錯，這種天氣坐戶外吃早午餐最棒了。

back.grammar: 「weather」可放在名詞前當形容詞用，如「patio brunch weather」表示適合在戶外吃早午餐的天氣。
variations: Oh nice, this is perfect patio brunch weather.
Nice! It's the best weather for brunch outside.
That sounds great. Patio brunch hits different in this weather.

extraExamples.en: This is perfect picnic weather.
extraExamples.zh: 這是超適合野餐的天氣。

extraExamples.en: Rooftop drinks are the best on a night like this.
extraExamples.zh: 這種晚上在屋頂喝飲料最棒了。

sentences.en: Totally, I just hope every restaurant isn't packed.
sentences.zh: 真的，我只希望每家餐廳不要都爆滿。

back.grammar: 「I just hope...」表示「我只希望……」；「isn't packed」口語指店裡不要擠滿人。
variations: Totally, I just hope the places aren't all packed.
For sure. Hopefully every restaurant isn't slammed.
Yeah, I just hope we don't have to wait forever.

extraExamples.en: I just hope the train isn't packed this morning.
extraExamples.zh: 我只希望今天早上火車不要太擠。

extraExamples.en: The coffee shop was packed, so we got drinks to go.
extraExamples.zh: 咖啡店爆滿，所以我們外帶飲料。

sentences.en: You should probably make a reservation just in case.
sentences.zh: 你最好還是先訂位，以防萬一。

back.grammar: 「should probably」語氣比「should」委婉，表示建議；「just in case」是「以防萬一」。
variations: You should probably book a table, just in case.
I'd make a reservation if I were you.
Might be smart to reserve a spot, just to be safe.

extraExamples.en: You should probably bring a jacket just in case.
extraExamples.zh: 你最好帶件外套，以防萬一。

extraExamples.en: We made a reservation because that place gets busy.
extraExamples.zh: 我們訂了位，因為那家店很容易客滿。

sentences.en: Good call, I'll look something up during lunch.
sentences.zh: 好主意，我午餐時間來查一下。

back.grammar: 「Good call」是口語中稱讚對方建議好的說法；「look something up」是片語動詞，表示查資料。
variations: Good idea, I'll look up a place during lunch.
Good call. I'll check a few spots on my lunch break.
Smart. I'll look for a reservation during lunch.

extraExamples.en: Good call, let's leave early before traffic gets bad.
extraExamples.zh: 好主意，我們趁交通變糟前早點走。

extraExamples.en: I'll look up the address before we head out.
extraExamples.zh: 我們出發前我會查一下地址。

sentences.en: If it gets warm enough, I might take my dog to the park.
sentences.zh: 如果夠暖的話，我可能會帶我的狗去公園。

back.grammar: If + 現在式, might + 原形動詞：表示「如果條件成立，可能會……」，談未來可能性時 if 子句不用 will。
variations: If it warms up enough, I might bring my dog to the park.
I might take my dog to the park if it’s nice enough out.
If the weather’s warm enough, maybe I’ll take my dog to the park.

extraExamples.en: If it stops raining, I might go for a walk later.
extraExamples.zh: 如果雨停了，我晚點可能會去散步。

extraExamples.en: If I finish work early, I might grab dinner with you.
extraExamples.zh: 如果我早點下班，我可能會跟你吃晚餐。

sentences.en: That's cute, he's gonna be so excited to be outside.
sentences.zh: 好可愛，他一定會超興奮可以出去玩。

back.grammar: gonna 是 going to 的口語縮讀，後面接原形動詞，表示「將要／一定會」。
variations: Aw, that’s adorable. He’s going to be so happy to get outside.
That’s so cute—he’s gonna be thrilled to go out.
Aww, he’s gonna be super excited to be outside.

extraExamples.en: She’s gonna be so excited when she sees the surprise.
extraExamples.zh: 她看到驚喜的時候一定會超興奮。

extraExamples.en: The kids are gonna be happy to play outside after school.
extraExamples.zh: 孩子們放學後可以出去玩會很開心。

sentences.en: For sure, he's been staring out the window all week.
sentences.zh: 一定會，他整個禮拜都在看著窗外。

back.grammar: has been + V-ing 是現在完成進行式，強調動作從過去持續到現在；all week 表示「整個禮拜」。
variations: Definitely. He’s been looking out the window all week.
For sure, he’s been glued to the window all week.
Totally—he’s been staring outside all week long.

keyPhrases.phrase: has been staring out the window

extraExamples.en: I’ve been checking my phone all morning for your message.
extraExamples.zh: 我整個早上一直在看手機等你的訊息。

extraExamples.en: They’ve been talking about that movie all week.
extraExamples.zh: 他們整個禮拜都一直在聊那部電影。

sentences.en: Well, fingers crossed the weather stays this nice through Sunday.
sentences.zh: 好啦，希望天氣到星期天都能維持這麼好。

back.grammar: fingers crossed 是口語片語，意思是「希望如此／祈禱順利」；後面可直接接子句表達希望的事。
variations: Well, let’s hope the weather stays this nice through Sunday.
Fingers crossed it stays this beautiful until Sunday.
Hopefully, the weather holds up through Sunday.

extraExamples.en: Fingers crossed I get the job after the interview.
extraExamples.zh: 希望我面試後能拿到那份工作。

extraExamples.en: Let’s hope the traffic stays light until we get there.
extraExamples.zh: 希望在我們到之前路況都能維持順暢。

sentences.en: Seriously, we deserve one good weekend after all that rain.
sentences.zh: 真的，下了那麼久的雨，我們值得有一個好天氣的週末。

back.grammar: deserve + 名詞 表示「值得／應得」；after all that... 用來強調「經歷了那麼多……之後」。
variations: Honestly, we’ve earned one nice weekend after all that rain.
Seriously, after all that rain, we deserve a sunny weekend.
No kidding—we need one good weekend after so much rain.

extraExamples.en: You deserve a break after working so hard all week.
extraExamples.zh: 你辛苦工作了一整週，值得休息一下。

extraExamples.en: After all that studying, she deserves a fun night out.
extraExamples.zh: 讀了那麼多書之後，她值得出去好好玩一晚。
